title: CLAUDE.md
text: file provides guidance Claude Code (claude.ai/code) working code repository.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: nlmixr2est provides core nonlinear mixed-effects (NLME) estimation routines nlmixr2 ecosystem. implements multiple estimation methods PK/PD modeling: SAEM, FOCEI, FOCE, FO/FOI, Laplace, AGQ, NLM-family methods (nlminb, nlm, bobyqa, newuoa, n1qn1, lbfgsb3c, optim, uobyqa, nls). depends heavily rxode2 ODE solving – models compiled rxode2 C code runtime.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: package requires compilation (NeedsCompilation: yes). C++17 required (set src/Makevars). changing C++ files, devtools::load_all() recompiles.
code: # Load package for interactive development devtools::load_all()  # Run all tests devtools::test()  # Run a single test file testthat::test_file("tests/testthat/test-focei-1.R")  # Build documentation (regenerates NAMESPACE via roxygen2) devtools::document()  # Full R CMD check devtools::check()  # Set the number of threads for parallel processing (e.g., SAEM) rxode2::setRxThreads(threads = 4)  # Use 4 threads

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Entry point nlmixr2() R/nlmixr2.R, calls nlmixr2Est() – S3 generic dispatching est argument string. estimation method R file (R/saem.R, R/focei.R, R/fo.R, R/foce.R, R/laplace.R, R/agq.R, R/nlm.R, R/nlminb.R, R/bobyqa.R, etc.) plus corresponding control function (e.g., saemControl(), foceiControl()).

title: CLAUDE.md
text: estimation, model UI objects translated rxode2-compatible representations via rxPipeline() (R/rxPipeline.R) method-specific *RxUiGet handlers (R/saemRxUiGet.R, R/saemRxUiGetModel.R, R/splitModelRxUiGet.R, R/nlmeRxUiGet.R). ini block sets initial estimates; mu-referencing (population parameters appear linearly ETAs) detected handled specially SAEM efficiency.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: R/hook.R provides hook system pre-processing steps run estimation: bounded parameter transforms (R/preProcessBoundedTransform.R), covariate handling (R/preProcessCovariatesPresent.R), data/UI validation (R/preProcessDataUi.R), zero-omega fixing (R/preProcessZeroOmega.R), literal fix processing (R/preProcessLiteralFix.R).

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Core numerical work C++17 RcppArmadillo/RcppEigen: - saem.cpp – SAEM algorithm kernel - nlm.cpp / innerPoint.cpp – NLM-family optimization - cwres.cpp, npde.cpp, ires.cpp – residual calculations - censResid.cpp, censEst.cpp – M2/M3/M4 censoring methods - nearPD.cpp – nearest positive-definite matrix - uninformativeEtas.cpp, shrink.cpp – ETA diagnostics

what: Registering C/C++ (.Call) routines – src/init.c is MANUAL
title: CLAUDE.md
text: src/init.c hand-maintains R_registerRoutines callMethods[] table (Rcpp’s generated R_init_nlmixr2est RcppExports.cpp used – init.c owns R_init_nlmixr2est). .Call entry lists explicit prototype HARDCODED argument count, e.g. {"_nlmixr2est_foo", (DL_FUNC) &_nlmixr2est_foo, 5}. add [[Rcpp::export]] function change existing one’s argument count, MUST : 1. Rcpp::compileAttributes(".") – regenerates src/RcppExports.cpp + R/RcppExports.R; 2. hand-edit src/init.c – add/update forward SEXP ...(SEXP, ...) prototype callMethods[] entry’s arg count. Skipping step 2 gives runtime .Call error like Incorrect number arguments (N), expecting M '_nlmixr2est_foo' (loaded registration table still old arity), compile error – survives clean rebuild. header change still needs rm -f src/*.o src/*.rebuilding.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Fit results nlmixr2FitData objects (data frame subclass). Post-fit accessors use nmObjGet S3 dispatch (R/nmObjGet.R, R/nmObjHandle.R). Residuals added lazily via addCwres() addNpde().

title: CLAUDE.md
text: tests/testthat/helper-zzz-fits.R pre-fits models caches individual test files can reference fitted objects without re-running estimation. Fixture .rds files live tests/testthat/fixtures/.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: tests/testthat.R keeps CI CRAN oversubscribing core-limited runners (historically caused 6h timeouts / exit-143 “runner received shutdown signal”): testthat workers: SERIAL (TESTTHAT_PARALLEL=FALSE) CI (CI=true) CRAN; everywhere else testthat manages Config/testthat/parallel (parallel: true, edition: 3 DESCRIPTION) normally. Serial deliberate, just “one worker”: parallel mode’s worker->orchestrator message pipe base64-serializes every non-success test event, ERRORING test whose backtrace inlines fit/data object (.call(f, list(<big>)) frame) ships object ~18x size orchestrator can kill whole run R’s 2GB string cap (“result string long”) – windows/devel runner-OOM failures . re-enable parallel CI without solving . rxode2 / data.table within-solve threads: capped 2 CRAN ; CI locally rxode2 manages threads.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Slow, multi-iteration/fit-based test files run WEEKLY, every push/PR. Push/PR CI runs “essential” subset: every test file EXCEPT listed .slowBatches tests/testthat.R. weekly slow-tests.yaml workflow runs batches one time via NLMIXR2EST_TEST_BATCH=<n> (matrix must match number .slowBatches entries). adding tests estimation method, split cost: Quickest core-functionality tests always run: leave .slowBatches (run essential push/PR subset). Keep set small – unit tests plus one basic end--end fit. Example (advi): advi-control, advi-dataprep, advi-inner, advi-grad, advi-fit stay essential; multi-iteration fit files run weekly. Everything else goes weekly batch: add file’s basename (test- prefix, .R) .slowBatches batch, sized measured single-worker times batch stays well hour. use skip_on_ci() batched files. weekly runner also sets CI=true, skip_on_ci() skip test , defeating batch. Batched files rely .slowBatches filter (skip_on_ci) stay essential run; keep skip_on_cran() CRAN must run . Likewise, always-run core file must call skip_on_ci() actually run push/PR.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: mu2 referencing (R/mu2.R) detects validates mu-referenced parameters; violations produce warnings, errors, SAEM IOV (inter-occasion variability) support R/iov.R special handling mu-referencing hooks sharedControl() function (R/sharedControl.R) defines options common across estimation methods R/nlmixr2Est.R contains central S3 dispatch table – look understand methods exist warning() raised estimation run collected fit’s $runInfo (run-time notes reach user). Warnings emitted estimation routines must therefore concise – keep 75 characters renders one line ($runInfo display adds bullet prefix). prefix method name (e.g. est="vae":) – fit already reports method run.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Avoid AI-typical verbosity: multi-paragraph explanations code comments, worked examples numeric anecdotes embedded comments, restating code obviously . One line usually enough; explain non-obvious . Roxygen descriptions short (sentence two) – keep every @param/@return tag, don’t pad @details tutorial-style prose. NEWS.md organized per version (# nlmixr2est X.Y.Z) way current rxode2 NEWS : user-facing changes first ## New features heading, ## Bug fixes heading. ## Bug fixes several entries, group subsystem/type ### <category> subsections (e.g. ### Estimation, ### Solving, ### Output / tables); single fix can just bullet directly ## Bug fixes. Add entry existing subsection one fits rather creating near-duplicate. Entries past-tense bullets may briefly state fix/feature user-visible effect, multi-paragraph root-cause narration. Unicode source, comments, docs (em dashes, smart quotes, arrows, etc.) – CRAN policy expects plain ASCII. Use --, '/", -> instead.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: R Style: variable function names R must camelCase (e.g., simData, fitSaem, oneCompartmentMix). use snake_case dot.case R objects. C++ Style: Follow Rcpp/Armadillo conventions. Use snake_case camelCase appropriate.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: emitting rxode2 model text programmatically (e.g. augmented sensitivity models R/foceiCovAnalytic.R / R/foceiGradAnalytic.R, inner/pred models R/focei.R): Name every generated output variable rx_<name>_ (leading rx_, trailing _), matching inner model’s rx_pred_ / rx__sens_..._ convention. Two reasons: (1) user can define model variable plain name (e.g. rvar, predf, f1), un-prefixed generated name can collide silently corrupt model; (2) rxode2 parses top-level x = <constant> initial value (x becomes parameter, dropping solve columns) UNLESS name rx_<name>_ form – keep constant assignment real lhs output column. constant sensitivity (e.g. d(R)/d(eta)=0 additive error) comes back column constant, special-casing needed. Use rx_predf_, rx_f1_<dir>, rx_rvarf_, etc. – never predf, f1_..., rvar, rxPredF (plain rxFoo without trailing _ still parsed init). Use = variable read back (output column solve). Use ~ (suppressed assignment) intermediate compute output – extra = output columns shift positional column layout add solve overhead. Declare theta/eta inputs model covariates (ui$allCovs) param(...) solve parameter order fixed positional (values keyed name, pinning order keeps input layout stable across builds). Covariates supplied event data solve time must still declared param(...).

title: Authors and Citation
text: Fidler M, Xiong Y, Schoemaker R, Wilkins J, Trame M, Hooijmaijers R, Post T, Wang W (2026). nlmixr: Nonlinear Mixed Effects Models Population Pharmacokinetics Pharmacodynamics. R package version 7.0.0, https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=nlmixr. Fidler M, Wilkins J, Hooijmaijers R, Post T, Schoemaker R, Trame M, Xiong Y, Wang W (2019). “Nonlinear Mixed-Effects Model Development Simulation Using nlmixr Related R Open-Source Packages.” CPT: Pharmacometrics & Systems Pharmacology, 8(9), 621–633. https://doi.org/10.1002/psp4.12445. Schoemaker R, Fidler M, Laveille C, Wilkins J, Hooijmaijers R, Post T, Trame M, Xiong Y, Wang W (2019). “Performance SAEM FOCEI Algorithms Open-Source, Nonlinear Mixed Effect Modeling Tool nlmixr.” CPT: Pharmacometrics & Systems Pharmacology, 8(12), 923–930. https://doi.org/10.1002/psp4.12471.
code: @Manual{,   title = {{nlmixr}: Nonlinear Mixed Effects Models in Population Pharmacokinetics and Pharmacodynamics},   author = {Matthew Fidler and Yuan Xiong and Rik Schoemaker and Justin Wilkins and Mirjam Trame and Richard Hooijmaijers and Teun Post and Wenping Wang},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 7.0.0},   url = {https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=nlmixr}, } @Article{,   title = {Nonlinear Mixed-Effects Model Development and Simulation Using nlmixr and Related R Open-Source Packages},   author = {Matthew Fidler and Justin Wilkins and Richard Hooijmaijers and Teun Post and Rik Schoemaker and Mirjam Trame and Yuan Xiong and Wenping Wang},   journal = {CPT: Pharmacometrics \& Systems Pharmacology},   year = {2019},   volume = {8},   pages = {621--633},   number = {9},   month = {sep},   abstract = {nlmixr is a free and open-source R package for fitting nonlinear pharmacokinetic (PK), pharmacodynamic (PD), joint PK-PD, and quantitative systems pharmacology mixed-effects models. Currently, nlmixr is capable of fitting both traditional compartmental PK models as well as more complex models implemented using ordinary differential equations. We believe that, over time, it will become a capable, credible alternative to commercial software tools, such as NONMEM, Monolix, and Phoenix NLME.},   address = {Hoboken},   publisher = {John Wiley and Sons Inc.},   url = {https://doi.org/10.1002/psp4.12445}, } @Article{,   title = {Performance of the SAEM and FOCEI Algorithms in the Open-Source, Nonlinear Mixed Effect Modeling Tool nlmixr},   author = {Rik Schoemaker and Matthew Fidler and Christian Laveille and Justin Wilkins and Richard Hooijmaijers and Teun Post and Mirjam Trame and Yuan Xiong and Wenping Wang},   journal = {CPT: Pharmacometrics \& Systems Pharmacology},   year = {2019},   volume = {8},   pages = {923--930},   number = {12},   month = {dec},   abstract = {The free and open-source package nlmixr implements pharmacometric nonlinear mixed effects model parameter estimation in R. It provides a uniform language to define pharmacometric models using ordinary differential equations. Performances of the stochastic approximation expectation-maximization (SAEM) and first order-conditional estimation with interaction (FOCEI) algorithms in nlmixr were compared with those found in the industry standards, Monolix and NONMEM, using the following two scenarios: a simple model fit to 500 sparsely sampled data sets and a range of more complex compartmental models with linear and nonlinear clearance fit to data sets with rich sampling. Estimation results obtained from nlmixr for FOCEI and SAEM matched the corresponding output from NONMEM/FOCEI and Monolix/SAEM closely both in terms of parameter estimates and associated standard errors. These results indicate that nlmixr may provide a viable alternative to existing tools for pharmacometric parameter estimation.},   url = {https://doi.org/10.1002/psp4.12471}, }

title: Copilot instructions for nlmixr2est
text: Purpose - Provide concise guidance future Copilot sessions repository (R package C++ sources). Build, test, lint commands - Load package (compiles C/C++): devtools::load_all() - Run full test suite: devtools::test() - Run single test file (example): testthat::test_file(“tests/testthat/test-focei-1.R”) - Build documentation (regenerates NAMESPACE): devtools::document() - Full R CMD check: devtools::check() shell: R CMD check . - Note: package NeedsCompilation: yes requires C++17 (see src/Makevars). devtools::load_all() usual dev workflow recompile native code. High-level architecture (big picture) - Entry points: R/nlmixr2.R -> nlmixr2Est() (S3 dispatch). See R/nlmixr2Est.R dispatch table. - Estimation methods: method R file control function (e.g., R/saem.R + R/saemControl.R, R/focei.R + R/foceiControl.R). - UI → rxode2 pipeline: model UI objects translated rxode2 via R/rxPipeline.R method-specific RxUiGet handlers (R/saemRxUiGet.R, R/splitModelRxUiGet.R, R/nlmeRxUiGet.R). - Pre-processing hooks: R/hook.R registers pre-processing steps. Implementations live R/preProcess.R (bounded transforms, covariates, data/ui validation, zero-omega handling, literal fix processing). - Core numerical layer: C++ src/ (saem.cpp, nlm.cpp, inner.cpp, cwres.cpp, nearPD.cpp, etc.). expose R interfaces via Rcpp exports (RcppExports.cpp / R/RcppExports.R). - Post-fit accessors: Fits nlmixr2FitData objects; accessors lazy computations implemented R/nmObjGet*.R addCwres()/addNpde(). - Tests fixtures: helper-zzz-fits.R pre-fits models stores .rds fixtures tests/testthat/fixtures avoid re-running long estimations. Key conventions repository-specific patterns - “mu2” (mu-referencing): Special handling detection R/mu2.R. SAEM optimizations depend mu-referencing; validate via mu2 code tests. - Preprocess hooks: Use R/hook.R add/remove pre-processing logic. Search preProcess* files see available hooks. - Control objects: sharedControl() centralizes options common estimators; method-specific control functions delegate . - Native code workflow: C++ changes require recompilation via devtools::load_all() (C++17, see src/Makevars). Pay attention Rcpp Armadillo/Eigen usage. - Tests: Many tests rely cached fixtures (tests/testthat/fixtures). adding tests require long fits, add fixtures avoid CI slowness. - Naming: Estimation methods map 1:1 R/*.R files (saem.R, focei.R, fo.R, nlm.R, etc.). Look understand behavior control options. Files read first (high signal) - CLAUDE.md (repo) — high-level notes architecture commands - README.md / README.Rmd — package overview - R/nlmixr2Est.R — S3 dispatch method registry - R/rxPipeline.R R/saemRxUiGet.R — UI → rxode2 translation - R/hook.R R/preProcess.R — pre-processing hook system - src/saem.cpp, src/nlm.cpp, src/inner.cpp — core numeric kernels - tests/testthat/helper-zzz-fits.R tests/testthat/fixtures/ — test fixtures expectations AI assistant configs - CLAUDE.md present — include guidance reasoning architecture commands. - .claude/settings.local.json exists (local CLAUDE settings); assistant rule files detected. Quick tips Copilot sessions - Prefer reading: R/nlmixr2Est.R, R/rxPipeline.R, R/hook.R proposing estimator changes. - change touching src/, ensure compilation via devtools::load_all() run smallest test covers change. - Avoid re-running long SAEM fits CI; use tests/fixtures helper-zzz-fits.R introducing heavy tests. Contact - uncertain design decision, point R/sharedControl.R method-specific control files precedent.

text: can install development version nlmixr2est GitHub : people, using nlmixr2 directly likely easier.
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("nlmixr2/rxode2") remotes::install_github("nlmixr2/nlmixr2data") remotes::install_github("nlmixr2/lotri") remotes::install_github("nlmixr2/nlmixr2est") library(nlmixr2est) #> Loading required package: nlmixr2data  ## The basic model consists of an ini block that has initial estimates one.compartment <- function() {   ini({     tka <- 0.45; label("Ka")     tcl <- 1; label("Cl")     tv <- 3.45; label("V")     eta.ka ~ 0.6     eta.cl ~ 0.3     eta.v ~ 0.1     add.sd <- 0.7   })   # and a model block with the error specification and model specification   model({     ka <- exp(tka + eta.ka)     cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)     v <- exp(tv + eta.v)     d/dt(depot) = -ka * depot     d/dt(center) = ka * depot - cl / v * center     cp = center / v     cp ~ add(add.sd)   }) }  ## The fit is performed by the function nlmixr/nlmixr2 specifying the model, data and estimate fit <- nlmixr2(one.compartment, theo_sd,  est="saem", saemControl(print=0)) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments are typically ignored in non-interactive mode #> ℹ Need to run with the source intact to parse comments #> → loading into symengine environment... #> → pruning branches (`if`/`else`) of saem model... #> ✔ done #> → finding duplicate expressions in saem model... #> [====|====|====|====|====|====|====|====|====|====] 0:00:00 #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in saem model... #> [====|====|====|====|====|====|====|====|====|====] 0:00:00 #> ✔ done #> → creating rxode2 include directory #> → getting R compile options #> → precompiling headers #> ✔ done #> using C compiler: 'gcc.exe (GCC) 14.2.0' #> ℹ calculate uninformed etas #> ℹ done #> rxode2 3.0.4.9000 using 8 threads (see ?getRxThreads) #>  #> Attaching package: 'rxode2' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:nlmixr2est': #>  #>     boxCox, yeoJohnson #> Calculating covariance matrix #> → loading into symengine environment... #> → pruning branches (`if`/`else`) of saem model... #> ✔ done #> → finding duplicate expressions in saem predOnly model 0... #> → finding duplicate expressions in saem predOnly model 1... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in saem predOnly model 1... #> → finding duplicate expressions in saem predOnly model 2... #> ✔ done #> using C compiler: 'gcc.exe (GCC) 14.2.0' #> → Calculating code: # \donttest{  one.cmt <- function() {   ini({     ## You may label each parameter with a comment     tka <- 0.45 # Log Ka     tcl <- log(c(0, 2.7, 100)) # Log Cl     ## This works with interactive models     ## You may also label the preceding line with label("label text")     tv <- 3.45; label("log V")     ## the label("Label name") works with all models     eta.ka ~ 0.6     eta.cl ~ 0.3     eta.v ~ 0.1     add.sd <- 0.7   })   model({     ka <- exp(tka + eta.ka)     cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)     v <- exp(tv + eta.v)     linCmt() ~ add(add.sd)   }) }  f <- try(nlmixr2(one.cmt, theo_sd, "saem")) #>   #>   #>   #>   #> ℹ parameter labels from comments are typically ignored in non-interactive mode #> ℹ Need to run with the source intact to parse comments #>   #>   #> → loading into symengine environment... #> → pruning branches (`if`/`else`) of saem model... #> ✔ done #> → finding duplicate expressions in saem model... #> ✔ done #> ℹ calculate uninformed etas #> ℹ done #> rxode2 5.1.4 using 2 threads (see ?getRxThreads) #>   no cache: create with `rxCreateCache()` #>  #> Attaching package: ‘rxode2’ #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:nlmixr2est’: #>  #>     boxCox, yeoJohnson #> covMethod="sa" could not be computed; what: Control for advi (automatic differentiation variational inference) in nlmixr2 — adviControl
title: Control for advi (automatic differentiation variational inference) in nlmixr2 — adviControl
text: Variational-inference NLME estimation following Kucukelbir et al. (2017): latent variables transformed unconstrained real coordinate space, Gaussian variational family posited , ELBO maximized stochastic gradient ascent using reparameterization trick.  gradient log-joint obtained FOCEi forward sensitivities (inner per-subject eta gradient outer population sensitivity contraction), automatic differentiation.  whole optimization loop runs C++.

title: Control for advi (automatic differentiation variational inference) in nlmixr2 — adviControl
code: adviControl(   seed = 42L,   iters = 300L,   nMc = 1L,   adviFamily = c("fullRank", "meanField"),   pointEstimate = TRUE,   optim = c("advi", "adam"),   adaptEta = TRUE,   etaCandidates = c(0.01, 0.025, 0.05, 0.1, 0.25),   tau = 1,   alpha = 0.1,   tol = 1e-04,   likelihood = c("focei", "foce", "focep", "laplace"),   returnAdvi = FALSE,   resume = NULL,   print = 1L,   useColor = NULL,   printNcol = NULL,   covMethod = c("advi", "analytic", "r,s", "r", "s", ""),   optExpression = TRUE,   sumProd = FALSE,   literalFix = TRUE,   literalFixRes = TRUE,   addProp = c("combined2", "combined1"),   calcTables = TRUE,   compress = FALSE,   adjObf = TRUE,   ci = 0.95,   sigdig = NULL,   sigdigTable = NULL,   stickyRecalcN = 4,   maxOdeRecalc = 5,   odeRecalcFactor = 10^(0.5),   indTolRelax = TRUE,   eventSens = c("jump", "fd"),   rxControl = NULL,   ... )

title: Control for advi (automatic differentiation variational inference) in nlmixr2 — adviControl
text: seed Random seed ADVI optimization (reparameterization sampling); default 42.  Monte-Carlo gradient stochastic, fixed seed makes every fit reproducible.  Reparameterization noise drawn counter-based stream keyed global iteration index, shorter run bit--bit prefix longer one results independent number cores. iters Total number ADVI (stochastic gradient ascent) iterations. nMc Number Monte-Carlo samples used approximate ELBO gradient iteration (paper's `M`; typically 1-10). adviFamily Variational family unconstrained space. `"fullRank"` (default) uses block full-rank Gaussian: dense `neta x neta` Cholesky factor per subject plus dense block population vector (mean-field across blocks).  `"meanField"` uses fully factorized (diagonal) Gaussian.  Mean-field faster known underestimate marginal variances. pointEstimate `TRUE` (default) run variational-EM hybrid: variational posterior covers per-subject etas , population parameters (thetas / omega / residual error) point estimates maximized ADVI gradient (stochastic maximum likelihood); output semantics match FOCEi/SAEM.  `FALSE` run full Bayes: variational posterior also covers unconstrained population vector, flat priors. optim Stochastic optimizer.  `"advi"` (default) uses paper's adaptive step-size sequence (Eqs 10-11); `"adam"` uses Adam. adaptEta `TRUE` (default) adaptively choose step-size scale `eta` short search `etaCandidates` main loop; `FALSE` use fixed `eta` (first `etaCandidates` entry). etaCandidates Candidate step-size scales searched `adaptEta` `TRUE` (paper searches `c(0.01, 0.1, 1, 10, 100)`). tau Stabilizing constant `tau > 0` step-size denominator (paper Eq 10); step-size insensitive . alpha Weighting `alpha` (0, 1) new vs old gradient information step-size memory recursion (paper Eq 11). tol Convergence tolerance relative change ELBO; loop may stop early change stays .  `0` disables early stopping (run `iters`). likelihood Inner likelihood used per-subject objective gradient, run FOCEi inner interface: `"focei"` (default), `"foce"`, `"focep"`, `"laplace"`. returnAdvi `TRUE` return raw ADVI optimization object instead nlmixr2 fit. resume Optional warm-resume state: previous `est="advi"` fit (`$env$adviState`).  optimization continues state `iters` iterations, bit--bit identical single fresh run combined length (counter-based RNG keyed global iteration index). print Either scalar print-frequency (`0` = suppress, `1` (default) = every evaluation, `N` = every Nth), pre-built [iterPrintControl()] object. Equivalent `iterPrintControl(every = print, ncol = printNcol, useColor = useColor)`. useColor Logical (`NULL`) emit ANSI bold/color escapes iteration print. `NULL` (default) defers [crayon::has_color()]. printNcol Integer (`NULL`) parameter columns per row wrapping. `NULL` (default) uses `floor((getOption("width") - 23) / 12)`. covMethod Method calculating covariance.      discussion, R Hessian matrix objective     function. S matrix sum individual's     gradient cross-product (evaluated individual empirical     Bayes estimates). "sa" (default) Use stochastic-approximation Fisher Information  Matrix.  estimation, dedicated covariance phase (nSaCov  iterations) holds parameters converged estimate keeps  resimulating individual parameters, Monte-Carlo averaging Louis  observed-information integrand converged FIM decoupled cooling  schedule (approach used Monolix; Kuhn & Lavielle 2005).  Always includes  every estimated population parameter (theta, Omega diagonal  variances, residual). "analytic" Compute FOCEI analytic observed-information covariance  converged SAEM estimates.  model analytic-covariance  scope (e.g. linCmt(), non-normal likelihood, non-SD IOV  parameterization) result positive definite, falls back  linearized Fisher information (linFim) message. "linFim" Use Linearized Fisher Information Matrix calculate covariance. "fim" Use Fisher Information Matrix accumulated SAEM  estimation calculate covariance.  Like sa inverts observed  information full theta + Omega diagonal + residual covariance,  uses (noisier) estimation-phase matrix rather dedicated cov phase. fim sa simulation-based Fisher information covers  structural theta, Omega diagonal variances, additive residual error.  -diagonal Omega covariances proportional/combined residual error  estimated reliably simulation FIM (complete-data correction unstable  -subject variability dominates residual), variance-block standard  errors spliced linearized FIM (linFim). "r,s" Uses sandwich matrix calculate covariance, : \(R^-1 \times S \times R^-1\) "r" Uses Hessian matrix calculate covariance \(2\times R^-1\) "s" Uses crossproduct matrix calculate covariance \(4\times S^-1\) "" calculate covariance step. optExpression Optimize rxode2 expression speed calculation. default turned . sumProd boolean indicating model change multiplication high precision multiplication sums high precision sums using PreciseSums package.  default FALSE. literalFix boolean, substitute fixed population values literals re-adjust ui parameter estimates optimization; Default `TRUE`. literalFixRes boolean, substitute fixed population values literals re-adjust ui parameter estimates optimization; Default `TRUE`. addProp Type additive-plus-proportional error: `"combined1"`, standard deviations add: $$y = f + (+ b\times f^c) \times \varepsilon$$; `"combined2"`, variances add: $$y = f + \sqrt{^2 + b^2\times f^{2\times c}} \times \varepsilon$$. y = observed, f = predicted, = additive sd, b = proportional/power sd, c = power exponent (1 proportional case). calcTables boolean determine foceiFit calculate tables. default TRUE compress object compressed items adjObf boolean indicate objective function adjusted closer NONMEM's default objective function.  default TRUE ci Confidence level tables.  default 0.95 95% confidence. sigdig Specifies "significant digits" ode solving requests.  specified controls relative absolute tolerances ODE solvers.  default tolerance 0.5*10^(-sigdig-2) regular ODEs. sensitivity equations default 0.5*10\^(-sigdig-1.5) (sensitivity changes applicable liblsoda).  also controls atol/rtol steady state solutions. ssAtol/ssRtol 0.5*10\^(-sigdig) sensitivities 0.5*10\^(-sigdig+0.625).  default unspecified (NULL) uses standard atol/rtol. sigdigTable Significant digits final output table. specified, matches significant digits `sigdig` optimization algorithm.  `sigdig` NULL, use 3. stickyRecalcN number bad ODE solves reducing atol/rtol rest problem. maxOdeRecalc Maximum number times reduce ODE tolerances try resolve system bad ODE solve. odeRecalcFactor ODE recalculation factor ODE solving goes bad, factor rtol/atol reduced indTolRelax `TRUE` (default), subjects whose ODE solve produced NaN/Inf tolerances relaxed, relaxed tolerance persists across optimizer calls (sticky). `FALSE`, subjects tolerances relaxed retry tolerances reset afterward. eventSens Controls dosing/event-parameter (`alag`, `F`, `rate`, `dur`) sensitivities computed THETA/ETA gradients: `"jump"` (default) uses rxode2's analytic event sensitivities; `"fd"` uses legacy finite-difference behavior. rxControl `rxode2` ODE solving options fitting, created `rxControl()` ... arguments control SAEM.

what: Control options for the agq estimation method — agqControl
title: Control options for the agq estimation method — agqControl
text: control options adaptive Gauss-Hermite quadrature likelihood.  Note nAGQ=1 Laplace method.

title: Control options for the agq estimation method — agqControl
code: agqControl(   sigdig = 3,   nAGQ = 2,   ...,   interaction = TRUE,   agqLow = -Inf,   agqHi = Inf )

title: Control options for the agq estimation method — agqControl
text: sigdig Optimization significant digits; controls inner/outer optimization tolerance (10^-sigdig), boundary check tolerance (5*10^(-sigdig+1)), ODE solver tolerances.  solver tolerances split solver stiffness keep atol well rtol: stiff solver (lsoda/liblsoda – auto-switching method – plus indLin, default) uses rtol = 10^(-sigdig-3), atol = 10^(-sigdig-5), non-stiff explicit dop853 uses looser rtol = 10^-sigdig, atol = 10^(-sigdig-3).  sensitivity (atolSens/rtolSens) steady-state (ssAtol/ssRtol) tolerances run one order looser corresponding main tolerance.  default sigdig = 4 stiff solve atol = 1e-9, rtol = 1e-7. nAGQ Number Gauss-Hermite adaptive quadrature points. `0` disables AGQ; `1` equivalent Laplace. Cost grows quickly ETAs: EBE found, expect `nAGQ^neta` (even `nAGQ`) `(nAGQ^neta)-1` (odd `nAGQ`) additional evaluations per subject. ... Parameters used default `foceiControl()` interaction boolean, Interaction term model, case default `TRUE`; adaptive quadrature, normal distribution Hessian calculated foce() approximation agqLow lower bound adaptive quadrature log-likelihood. default -Inf; original nlmixr's gnlmm -700. agqHi upper bound adaptive quadrature log-likelihood.  default Inf; original nlmixr's gnlmm 400.

=  Litter size after 21 days, and the modeled value  r <- rats r$dv <- r$x  # Time is not used in this model, but it is required in nlmixr2 # currently, add a dummy value  r$time <- 0  f <- function() {   ini({     t1 <- 1     t2 <- 1     t3 <- 1     eta1 ~ 1   })   model({     lp <- t1 * x1 + t2 * x2 + (x1 + x2*t3) * eta1     p <- pnorm(lp)     m1 <- m # need to add outside of model specification     x ~ dbinom(m1, p)   }) }  fit <- nlmixr(f, r, est="agq") #>   #>   #>   #>   #> ℹ parameter labels from comments are typically ignored in non-interactive mode #> ℹ Need to run with the source intact to parse comments #> Warning: some etas defaulted to non-mu referenced, possible parsing error: eta1 #> as a work-around try putting the mu-referenced expression on a simple line #> Warning: some etas defaulted to non-mu referenced, possible parsing error: eta1 #> as a work-around try putting the mu-referenced expression on a simple line #> → loading into symengine environment... #> → pruning branches (`if`/`else`) of full model... #> ✔ done #> → calculate ∂(f)/∂(η) #> → finding duplicate expressions in inner model... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in inner model... #> → finding duplicate expressions in EBE model... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in EBE model... #> → compiling inner model... #>   #>   #> ✔ done #> → finding duplicate expressions in FD model... #> → compiling EBE model... #>   #>   #> ✔ done #> → compiling events FD model... #>   #>   #> ✔ done #> → Calculating residuals/tables #> ✔ done   p <- pump  p$dv <- p$y p$time <- 0 # dummy time  f <- function() {   ini({     t1 <- 1     t2 <- 1     t3 <- 1     t4 <- 1     eta1 ~ 1   })   model({     if (group == 1) {        lp <- t1 + t2 * logtstd     } else {        lp <- t3 + t4 * logtstd     }     lp <- lp + eta1     lam <- exp(lp)     y ~ dpois(lam)   }) }  fit <- nlmixr(f, p, est="agq", control=agqControl(nAGQ=5)) #>   #>   #>   #>   #> ℹ parameter labels from comments are typically ignored in non-interactive mode #> ℹ Need to run with the source intact to parse comments #> → loading into symengine environment... #> → pruning branches (`if`/`else`) of full model... #> ✔ done #> → calculate ∂(f)/∂(η) #> → finding duplicate expressions in inner model... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in inner model... #> → finding duplicate expressions in EBE model... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in EBE model... #> → compiling inner model... #>   #>   #> ✔ done #> → finding duplicate expressions in FD model... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in FD model... #> → compiling EBE model... #>   #>   #> ✔ done #> → compiling events FD model... #>   #>   #> ✔ done #> → Calculating residuals/tables #> ✔ done  one.cmt <- function() {   ini({     ## You may label each parameter with a comment     tka <- 0.45 # Log Ka     tcl <- log(c(0, 2.7, 100)) # Log Cl     ## This works with interactive models     ## You may also label the preceding line with label("label text")     tv <- 3.45; label("log V")     ## the label("Label name") works with all models     eta.ka ~ 0.6     eta.cl ~ 0.3     eta.v ~ 0.1     add.sd <- 0.7  })  model({    ka <- exp(tka + eta.ka)    cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)    v <- exp(tv + eta.v)    linCmt() ~ add(add.sd)  }) }  fit <- nlmixr(one.cmt, theo_sd, est="agq") #>   #>   #>   #>   #> ℹ parameter labels from comments are typically ignored in non-interactive mode #> ℹ Need to run with the source intact to parse comments #> → loading into symengine environment... #> → pruning branches (`if`/`else`) of full model... #> ✔ done #> → calculate ∂(f)/∂(η) #> → calculate ∂(R²)/∂(η) #> → finding duplicate expressions in inner model... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in inner model... #> → finding duplicate expressions in EBE model... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in EBE model... #> → compiling inner model... #>   #>   #> ✔ done #> → finding duplicate expressions in FD model... #> → compiling EBE model... #>   #>   #> ✔ done #> → compiling events FD model... #>   #>   #> ✔ done #> → Calculating residuals/tables #> ✔ done  # }

title: Control for bobyqa estimation method in nlmixr2 — bobyqaControl
text: npt Number points quadratic approximation objective; must `[n+2, (n+1)(n+2)/2]`. Defaults `min(n*2, n+2)`. rhobeg Initial trust region radius (`rhoend`, must satisfy `0 < rhoend < rhobeg`). Defaults `min(0.95, 0.2*max(abs(par)))`; adjusted upward smaller `abs(upper-lower)/2`. rhoend Final trust region radius.  `NULL` (default) derived `sigdig` way `foceiControl()` (`10^(-sigdig-1)`); otherwise minqa `1e-6*rhobeg` default applies. iprint Controls amount printing (`0`=none, `1`=start/end , `2`=new rho, `3`=every function evaluation, `>3`=every `iprint` evaluations). Default `0`. maxfun maximum allowed number function evaluations. exceeded, method terminate. returnBobyqa return bobyqa output instead nlmixr2 fit stickyRecalcN number bad ODE solves reducing atol/rtol rest problem. maxOdeRecalc Maximum number times reduce ODE tolerances try resolve system bad ODE solve. odeRecalcFactor ODE recalculation factor ODE solving goes bad, factor rtol/atol reduced indTolRelax `TRUE` (default), subjects whose ODE solve produced NaN/Inf tolerances relaxed, relaxed tolerance persists across optimizer calls (sticky). `FALSE`, subjects tolerances relaxed retry tolerances reset afterward. useColor Logical (`NULL`) emit ANSI bold/color escapes iteration print. `NULL` (default) defers [crayon::has_color()]. printNcol Integer (`NULL`) parameter columns per row wrapping. `NULL` (default) uses `floor((getOption("width") - 23) / 12)`. print Either scalar print-frequency (`0` = suppress, `1` (default) = every evaluation, `N` = every Nth), pre-built [iterPrintControl()] object. Equivalent `iterPrintControl(every = print, ncol = printNcol, useColor = useColor)`. normType Parameter normalization/scaling used get scaled initial values scaleType, form Vscaled = (Vunscaled-C1)/C2 (see Feature Scaling; rescale2 follows OptdesX manual): "rescale2" scales parameters (-1, 1); "rescale" (min-max) scales (0, 1); "mean" centers mean range (0, 1); "std" standardizes mean/sd; "len" scales unit (Euclidean) length; "constant" performs normalization (C1=0, C2=1). scaleType scaling scheme nlmixr2: "nlmixr2" (default) scales (current-init)*scaleC[] + scaleTo, scaleTo normType scales scaleC; "norm" uses simple scaling normType; "mult" scales multiplicatively current/init*scaleTo; "multAdd" scales linearly ((current-init)+scaleTo) parameters exponential block (e.g. exp(theta)) multiplicatively otherwise. scaleCmax Maximum value scaleC prevent overflow. scaleCmin Minimum value scaleC prevent underflow. scaleC Scaling constant used scaleType="nlmixr2"; specified, chosen parameter type keep gradient sizes similar log scale: `1` exp()-transformed/power/boxCox/ yeoJohnson parameters, `0.5*abs(est)` additive/proportional/ lognormal error parameters, `abs(1/digamma(est+1))` factorials, `log(abs(est))*abs(est)` log-scale parameters. May set explicitly per parameter defaults apply well. scaleTo Scale initial parameter estimate value. default 1.  zero , scaling performed. rxControl `rxode2` ODE solving options fitting, created `rxControl()` optExpression Optimize rxode2 expression speed calculation. default turned . sumProd boolean indicating model change multiplication high precision multiplication sums high precision sums using PreciseSums package.  default FALSE. literalFix boolean, substitute fixed population values literals re-adjust ui parameter estimates optimization; Default `TRUE`. literalFixRes boolean, substitute fixed population values literals re-adjust ui parameter estimates optimization; Default `TRUE`. addProp Type additive-plus-proportional error: `"combined1"`, standard deviations add: $$y = f + (+ b\times f^c) \times \varepsilon$$; `"combined2"`, variances add: $$y = f + \sqrt{^2 + b^2\times f^{2\times c}} \times \varepsilon$$. y = observed, f = predicted, = additive sd, b = proportional/power sd, c = power exponent (1 proportional case). calcTables boolean determine foceiFit calculate tables. default TRUE compress object compressed items covMethod Method calculating covariance.  "r" (default) uses nlmixr2's nlmixr2Hess() Hessian; "" skips covariance step. adjObf boolean indicate objective function adjusted closer NONMEM's default objective function.  default TRUE ci Confidence level tables.  default 0.95 95% confidence. sigdig Optimization significant digits; controls inner/outer optimization tolerance (10^-sigdig), boundary check tolerance (5*10^(-sigdig+1)), ODE solver tolerances.  solver tolerances split solver stiffness keep atol well rtol: stiff solver (lsoda/liblsoda – auto-switching method – plus indLin, default) uses rtol = 10^(-sigdig-3), atol = 10^(-sigdig-5), non-stiff explicit dop853 uses looser rtol = 10^-sigdig, atol = 10^(-sigdig-3).  sensitivity (atolSens/rtolSens) steady-state (ssAtol/ssRtol) tolerances run one order looser corresponding main tolerance.  default sigdig = 4 stiff solve atol = 1e-9, rtol = 1e-7. sigdigTable Significant digits final output table. specified, matches significant digits `sigdig` optimization algorithm.  `sigdig` NULL, use 3. eventSens Controls dosing/event-parameter (`alag`, `F`, `rate`, `dur`) sensitivities computed THETA/ETA gradients: `"jump"` (default) uses rxode2's analytic event sensitivities; `"fd"` uses legacy finite-difference behavior. ... Ignored parameters

text: Matthew L. Fidler

title: Control for bobyqa estimation method in nlmixr2 — bobyqaControl
code: # \donttest{ # A logit regression example with emax model  dsn <- data.frame(i=1:1000) dsn$time <- exp(rnorm(1000)) dsn$DV=rbinom(1000,1,exp(-1+dsn$time)/(1+exp(-1+dsn$time)))  mod <- function() {  ini({    E0 <- 0.5    Em <- 0.5    E50 <- 2    g <- fix(2)  })  model({    v <- E0+Em*time^g/(E50^g+time^g)    ll(bin) ~ DV * v - log(1 + exp(v))  }) }  fit2 <- nlmixr(mod, dsn, est="bobyqa") #>   #>   #>   #>   #> ℹ parameter labels from comments are typically ignored in non-interactive mode #> ℹ Need to run with the source intact to parse comments #> → pruning branches (`if`/`else`) of population log-likelihood model... #> ✔ done #> → loading llik model into symengine environment... #> → finding duplicate expressions in population log-likelihood model... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in population log-likelihood model... #> ✔ done #>   #>   #> → calculating covariance #> ✔ done #> → loading into symengine environment... #> → pruning branches (`if`/`else`) of full model... #> ✔ done #> → finding duplicate expressions in EBE model... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in EBE model... #> → compiling EBE model... #>   #>   #> ✔ done #> → Calculating residuals/tables #> ✔ done  print(fit2) #> ── nlmixr² log-likelihood bobyqa ── #>  #>           OBJF      AIC      BIC Log-likelihood Condition#(Cov) Condition#(Cor) #> lPop -720.4741 1123.403 1138.126      -558.7015        4044.283        210.6512 #>  #> ── Time (sec $time): ── #>  #>            setup  optimize covariance preprocess postprocess table compress #> elapsed 0.856484 0.8373057  8.262e-06      0.045       0.009 0.036    0.001 #>              other #> elapsed 0.09520204 #>  #> ── ($parFixed or $parFixedDf): ── #>  #>        Est.     SE  %RSE   Back-transformed(95%CI) #> E0  -0.5761 0.2304 39.99 -0.5761 (-1.028, -0.1246) #> Em    8.773  8.361 95.31     8.773 (-7.614, 25.16) #> E50   4.054  2.863 70.62     4.054 (-1.557, 9.666) #> g     2.000  FIXED FIXED                     2.000 #>   #>   Covariance Type ($covMethod): r #>   Censoring ($censInformation): No censoring #>  #> ── Fit Data (object is a modified tibble): ── #> # A tibble: 1,000 × 5 #>   ID      TIME    DV  IPRED      v #>   <fct>  <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1 1     0.0564     0 -0.447 -0.574 #> 2 1     0.0595     1 -1.02  -0.574 #> 3 1     0.0650     1 -1.02  -0.574 #> # ℹ 997 more rows  # you can also get the bobyqa output with  fit2$bobyqa #> parameter estimates: -0.576143958149363, 8.77251588922663, 4.05410531800092  #> objective: 558.701494345629  #> number of function evaluations: 391  # }

what: Translate a mixed `linCmt()`/ODE model to all-ODEs — .preProcessLinCmtOde
title: Translate a mixed `linCmt()`/ODE model to all-ODEs — .preProcessLinCmtOde
text: rxode2 requires `linCmt()` compartments last states solve (`op$linOffset = neq - numLin - numLinSens`), compartment number one past ODE states.  FOCEi inner model adds ODE state per eta, nlm family one per theta, pushes depot/central past compartment numbers data translated (`.foceiPreProcessData()` uses plain model).  dose silently lands sensitivity state – every prediction comes back 0.  Solving linear part ODEs removes `linCmt()` block, numbering agrees . SAEM builds sensitivity states left alone.

what: Nudge exactly-zero theta initial estimates off zero (FOCEi family) — .preProcessZeroTheta
title: Nudge exactly-zero theta initial estimates off zero (FOCEi family) — .preProcessZeroTheta
text: FOCEi scales linear parameter native magnitude `|init|`, `0` (scale) population parameter initialized exactly `0` (parameter freezes).  hook moves every estimated, non-fixed `theta` whose initial estimate exactly `0` `+zeroTheta` within bounds, otherwise `-zeroTheta`; neither within bounds errors.  runs FOCEi family (`foceiControl`), runs `.preProcessBoundedTransform` nudged value gets transformed.

title: Control options for the FO estimation method — foControl
text: sigdig Optimization significant digits; controls inner/outer optimization tolerance (10^-sigdig), boundary check tolerance (5*10^(-sigdig+1)), ODE solver tolerances.  solver tolerances split solver stiffness keep atol well rtol: stiff solver (lsoda/liblsoda – auto-switching method – plus indLin, default) uses rtol = 10^(-sigdig-3), atol = 10^(-sigdig-5), non-stiff explicit dop853 uses looser rtol = 10^-sigdig, atol = 10^(-sigdig-3).  sensitivity (atolSens/rtolSens) steady-state (ssAtol/ssRtol) tolerances run one order looser corresponding main tolerance.  default sigdig = 4 stiff solve atol = 1e-9, rtol = 1e-7. ... Parameters used default `foceiConrol()` posthoc Logical indicating estimation method calculate `foce` posthoc predicted parameters. interaction Interaction term model; ignored fo fo Logical indicating estimation method FO (first order), controlled estimation method ignored.

what: Control options for the foce estimation method — foceControl
title: Control options for the foce estimation method — foceControl
text: first order option without interaction residuals etas.

title: Control options for the foce estimation method — foceControl
code: foceControl(sigdig = 3, ..., interaction = FALSE)

title: Control options for the foce estimation method — foceControl
text: sigdig Optimization significant digits; controls inner/outer optimization tolerance (10^-sigdig), boundary check tolerance (5*10^(-sigdig+1)), ODE solver tolerances.  solver tolerances split solver stiffness keep atol well rtol: stiff solver (lsoda/liblsoda – auto-switching method – plus indLin, default) uses rtol = 10^(-sigdig-3), atol = 10^(-sigdig-5), non-stiff explicit dop853 uses looser rtol = 10^-sigdig, atol = 10^(-sigdig-3).  sensitivity (atolSens/rtolSens) steady-state (ssAtol/ssRtol) tolerances run one order looser corresponding main tolerance.  default sigdig = 4 stiff solve atol = 1e-9, rtol = 1e-7. ... Parameters used default `foceiControl()` interaction Interaction term model, case default `FALSE`; changed, use `focei` instead

title: Control Options for FOCEi — foceiControl
text: sigdig Optimization significant digits; controls inner/outer optimization tolerance (10^-sigdig), boundary check tolerance (5*10^(-sigdig+1)), ODE solver tolerances.  solver tolerances split solver stiffness keep atol well rtol: stiff solver (lsoda/liblsoda – auto-switching method – plus indLin, default) uses rtol = 10^(-sigdig-3), atol = 10^(-sigdig-5), non-stiff explicit dop853 uses looser rtol = 10^-sigdig, atol = 10^(-sigdig-3).  sensitivity (atolSens/rtolSens) steady-state (ssAtol/ssRtol) tolerances run one order looser corresponding main tolerance.  default sigdig = 4 stiff solve atol = 1e-9, rtol = 1e-7. ... Ignored parameters epsilon Precision estimate n1qn1 optimization. maxInnerIterations Number iterations n1qn1 optimization. maxOuterIterations Maximum number L-BFGS-B optimization outer problem. n1qn1nsim Number function evaluations n1qn1 optimization. print Either scalar print-frequency (`0` = suppress, `1` (default) = every evaluation, `N` = every Nth), pre-built [iterPrintControl()] object. Equivalent `iterPrintControl(every = print, ncol = printNcol, useColor = useColor)`. printNcol Integer (`NULL`) parameter columns per row wrapping. `NULL` (default) uses `floor((getOption("width") - 23) / 12)`. scaleTo Scale initial parameter estimate value. default 1.  zero , scaling performed. scaleObjective Scale initial objective function value.  default 0 (meaning scale) normType Parameter normalization/scaling used get scaled initial values scaleType, form Vscaled = (Vunscaled-C1)/C2 (see Feature Scaling; rescale2 follows OptdesX manual): "rescale2" scales parameters (-1, 1); "rescale" (min-max) scales (0, 1); "mean" centers mean range (0, 1); "std" standardizes mean/sd; "len" scales unit (Euclidean) length; "constant" performs normalization (C1=0, C2=1). scaleType scaling scheme nlmixr2: "nlmixr2" (default) scales (current-init)*scaleC[] + scaleTo, scaleTo normType scales scaleC; "norm" uses simple scaling normType; "mult" scales multiplicatively current/init*scaleTo; "multAdd" scales linearly ((current-init)+scaleTo) parameters exponential block (e.g. exp(theta)) multiplicatively otherwise. scaleCmax Maximum value scaleC prevent overflow. scaleCmin Minimum value scaleC prevent underflow. scaleCband Length-2 increasing pair `c(low, high)` (default `c(0.1, 10)`).  `theta`'s derivative-based scaling constant (`1/|init|` linear parameter, transform-specific formula) kept lands inside band, otherwise replaced parameter's native magnitude `|init|`.  catches singular cases – `1/|init|` blowing small covariate initial estimate, `log()` init `1`, `logit` interval midpoint, `factorial`/`gamma` digamma zero – leaving well-scaled common case (results) untouched. scaleC Scaling constant used scaleType="nlmixr2"; specified, chosen parameter type keep gradient sizes similar log scale: `1` exp()-transformed/power/boxCox/ yeoJohnson parameters, `0.5*abs(est)` additive/proportional/ lognormal error parameters, `abs(1/digamma(est+1))` factorials, `log(abs(est))*abs(est)` log-scale parameters. May set explicitly per parameter defaults apply well. scaleC0 Number adjust scaling factor initial gradient zero. derivEps Forward difference tolerances (relative, absolute); step size h = abs(x)*derivEps[1] + derivEps[2]. derivMethod Derivative method outer problem: "switch", "central", "forward". "switch" starts forward toggles central abs(delta(OFV)) <= derivSwitchTol. derivSwitchTol tolerance switch forward central differences. covDerivMethod indicates method calculating derivatives calculating covariance components (Hessian S). covMethod Method calculating covariance.  "r,s" (default) sandwich estimator (see ).  "analytic" uses exact analytic observed-information R-matrix (reported \(R^{-1}\)) additionally returns residual Omega standard errors; covers FOCEI/FOCE fits additive, proportional, combined error, mu-referenced/covariate/structural parameters (non-mu-referenced etas), SD-scale inter-occasion variability, emits message falls back finite-difference Hessian anything scope (FO, nAGQ > 1, censoring, DV-transformed error, bounded-parameter transforms, structural theta shared two etas, non-SD iovXform, pure-proportional variance vanishes near-zero prediction).  finite-difference methods use R (Hessian) S (sum individual gradient cross-products empirical Bayes estimates): "r,s" sandwich (solve(R)%*%S%*%solve(R)), "r" Hessian-based (solve(R)), "s" cross-product-based (solve(S)), "" skip covariance step.  "sa" (SAEM Louis stochastic-approximation FIM) "imp" (importance-sampling Monte-Carlo observed information) also accepted method; computed post-fit converged estimates decoupled recompute engine. covSolveTol absolute/relative ODE tolerance covariance solves – augmented-sensitivity solves behind covMethod="analytic" perturbed solves behind finite-difference methods.  NULL (default) derives tight tolerance sigdig; supply number override . covFull shape fit$cov.  TRUE (default) installs full theta + residual sigma + Omega covariance (assembled analytically covMethod="analytic", central finite differences parameter set otherwise).  finite-difference methods follows covMethod: "r,s" full sandwich solve(Rfull) %*% Sfull %*% solve(Rfull), "s" solve(Sfull), "r" solve(Rfull).  FALSE installs structural-theta block (historical shape). fast TRUE, compute outer (population) gradient analytically Almquist (2015) sensitivity equations instead finite differences, use Eq-48 random-effect extrapolation next inner-problem starting values.  Requires analytic-scope model (single additive/proportional Gaussian endpoint); --scope models fall back finite-difference gradient message (linCmt() log-likelihood models downgrade fast=FALSE front).  unspecified, outer optimizer defaults "lbfgsb3c" (vs "nlminb" fast=FALSE); pairing fast=TRUE derivative-free outerOpt reverts fast=FALSE.  *f methods (e.g. foceif) default TRUE. hessEps double value representing epsilon Hessian calculation. used R matrix calculation. hessEpsLlik double value representing epsilon Hessian calculation focei generalized log-likelihood estimation.  used R matrix calculation. optimHessType Hessian type numeric-difference individual Hessians generalized log-likelihood estimation: "central" (matches R's `optimHess()`, default) "forward" (faster). optimHessCovType Hessian type numeric-difference individual Hessians used covariance step/final likelihood: "central" (accurate, used ) "forward". censOption Treatment second derivative censored (M2/M3/M4/BLQ) observations FOCEI family.  "gauss" (default) keeps historic uncensored Gauss-Newton curvature, matching common PMx tools; "laplace" uses exact censored second derivative objective (proper Laplace inner Hessian analytic covariance).  Accepted saemControl/nlmControl uniform interface inert – SAEM (stochastic EM) Laplace inner Hessian, NLM uses finite-difference Hessian already reflects censoring exactly. eventType Event gradient type dosing events; Can "central" "forward" eventSens Controls dosing/event-parameter (`alag`, `F`, `rate`, `dur`) sensitivities computed THETA/ETA gradients: `"jump"` (default) uses rxode2's analytic event sensitivities; `"fd"` uses legacy finite-difference behavior. centralDerivEps Central difference tolerances (relative, absolute); step size h = abs(x)*derivEps[1] + derivEps[2]. lbfgsLmm integer giving number BFGS updates retained "L-BFGS-B" method, defaults 7. lbfgsPgtol Projected-gradient convergence tolerance "L-BFGS-B": iteration stops max(| proj g_i |) <= lbfgsPgtol. Defaults `0` (check suppressed). lbfgsFactr Convergence factor "L-BFGS-B": converges objective reduction within lbfgsFactr * .Machine$double.eps. Default `1e10` (~4 sigdigs, 2e-6). eigen boolean indicating eigenvectors calculated include condition number calculation. diagXform Transformation used diagonal chol(solve(omega)) (FOCEi-estimated parameters): one "sqrt" (default), "log", "identity". iovXform Transformation used diagonal IOV: one "sd", "var", "logsd", "logvar". sumProd boolean indicating model change multiplication high precision multiplication sums high precision sums using PreciseSums package.  default FALSE. optExpression Optimize rxode2 expression speed calculation. default turned . literalFix boolean, substitute fixed population values literals re-adjust ui parameter estimates optimization; Default `TRUE`. literalFixRes boolean, substitute fixed population values literals re-adjust ui parameter estimates optimization; Default `TRUE`. ci Confidence level tables.  default 0.95 95% confidence. useColor Logical (`NULL`) emit ANSI bold/color escapes iteration print. `NULL` (default) defers [crayon::has_color()]. boundTol Tolerance boundary issues. calcTables boolean determine foceiFit calculate tables. default TRUE noAbort Boolean indicate abort FOCEi evaluation runs troubles.  (default TRUE) interaction Boolean indicate FOCEi used (TRUE) instead FOCE (FALSE) foce Controls FOCE (interaction = FALSE) evaluates     residual variance R inner objective; ignored FOCEi.  Either     "nonmem" (default) "foce+":  "nonmem" freezes R eta = 0 population     prediction holds constant across inner optimization, matching     NONMEM's FOCE.  Advantage: reproduces NONMEM FOCE objective standard     errors, ODE model agrees closed-form (linCmt)     equivalent.  Disadvantage: R ignores individual (conditional)     heteroscedasticity, can slightly less accurate     "foce+" proportional/combined error. "foce+" evaluates R current conditional     eta (live variance), keeping truncated FOCE     inner gradient.  Advantage: uses conditional variance     bit accurate NONMEM's FOCE cases.     Disadvantage: match NONMEM FOCE.  FOCE     behavior nlmixr2est 6.0.1 earlier.     use gradient eta like full focei     method, accurate focei. cholSEtol tolerance Generalized Cholesky Decomposition.  Defaults suggested (.Machine$double.eps)^(1/3) cholAccept Tolerance accept Generalized Cholesky Decomposition R S matrix. resetEtaP P-value resetting individual ETA 0 optimization, based z-test chol(omega^-1) %*% eta eta/sd(allEtas). `0` = never reset, `1` = always reset. resetThetaP P-value resetting mu-referenced THETAs based ETA drift, checked start near local minimum (see resetThetaCheckPer). `0` = never reset; `1` allowed. resetThetaFinalP represents p-value reseting population mu-referenced THETA parameters based ETA drift optimization, resetting optimization one final time. diagOmegaBoundUpper Upper bound diagonal omega matrix, diag(omega)*diagOmegaBoundUpper. `1` = upper bound. diagOmegaBoundLower Lower bound diagonal omega matrix, diag(omega)/diagOmegaBoundLower. `1` = lower bound. cholSEOpt Boolean indicating generalized Cholesky used optimizing. cholSECov Boolean indicating generalized Cholesky used calculating Covariance Matrix. fo boolean indicating FO approximation routine. covTryHarder R matrix non-positive definite corrected non-positive definite try estimating Hessian unscaled parameter space. outerOpt optimization method outer problem innerOpt optimization method inner problem (implemented yet.) rhobeg Initial trust region radius bobyqa outer optimizer (`rhoend`, must satisfy `0 < rhoend < rhobeg`). Default `0.2` (20 `abs(upper-lower)/2`. (bobyqa) rhoend Final trust region radius. defined, `10^(-sigdig-1)` used. (bobyqa) npt Number points bobyqa's quadratic approximation objective; must `[n+2, (n+1)(n+2)/2]`. Defaults `2*n + 1`. (bobyqa) rel.tol Relative tolerance nlminb stops (nlmimb). x.tol X tolerance nlmixr2 optimizer eval.max Number maximum evaluations objective function (nlmimb) iter.max Maximum number iterations allowed (nlmimb) abstol Absolute tolerance nlmixr2 optimizer (BFGS) reltol tolerance nlmixr2 (BFGS) resetHessianAndEta boolean representing individual Hessian reset ETAs reset using option resetEtaP. muModel Mu-referenced-FOCEI-family regression variant: "none" (default, ordinary FOCEI); "lin" (mfocei/mfoce/magq/mlaplace) profiles mu-referenced population thetas covariate coefficients outer optimizer via closed-form OLS regression subject's back-calculated value covariates (muModelTol/ muModelMaxCycles); "irls" (ifocei/ifoce/iagq/ilaplace) reweights inner-optimization curvature.  Bounded mu parameters regression-updated clamped step (muModelClampRetries); user-fixed (fix()) mu theta never updated. muRefCovAlg `TRUE` (default), algebraic expressions can mu-referenced internally rewritten mu-referenced covariates restored optimization. Mirrors saemControl(muRefCovAlg=)/nlmeControl(muRefCovAlg=); foceiControl() takes effect muModel != "none". muModelTol Convergence tolerance mu-referenced-FOCEI-family "re-optimize etas, regress" cycle (muModel != "none"): repeats max mu-group theta change drops value muModelMaxCycles reached. muModelMaxCycles Maximum number "re-optimize etas, regress" cycles per outer iteration (see muModel, muModelTol). muModelClampRetries Maximum number active-set re-solve passes per group per regression update bounded mu-referenced parameter must clamped bound (see muModel); hitting cap current clamped-feasible solution used. stateTrim Trim state amounts/concentrations value. shi21maxOuter maximum number steps optimization forward-difference step size.  zero, use instead Gill differences. individual Hessian matrices generalized likelihood problem covariance step. 0, un-optimized finite differences used. shi21maxFD maximum number steps optimization forward difference step size using dosing events (lag time, modeled duration/rate bioavailability) shi21hMax Upper bound adaptive shi21 finite-difference step size FOCEi gradients (inner eta outer theta/covariate finite differences).  step-size search never probes parameter estimation scale; larger value lets gradient flat, small-magnitude parameter (e.g. covariate coefficient near 0) clear ODE-solver noise floor, cost risking degenerate solve probe. shi21hMin Lower bound adaptive shi21 finite-difference step size FOCEi gradients.  floor limited ODE solver tolerance (atol/rtol), machine precision; finite difference dominated solver noise. gillK Max steps determine optimal forward/central difference step size per parameter (Gill 1983). `0` = optimal step size determined. gillStep looking optimal forward difference step size, step size increase initial estimate .  iteration new step size = (prior step size)*gillStep gillFtol gillFtol gradient error tolerance acceptable issuing warning/error gradient estimates. gillRtol relative tolerance used Gill 1983 determination optimal step size. gillKcov Max steps determine optimal forward/central difference step size per parameter (Gill 1983) covariance step. `0` = optimal step size determined. gillKcovLlik gillK generalized focei log-likelihood method (Gill 1986). gillStepCovLlik generalized focei log-likelihood gillStepCov looking optimal forward difference step size, step size increase initial estimate .  iteration covariance step equal new step size = (prior step size)*gillStepCov gillFtolCov gillFtol gradient error tolerance acceptable issuing warning/error gradient estimates covariance step. gillFtolCovLlik applied generalized log-likelihood estimation. rmatNorm parameter normalize gradient step size parameter value calculation R matrix rmatNormLlik parameter normalize gradient step size parameter value calculation R matrix using generalized log-likelihood Hessian matrix. smatNorm parameter normalize gradient step size parameter value calculation S matrix smatNormLlik parameter normalize gradient step size parameter value calculation S matrix using generalized log-likelihood. covGillF Use Gill calculated optimal Forward difference step size instead central difference step size central difference gradient calculation. optGillF Use Gill calculated optimal Forward difference step size instead central difference step size central differences optimization. covSmall Small number used compare covariance estimates (sandwich vs R/S matrix) rejecting one small final covariance estimate. adjLik `TRUE`, adjusts likelihood 2*pi constant nlmixr2's objective function otherwise omits (match NONMEM), closely matching nlme/SAS likelihood approximations. objective function always matches NONMEM regardless. gradTrim parameter adjust gradient |gradient| large. maxOdeRecalc Maximum number times reduce ODE tolerances try resolve system bad ODE solve. odeRecalcFactor ODE recalculation factor ODE solving goes bad, factor rtol/atol reduced gradCalcCentralSmall small number represents value |grad| < gradCalcCentralSmall forward differences switch central differences. gradCalcCentralLarge large number represents value |grad| > gradCalcCentralLarge forward differences switch central differences. etaNudge n1qn1 optimization ETA (starting zero) misbehaves, reset Hessian nudge ETA value, still move. Defaults `qnorm(1-0.05/2)*1/sqrt(3)`. Falls back etaNudge2, zero (stop optimizing) unsuccessful. etaNudge2 second eta nudge.  default qnorm(1-0.05/2)*sqrt(3/5), n=3 quadrature point (excluding zero) times 0.95% normal region nRetries FOCEi fit current parameter estimates, randomly sample new parameter estimates restart problem.  similar 'PsN' resampling. seed Integer seed (default `42`) used make FOCEi fit reproducible self-contained.  fit (including `mceta` Monte-Carlo initial-ETA draws, pull rxode2's threefry engine) runs inside [rxode2::rxWithSeed()], neither depends ambient RNG state advances/leaks – repeated fits session, fits following estimation methods, give identical results. resetThetaCheckPer represents objective function % percentage resetThetaP checked. etaMat Initial (final) ETA estimates; can also prior fit, whose final ETAs used initial values. default, uses last fit's ETAs supplied, else ETAs start zero (`NULL`). `NA` disables reuse prior fit. repeatGillMax tolerances reduced calculating initial Gill differences, Gill difference repeated maximum number times defined parameter. stickyRecalcN number bad ODE solves reducing atol/rtol rest problem. indTolRelax `TRUE` (default), subjects whose ODE solve produced NaN/Inf tolerances relaxed, relaxed tolerance persists across optimizer calls (sticky). `FALSE`, subjects tolerances relaxed retry tolerances reset afterward. gradProgressOfvTime time single objective function evaluation (seconds) start progress bars gradient evaluations addProp Type additive-plus-proportional error: `"combined1"`, standard deviations add: $$y = f + (+ b\times f^c) \times \varepsilon$$; `"combined2"`, variances add: $$y = f + \sqrt{^2 + b^2\times f^{2\times c}} \times \varepsilon$$. y = observed, f = predicted, = additive sd, b = proportional/power sd, c = power exponent (1 proportional case). badSolveObjfAdj objective function adjustment ODE system solved.  based individual bad solve. compress object compressed items rxControl `rxode2` ODE solving options fitting, created `rxControl()` sigdigTable Significant digits final output table. specified, matches significant digits `sigdig` optimization algorithm.  `sigdig` NULL, use 3. fallbackFD Fallback finite differences sensitivity equations solve. smatPer Percentage failed per-individual parameter gradients (replaced overall parameter gradient) total (`ntheta*nsub`) S matrix considered bad. sdLowerFact Factor multiplying estimate lower bound zero standard-deviation error parameter (add.sd, prop.sd, etc); e.g. estimate 0.15 lower bound 0 assumes lower bound 0.00015. `0` disables . zeroGradFirstReset `TRUE` (default), reset zero first gradient `sqrt(.Machine$double.eps)` instead erroring; `FALSE` errors; `NA` ignores last reset attempt. zeroGradRunReset `TRUE` (default), reset zero gradient encountered mid-run `sqrt(.Machine$double.eps)` instead erroring. zeroGradBobyqa `TRUE` (default), zero-gradient reset switches gradient-free bobyqa method; `NA` first zero gradient. mceta Monte Carlo sampling best initial ETA estimate (based `omega`): `-2` (default) uses Almquist (2015) Eq-48 extrapolation `eta^0 = eta* + (d eta*/d theta)(theta_new - theta_old)` analytic gradient supplies `d eta*/d theta` (`fast = TRUE`), accepting extrapolated eta within standardized-eta reset bound (else keeping last eta, resetting 0 also bound); `-1` jumps extrapolated eta eta=0, keeping better; `-2` `-1` fall back keeping last eta analytic `d eta*/d theta` available (`fast = FALSE`).  `0` uses eta=0 inner optimization; `n>0`, last eta, eta=0, n-1 etas sampled omega evaluated best (inner objective) used. warm Seeding n1qn1 inner-optimization Hessian: `"calc"` (default) warm-starts inner problem eta Hessian calculated starting eta current theta; since theta moves outer evaluations always recalculated, never reused earlier round.  `"save"` uses classic self-initialized Hessian. nAGQ Number Gauss-Hermite adaptive quadrature points. `0` disables AGQ; `1` equivalent Laplace. Cost grows quickly ETAs: EBE found, expect `nAGQ^neta` (even `nAGQ`) `(nAGQ^neta)-1` (odd `nAGQ`) additional evaluations per subject. agqLow lower bound adaptive quadrature log-likelihood. default -Inf; original nlmixr's gnlmm -700. agqHi upper bound adaptive quadrature log-likelihood.  default Inf; original nlmixr's gnlmm 400. sensMethod Method used compute ODE parameter sensitivities: `"default"` (default) defers global option `getOption("nlmixr2est.adjoint")` (`"forward"` default); `"forward"` uses classic variational (forward) sensitivity ODEs; `"adjoint"` uses -engine discrete adjoint matching adjoint (`s`) method. zeroTheta Positive magnitude (default `0.001`) used nudge population parameter (`theta`) whose initial estimate exactly `0` zero estimation.  FOCEi scales linear parameter native magnitude `|init|`, `0` (scale) zero initial estimate, parameter moved `+zeroTheta` within parameter's bounds, otherwise `-zeroTheta`; neither within bounds error raised.  Fixed parameters (including fixed `0`) left untouched. boundedTransform `TRUE` (default), bounded parameters transformed unbounded optimization methods back-transformed final estimates. `FALSE` optimizes original scale bounds passed optimizer. `NA` transforms optimization skips final back-transform.

title: Load a general FOCE-family likelihood into memory — foceiLikLoad
text: Compiles inner (FOCEi sensitivity) model rxode2 UI model, preprocesses data, sets FOCEi inner problem memory individual log-likelihoods can evaluated repeatedly (parallel per subject) supplied etas without recompiling – setup used internally `est="advi"`, `est="vae"` f-SAEM fast kernel, exposed MCMC/SAMBA-style callers (issue #414).

title: Load a general FOCE-family likelihood into memory — foceiLikLoad
code: foceiLikLoad(   object,   data,   likelihood = c("focei", "focep", "foce"),   rxControl = rxode2::rxControl(),   ... )

title: Load a general FOCE-family likelihood into memory — foceiLikLoad
text: object `rxode2`/`nlmixr2` UI model (model function compiled UI). data estimation data (data frame usual nlmixr2 columns). likelihood individual likelihood type: `"focei"` (FOCE interaction), `"focep"` (FOCE+, interaction residual variance conditional eta) `"foce"` (NONMEM-style FOCE, residual variance frozen eta=0). rxControl [rxode2::rxControl()] object ODE solving options. ... Additional solving/model options passed `.foceiLikControl` (e.g. `optExpression`, `addProp`, `eventSens`).

title: Load a general FOCE-family likelihood into memory — foceiLikLoad
text: Invisibly, handle list loaded system's dimensions:   `initPar` (estimation-scale parameter vector model's initial   estimates, ready `theta` [foceiLikRun()]), `npars`, `ntheta`,   `neta`, `nid`, `thetaNames`, `etaNames`, `idLvl` `likelihood`.

title: Load a general FOCE-family likelihood into memory — foceiLikLoad
text: one likelihood system may loaded time; loading errors one already loaded.  Use [foceiLikRun()] evaluate [foceiLikUnload()] free.

title: Load a general FOCE-family likelihood into memory — foceiLikLoad
text: Matthew L. Fidler

title: Load a general FOCE-family likelihood into memory — foceiLikLoad
code: # \donttest{  one.cmt <- function() {   ini({     tka <- 0.45     tcl <- 1     tv <- 3.45     add.sd <- 0.7     eta.ka ~ 0.6     eta.cl ~ 0.3     eta.v ~ 0.1   })   model({     ka <- exp(tka + eta.ka)     cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)     v <- exp(tv + eta.v)     linCmt() ~ add(add.sd)   }) }  # Set the likelihood up in memory once; only one may be loaded at a time h <- foceiLikLoad(one.cmt, theo_sd, "focei") #>   #>   #> ℹ parameter labels from comments are typically ignored in non-interactive mode #> ℹ Need to run with the source intact to parse comments  # The handle carries the dimensions and a ready starting parameter vector h$nid #> [1] 12 h$neta #> [1] 3 h$initPar #> [1] 0.450000 1.000000 3.450000 0.700000 1.136219 1.351200 1.778279  # Individual joint log-likelihood at eta = 0, one value per subject eta <- matrix(0, h$nid, h$neta) foceiLikRun(h$initPar, eta) #>           1           2           3           4           5           6  #> -24.4017587  -9.3365473  -0.6718843  -5.5542461 -19.0895200 -33.8105271  #>           7           8           9          10          11          12  #> -24.9543558  -8.8147973 -32.7217521 -30.0008367 -18.5848785 -13.1123082   # Free it when done (loading again before this errors) foceiLikUnload() # }

what: Evaluate a loaded general FOCE-family likelihood at supplied etas — foceiLikRun
title: Evaluate a loaded general FOCE-family likelihood at supplied etas — foceiLikRun
text: Writes population parameter vector loaded system returns per-subject log-likelihood supplied etas, computed parallel subjects.  Requires system loaded [foceiLikLoad()].

title: Evaluate a loaded general FOCE-family likelihood at supplied etas — foceiLikRun
code: foceiLikRun(   theta,   eta,   type = c("joint", "cond"),   cores = rxode2::getRxThreads() )

title: Evaluate a loaded general FOCE-family likelihood at supplied etas — foceiLikRun
text: theta estimation-scale parameter vector (length `handle$npars`), matching FOCEi optimizer parameterization: population thetas followed estimated Omega elements.  `handle$initPar` [foceiLikLoad()] ready starting value. eta `nid` `neta` matrix random effects (one row per subject, loaded system's subject order). type `"joint"` (default) returns individual joint log density `log p(y_i, eta_i)`; `"cond"` returns conditional data log-likelihood `log p(y_i | eta_i)` alone.  See Details. cores Number threads parallel per-subject evaluation.

title: Evaluate a loaded general FOCE-family likelihood at supplied etas — foceiLikRun
text: named numeric vector (length `nid`, named subject id)   per-subject log-likelihoods.

title: Evaluate a loaded general FOCE-family likelihood at supplied etas — foceiLikRun
text: types evaluated etas supply, use subject's individual predictions; neither population (eta = 0) quantity.  differ random-effect prior term: - `"cond"` conditional data log-likelihood `log p(y_i | eta_i)`,   observation contribution alone. - `"joint"` `log p(y_i, eta_i) = log p(y_i | eta_i) + log p(eta_i)`,   adds Gaussian random-effect prior   `log p(eta_i) = -0.5 eta_i' Omega^-1 eta_i + 0.5 log|Omega^-1| - neta/2 log(2 pi)`. `"joint"` minus `"cond"` exactly `log p(eta_i)`.  `"joint"` default usual target MCMC/SAMBA-style samplers: function `eta_i` individual's posterior kernel, quantity FOCEi inner problem optimizes etas.  Use `"cond"` supply random-effect density , need observation contribution separately. prior built loaded system's `Omega^-1` log determinant – Omega inner likelihood uses – `"joint"` stays internally consistent engine rather nominal `ini()` values (two differ small amount Omega's internal `rxSymInv` representation). Gaussian endpoints observation contribution follows nlmixr2's internal residual-likelihood convention, `-0.5 err^2/r - 0.5 log(r)`, omits additive `-0.5 log(2 pi)` per observation; general log-likelihood (`ll()`) endpoints contribute user's log density written.  eta prior fully normalized.  types therefore proper log densities fixed per-observation constant depend `theta` `eta`, likelihood ratios, sampler uses , unaffected.

title: Evaluate a loaded general FOCE-family likelihood at supplied etas — foceiLikRun
text: Matthew L. Fidler

title: Evaluate a loaded general FOCE-family likelihood at supplied etas — foceiLikRun
code: # \donttest{  one.cmt <- function() {   ini({     tka <- 0.45     tcl <- 1     tv <- 3.45     add.sd <- 0.7     eta.ka ~ 0.6     eta.cl ~ 0.3     eta.v ~ 0.1   })   model({     ka <- exp(tka + eta.ka)     cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)     v <- exp(tv + eta.v)     linCmt() ~ add(add.sd)   }) }  h <- foceiLikLoad(one.cmt, theo_sd, "focei") #>   #>   #> ℹ parameter labels from comments are typically ignored in non-interactive mode #> ℹ Need to run with the source intact to parse comments  eta <- matrix(0, h$nid, h$neta)  # The individual joint log density log p(y_i, eta_i) (the default) foceiLikRun(h$initPar, eta) #>           1           2           3           4           5           6  #> -24.4017587  -9.3365473  -0.6718843  -5.5542461 -19.0895200 -33.8105271  #>           7           8           9          10          11          12  #> -24.9543558  -8.8147973 -32.7217521 -30.0008367 -18.5848785 -13.1123082   # The conditional data log-likelihood log p(y_i | eta_i) alone; the two # differ by the Gaussian eta prior foceiLikRun(h$initPar, eta, type = "cond") #>            1            2            3            4            5            6  #> -23.65371163  -8.58850024   0.07616276  -4.80619898 -18.34147295 -33.06248005  #>            7            8            9           10           11           12  #> -24.20630875  -8.06675025 -31.97370504 -29.25278965 -17.83683139 -12.36426108   # Non-zero etas set.seed(42) foceiLikRun(h$initPar, matrix(stats::rnorm(h$nid * h$neta, 0, 0.1), h$nid, h$neta)) #>           1           2           3           4           5           6  #> -36.9555993 -11.2007086  -0.5086688 -22.2009369 -21.6157454 -67.7256691  #>           7           8           9          10          11          12  #> -34.2146289  -2.3798982 -29.7461021 -21.1068628 -14.4055705 -32.8805294   # A new population parameter vector needs no reload theta <- h$initPar theta[1] <- theta[1] + 0.1 foceiLikRun(theta, eta) #>           1           2           3           4           5           6  #> -24.4017439  -9.3365336  -0.6718685  -5.5542670 -19.0895071 -33.8105676  #>           7           8           9          10          11          12  #> -24.9544094  -8.8148170 -32.7217044 -30.0008741 -18.5848526 -13.1123297   foceiLikUnload() # }

what: Unload the general FOCE-family likelihood from memory — foceiLikUnload
title: Unload the general FOCE-family likelihood from memory — foceiLikUnload
text: Frees FOCEi inner problem set [foceiLikLoad()].  -op (returns `FALSE`) nothing loaded.

title: Unload the general FOCE-family likelihood from memory — foceiLikUnload
code: foceiLikUnload()

title: Unload the general FOCE-family likelihood from memory — foceiLikUnload
text: Invisibly `TRUE` system freed, `FALSE` none loaded.

title: Unload the general FOCE-family likelihood from memory — foceiLikUnload
text: Matthew L. Fidler

what: Control options for the focep (FOCE+) estimation method — focepControl
title: Control options for the focep (FOCE+) estimation method — focepControl
text: first order conditional estimation without eta/residual interaction, keeping live conditional residual variance R (`foce = "foce+"` option [foceiControl()]).  `foce` method `foce = "foce+"` forced; use `foce` (est = "foce") NONMEM-matching frozen-R behavior.

title: Control options for the focep (FOCE+) estimation method — focepControl
code: focepControl(sigdig = 3, ..., interaction = FALSE, foce = "foce+")

title: Control options for the focep (FOCE+) estimation method — focepControl
text: sigdig Optimization significant digits; controls inner/outer optimization tolerance (10^-sigdig), boundary check tolerance (5*10^(-sigdig+1)), ODE solver tolerances.  solver tolerances split solver stiffness keep atol well rtol: stiff solver (lsoda/liblsoda – auto-switching method – plus indLin, default) uses rtol = 10^(-sigdig-3), atol = 10^(-sigdig-5), non-stiff explicit dop853 uses looser rtol = 10^-sigdig, atol = 10^(-sigdig-3).  sensitivity (atolSens/rtolSens) steady-state (ssAtol/ssRtol) tolerances run one order looser corresponding main tolerance.  default sigdig = 4 stiff solve atol = 1e-9, rtol = 1e-7. ... Parameters used default `foceiControl()` interaction Interaction term model, case default `FALSE`; changed, use `focei` instead foce FOCE residual-variance mode; `focepControl()` always `"foce+"` changed – use `foceControl()` `"nonmem"`

title: Control options for the focep (FOCE+) estimation method — focepControl

title: Control options foir the FOI estimation method — foiControl
text: sigdig Optimization significant digits; controls inner/outer optimization tolerance (10^-sigdig), boundary check tolerance (5*10^(-sigdig+1)), ODE solver tolerances.  solver tolerances split solver stiffness keep atol well rtol: stiff solver (lsoda/liblsoda – auto-switching method – plus indLin, default) uses rtol = 10^(-sigdig-3), atol = 10^(-sigdig-5), non-stiff explicit dop853 uses looser rtol = 10^-sigdig, atol = 10^(-sigdig-3).  sensitivity (atolSens/rtolSens) steady-state (ssAtol/ssRtol) tolerances run one order looser corresponding main tolerance.  default sigdig = 4 stiff solve atol = 1e-9, rtol = 1e-7. ... Parameters used default `foceiConrol()` posthoc Logical indicating estimation method calculate `foce` posthoc predicted parameters. interaction Interaction term model; ignored fo fo Logical indicating estimation method FO (first order), controlled estimation method ignored.
