what: Control options for the iagq estimation method — iagqControl
title: Control options for the iagq estimation method — iagqControl
text: Mu-referenced-FOCEI-family reweighted-regression (`"irls"`) variant adaptive Gauss-Hermite quadrature; see `foceiControl(muModel=)`.

title: Control options for the iagq estimation method — iagqControl
code: iagqControl(   sigdig = 3,   nAGQ = 2,   ...,   interaction = TRUE,   agqLow = -Inf,   agqHi = Inf,   muModel = c("irls", "lin", "none") )

title: Control options for the iagq estimation method — iagqControl
text: sigdig Optimization significant digits; controls inner/outer optimization tolerance (10^-sigdig), boundary check tolerance (5*10^(-sigdig+1)), ODE solver tolerances.  solver tolerances split solver stiffness keep atol well rtol: stiff solver (lsoda/liblsoda – auto-switching method – plus indLin, default) uses rtol = 10^(-sigdig-3), atol = 10^(-sigdig-5), non-stiff explicit dop853 uses looser rtol = 10^-sigdig, atol = 10^(-sigdig-3).  sensitivity (atolSens/rtolSens) steady-state (ssAtol/ssRtol) tolerances run one order looser corresponding main tolerance.  default sigdig = 4 stiff solve atol = 1e-9, rtol = 1e-7. nAGQ Number Gauss-Hermite adaptive quadrature points. `0` disables AGQ; `1` equivalent Laplace. Cost grows quickly ETAs: EBE found, expect `nAGQ^neta` (even `nAGQ`) `(nAGQ^neta)-1` (odd `nAGQ`) additional evaluations per subject. ... Parameters used default `foceiControl()` interaction boolean, Interaction term model, case default `TRUE`; adaptive quadrature, normal distribution Hessian calculated foce() approximation agqLow lower bound adaptive quadrature log-likelihood. default -Inf; original nlmixr's gnlmm -700. agqHi upper bound adaptive quadrature log-likelihood.  default Inf; original nlmixr's gnlmm 400. muModel Selects regression variant; `iagqControl()` always `"irls"` changed – use `magqControl()` closed-form OLS variant.

what: Control options for the ifoce estimation method — ifoceControl
title: Control options for the ifoce estimation method — ifoceControl
text: Mu-referenced-FOCEI-family reweighted-regression (`"irls"`) variant FOCE (interaction); see `foceiControl(muModel=)`.

title: Control options for the ifoce estimation method — ifoceControl
code: ifoceControl(   sigdig = 3,   ...,   interaction = FALSE,   muModel = c("irls", "lin", "none") )

title: Control options for the ifoce estimation method — ifoceControl
text: sigdig Optimization significant digits; controls inner/outer optimization tolerance (10^-sigdig), boundary check tolerance (5*10^(-sigdig+1)), ODE solver tolerances.  solver tolerances split solver stiffness keep atol well rtol: stiff solver (lsoda/liblsoda – auto-switching method – plus indLin, default) uses rtol = 10^(-sigdig-3), atol = 10^(-sigdig-5), non-stiff explicit dop853 uses looser rtol = 10^-sigdig, atol = 10^(-sigdig-3).  sensitivity (atolSens/rtolSens) steady-state (ssAtol/ssRtol) tolerances run one order looser corresponding main tolerance.  default sigdig = 4 stiff solve atol = 1e-9, rtol = 1e-7. ... Parameters used default `foceiControl()` interaction Interaction term model, case default `FALSE`; changed, use `ifocei` instead muModel Selects regression variant; `ifoceControl()` always `"irls"` changed – use `mfoceControl()` closed-form OLS variant.

title: Control options for the ifoce estimation method — ifoceControl
text: ifoceControl object

title: Control options for the ifoce estimation method — ifoceControl
text: `mfocei`/`ifocei` (related) methods apply mu2+ covariate hooks, expand algebraic mu-referenced covariate expressions (e.g. `cl.wt*log(WT/70)`) estimable mu-referenced parameters split covariates non-time-varying (absorbed phi term) time-varying (kept `beta` regressors).  Calling `focei` directly apply hooks, methods can estimate mu-referenced models plain `focei` – genuine difference calling e.g. `est="mfocei"` `est="focei"`. mu-referenced population thetas – without covariates – profiled outer optimizer -C++ regression (intercept-covariate-free pairs), outer gradients calculated non-mu-referenced parameters (residual errors, omegas, non-mu thetas).  Bounded mu-referenced parameters regression-updated update clamped bounds (clamp reported fit note); user-fixed (`fix()`) mu thetas stay regression.

text: Matthew L. Fidler

what: Control options for the ifocei estimation method — ifoceiControl
title: Control options for the ifocei estimation method — ifoceiControl
text: Mu-referenced-FOCEI-family reweighted-regression (`"irls"`) variant FOCEI; see `foceiControl(muModel=)`.

title: Control options for the ifocei estimation method — ifoceiControl
code: ifoceiControl(sigdig = 3, ..., muModel = c("irls", "lin", "none"))

title: Control options for the ifocei estimation method — ifoceiControl
text: sigdig Optimization significant digits; controls inner/outer optimization tolerance (10^-sigdig), boundary check tolerance (5*10^(-sigdig+1)), ODE solver tolerances.  solver tolerances split solver stiffness keep atol well rtol: stiff solver (lsoda/liblsoda – auto-switching method – plus indLin, default) uses rtol = 10^(-sigdig-3), atol = 10^(-sigdig-5), non-stiff explicit dop853 uses looser rtol = 10^-sigdig, atol = 10^(-sigdig-3).  sensitivity (atolSens/rtolSens) steady-state (ssAtol/ssRtol) tolerances run one order looser corresponding main tolerance.  default sigdig = 4 stiff solve atol = 1e-9, rtol = 1e-7. ... Parameters used default `foceiControl()` muModel Selects regression variant; `ifoceiControl()` always `"irls"` changed – use `mfoceiControl()` closed-form OLS variant.

title: Control options for the ifocei estimation method — ifoceiControl
text: ifoceiControl object

title: Control options for the ifocei estimation method — ifoceiControl
text: `mfocei`/`ifocei` (related) methods apply mu2+ covariate hooks, expand algebraic mu-referenced covariate expressions (e.g. `cl.wt*log(WT/70)`) estimable mu-referenced parameters split covariates non-time-varying (absorbed phi term) time-varying (kept `beta` regressors).  Calling `focei` directly apply hooks, methods can estimate mu-referenced models plain `focei` – genuine difference calling e.g. `est="mfocei"` `est="focei"`. mu-referenced population thetas – without covariates – profiled outer optimizer -C++ regression (intercept-covariate-free pairs), outer gradients calculated non-mu-referenced parameters (residual errors, omegas, non-mu thetas).  Bounded mu-referenced parameters regression-updated update clamped bounds (clamp reported fit note); user-fixed (`fix()`) mu thetas stay regression.

title: Control options for the ifocei estimation method — ifoceiControl
text: Matthew L. Fidler

what: Control options for the ifocep (IRLS FOCE+) estimation method — ifocepControl
title: Control options for the ifocep (IRLS FOCE+) estimation method — ifocepControl
text: Mu-referenced-FOCEI-family reweighted-regression (`"irls"`) variant FOCE+ (interaction, live conditional residual variance R); `ifoce` `foce = "foce+"` forced (see `foceiControl(foce=)`).

title: Control options for the ifocep (IRLS FOCE+) estimation method — ifocepControl
code: ifocepControl(   sigdig = 3,   ...,   interaction = FALSE,   muModel = c("irls", "lin", "none"),   foce = "foce+" )

title: Control options for the ifocep (IRLS FOCE+) estimation method — ifocepControl
text: sigdig Optimization significant digits; controls inner/outer optimization tolerance (10^-sigdig), boundary check tolerance (5*10^(-sigdig+1)), ODE solver tolerances.  solver tolerances split solver stiffness keep atol well rtol: stiff solver (lsoda/liblsoda – auto-switching method – plus indLin, default) uses rtol = 10^(-sigdig-3), atol = 10^(-sigdig-5), non-stiff explicit dop853 uses looser rtol = 10^-sigdig, atol = 10^(-sigdig-3).  sensitivity (atolSens/rtolSens) steady-state (ssAtol/ssRtol) tolerances run one order looser corresponding main tolerance.  default sigdig = 4 stiff solve atol = 1e-9, rtol = 1e-7. ... Parameters used default `foceiControl()` interaction Interaction term model, case default `FALSE`; changed, use `ifocei` instead muModel Selects regression variant; `ifocepControl()` always `"irls"` changed – use `mfocepControl()` closed-form OLS variant. foce FOCE residual-variance mode; `ifocepControl()` always `"foce+"` changed – use `ifoceControl()` `"nonmem"`

title: Control options for the ifocep (IRLS FOCE+) estimation method — ifocepControl
text: ifocepControl object

title: Control options for the ifocep (IRLS FOCE+) estimation method — ifocepControl
text: `mfocei`/`ifocei` (related) methods apply mu2+ covariate hooks, expand algebraic mu-referenced covariate expressions (e.g. `cl.wt*log(WT/70)`) estimable mu-referenced parameters split covariates non-time-varying (absorbed phi term) time-varying (kept `beta` regressors).  Calling `focei` directly apply hooks, methods can estimate mu-referenced models plain `focei` – genuine difference calling e.g. `est="mfocei"` `est="focei"`. mu-referenced population thetas – without covariates – profiled outer optimizer -C++ regression (intercept-covariate-free pairs), outer gradients calculated non-mu-referenced parameters (residual errors, omegas, non-mu thetas).  Bounded mu-referenced parameters regression-updated update clamped bounds (clamp reported fit note); user-fixed (`fix()`) mu thetas stay regression.

title: Control options for the ifocep (IRLS FOCE+) estimation method — ifocepControl
text: Matthew L. Fidler

what: Control options for the ilaplace estimation method — ilaplaceControl
title: Control options for the ilaplace estimation method — ilaplaceControl
text: Mu-referenced-FOCEI-family reweighted-regression (`"irls"`) variant Laplace method (`nAGQ=1`); see `foceiControl(muModel=)`.

title: Control options for the ilaplace estimation method — ilaplaceControl
code: ilaplaceControl(sigdig = 3, ..., nAGQ = 1, muModel = c("irls", "lin", "none"))

title: Control options for the ilaplace estimation method — ilaplaceControl
text: sigdig Optimization significant digits; controls inner/outer optimization tolerance (10^-sigdig), boundary check tolerance (5*10^(-sigdig+1)), ODE solver tolerances.  solver tolerances split solver stiffness keep atol well rtol: stiff solver (lsoda/liblsoda – auto-switching method – plus indLin, default) uses rtol = 10^(-sigdig-3), atol = 10^(-sigdig-5), non-stiff explicit dop853 uses looser rtol = 10^-sigdig, atol = 10^(-sigdig-3).  sensitivity (atolSens/rtolSens) steady-state (ssAtol/ssRtol) tolerances run one order looser corresponding main tolerance.  default sigdig = 4 stiff solve atol = 1e-9, rtol = 1e-7. ... Parameters used default `foceiControl()` nAGQ Number Gauss-Hermite adaptive quadrature points. `0` disables AGQ; `1` equivalent Laplace. Cost grows quickly ETAs: EBE found, expect `nAGQ^neta` (even `nAGQ`) `(nAGQ^neta)-1` (odd `nAGQ`) additional evaluations per subject. muModel Selects regression variant; `ilaplaceControl()` always `"irls"` changed – use `mlaplaceControl()` closed-form OLS variant.

what: Control options for the impmap (importance-sampling EM) estimation method — impmapControl
title: Control options for the impmap (importance-sampling EM) estimation method — impmapControl
text: NONMEM-style Monte Carlo importance-sampling EM built mu-referenced FOCEI MAP.  proposal density subject centered MAP mode (`muModel="lin"`); mu-referenced population parameters updated EM gradient, non-mu parameters (structural residual error) updated symbolic-sensitivity Newton step – importance-sampling-weighted score Gauss-Newton information built analytic `d(f)/d(theta)` `d(V)/d(theta)` (exact censored partials BLQ/M2/M3/M4 points).

title: Control options for the impmap (importance-sampling EM) estimation method — impmapControl
code: impmapControl(   sigdig = 3,   ...,   isample = 300L,   nIter = 100L,   mapIter = 1L,   gamma = 1,   iscaleMin = 0.1,   iscaleMax = 10,   iaccept = 0.4,   ctol = NULL,   nConvWindow = 10L,   impSeed = 42L,   covMethod = c("imp", "analytic", "r,s", "r", "s", "sa", ""),   qr = FALSE,   qrShift = TRUE,   qrRefresh = TRUE,   sir = FALSE,   sirSample = NULL,   muModel = c("lin", "none") )

title: Control options for the impmap (importance-sampling EM) estimation method — impmapControl
text: sigdig Optimization significant digits; controls inner/outer optimization tolerance (10^-sigdig), boundary check tolerance (5*10^(-sigdig+1)), ODE solver tolerances.  solver tolerances split solver stiffness keep atol well rtol: stiff solver (lsoda/liblsoda – auto-switching method – plus indLin, default) uses rtol = 10^(-sigdig-3), atol = 10^(-sigdig-5), non-stiff explicit dop853 uses looser rtol = 10^-sigdig, atol = 10^(-sigdig-3).  sensitivity (atolSens/rtolSens) steady-state (ssAtol/ssRtol) tolerances run one order looser corresponding main tolerance.  default sigdig = 4 stiff solve atol = 1e-9, rtol = 1e-7. ... Parameters used default `foceiControl()` isample Number importance samples drawn per subject per iteration (NONMEM ISAMPLE). nIter Maximum number importance-sampling EM iterations. mapIter Number MAP re-centering iterations per EM step; `> 0` re-centers proposal MAP mode iteration. gamma Initial proposal-variance inflation factor (NONMEM ISCALE); proposal covariance `gamma` times inverse inner information matrix mode. iscaleMin, iscaleMax Lower/upper bounds adapted `gamma` (NONMEM ISCALE_MIN / ISCALE_MAX). iaccept Minimum importance-sampling effective-sample fraction (NONMEM IACCEPT).  proposal scale `gamma` kept efficient starting value achieved fraction stays `iaccept`, inflated (toward `iscaleMax`) drops floor. ctol Convergence tolerance windowed objective-function change; `NULL` derives `sigdig`. nConvWindow Length trailing iteration window used average objective-function change convergence (NONMEM-style CTYPE). impSeed Base seed per-subject thread-safe (threefry) RNG streams; results reproducible independent thread count. covMethod Covariance method.  `"imp"` (default) computes Monte-Carlo importance-sampling observed-information covariance estimated thetas Omega parameters (finite-difference Hessian importance-sampling objective fixed common-random-number samples), stashed `$impCov` / `$impSe` installed fit covariance; theta standard errors match Hessian-based FOCEI covariance, though variance tightly-determined random effect (Omega diagonal) can -estimated fixed samples barely span prior variation. `"analytic"`, `"r,s"`, `"r"`, `"s"` instead compute FOCEI covariance post-fit converged estimates (see [foceiControl()]); `""` skips covariance step. qr `TRUE`, draw quasi-random (Sobol low-discrepancy) importance samples instead pseudo-random Gaussian samples (QRPEM, Leary & Dunlavey PAGE 2012); E-step integrals converge O(1/N) instead O(1/sqrt(N)). qrShift used `qr=TRUE`.  `TRUE` (iteration, subject) applies random Cranley-Patterson shift Sobol points (seeded, thread-count independent); `FALSE` reuses one fixed Sobol point set everywhere (fully deterministic E-step, RNG draw). qrRefresh used `qr=TRUE` `qrShift=TRUE`.  `TRUE` shift redrawn iteration residual quasi-random error averages EM; `FALSE` draws one shift per subject fit start, making EM iteration deterministic map (smoothest objective trace). sir `TRUE`, accelerate non-mu / residual-error M-step SIR (sampling-importance-resampling): theta-sensitivity Newton step uses `sirSample` equal-weight resampled points per subject instead `isample` weighted samples. sirSample Number SIR resampled points per subject; `NULL` uses `max(25, ceiling(isample/10))`.  Must `isample`. muModel Mu-referencing variant MAP inner problem; `impmapControl()` always `"lin"` changed.

title: Control iteration-time print formatting — iterPrintControl
text: Bundles options controlling iteration progress output emitted `nlmixr2` estimators. Pass `print` argument `*Control()` function; scalar form (`print = N`) still works wrapped `iterPrintControl()` internally.

title: Control iteration-time print formatting — iterPrintControl
code: iterPrintControl(   every = 1L,   ncol = NULL,   headerEvery = NULL,   useColor = NULL,   simple = FALSE )

title: Control iteration-time print formatting — iterPrintControl
text: every Integer. Print one iteration row every `every` parameter evaluations; `0` suppresses output. Defaults `1L`. ncol Integer `NULL`. Parameter columns per row wrapping. `NULL` (default) uses `floor((getOption("width") - 23) / 12)`. headerEvery Integer `NULL`. Re-emit column header every `headerEvery` parameter-print events; `0` prints fit start. `NULL` (default) uses `10L`. useColor Logical (`NULL`) emit ANSI bold/color escapes iteration print. `NULL` (default) defers [crayon::has_color()]. simple Logical. `TRUE`, print single row per iteration, suppressing unscaled (`U`) / back-transformed (`X`) rows. Defaults `FALSE`.

title: Control iteration-time print formatting — iterPrintControl
text: list validated, defaulted iteration-print   options.  class `"iterPrintControl"` outer `*Control()`   functions can distinguish pre-built object scalar   `print = N`.

title: Control iteration-time print formatting — iterPrintControl
text: Bill Denney, Matthew L. Fidler

text: Shared `@param` docs iteration-print formatting, used scalar `*Control()` arguments [iterPrintControl()] via `@inheritParams`.

title: Iteration-print configuration parameters (documentation stub) — iterPrintParams
text: print Either scalar print-frequency (`0` = suppress, `1` (default) = every evaluation, `N` = every Nth), pre-built [iterPrintControl()] object. Equivalent `iterPrintControl(every = print, ncol = printNcol, useColor = useColor)`. printNcol Integer (`NULL`) parameter columns per row wrapping. `NULL` (default) uses `floor((getOption("width") - 23) / 12)`. every Integer. Print one iteration row every `every` parameter evaluations; `0` suppresses output. Defaults `1L`. ncol Integer `NULL`. Parameter columns per row wrapping. `NULL` (default) uses `floor((getOption("width") - 23) / 12)`. headerEvery Integer `NULL`. Re-emit column header every `headerEvery` parameter-print events; `0` prints fit start. `NULL` (default) uses `10L`. useColor Logical (`NULL`) emit ANSI bold/color escapes iteration print. `NULL` (default) defers [crayon::has_color()]. simple Logical. `TRUE`, print single row per iteration, suppressing unscaled (`U`) / back-transformed (`X`) rows. Defaults `FALSE`.

title: Iteration-print configuration parameters (documentation stub) — iterPrintParams

what: Control options for the Laplace estimation method — laplaceControl
title: Control options for the Laplace estimation method — laplaceControl
text: control options adaptive Gauss-Hermite quadrature likelihood.  Note nAGQ=1 Laplace method.

title: Control options for the Laplace estimation method — laplaceControl
code: laplaceControl(sigdig = 3, ..., nAGQ = 1)

title: Control options for the Laplace estimation method — laplaceControl
text: sigdig Optimization significant digits; controls inner/outer optimization tolerance (10^-sigdig), boundary check tolerance (5*10^(-sigdig+1)), ODE solver tolerances.  solver tolerances split solver stiffness keep atol well rtol: stiff solver (lsoda/liblsoda – auto-switching method – plus indLin, default) uses rtol = 10^(-sigdig-3), atol = 10^(-sigdig-5), non-stiff explicit dop853 uses looser rtol = 10^-sigdig, atol = 10^(-sigdig-3).  sensitivity (atolSens/rtolSens) steady-state (ssAtol/ssRtol) tolerances run one order looser corresponding main tolerance.  default sigdig = 4 stiff solve atol = 1e-9, rtol = 1e-7. ... Parameters used default `foceiControl()` nAGQ Number Gauss-Hermite adaptive quadrature points. `0` disables AGQ; `1` equivalent Laplace. Cost grows quickly ETAs: EBE found, expect `nAGQ^neta` (even `nAGQ`) `(nAGQ^neta)-1` (odd `nAGQ`) additional evaluations per subject.

p$time <- 0 # dummy time  f <- function() {   ini({     t1 <- 1     t2 <- 1     t3 <- 1     t4 <- 1     eta1 ~ 1   })   model({     if (group == 1) {        lp <- t1 + t2 * logtstd     } else {        lp <- t3 + t4 * logtstd     }     lp <- lp + eta1     lam <- exp(lp)     y ~ dpois(lam)   }) }  fit <- nlmixr(f, p, est="laplace") #>   #>   #>   #>   #> ℹ parameter labels from comments are typically title: Control for lbfgsb3c estimation method in nlmixr2 — lbfgsb3cControl
text: trace positive, print tracing information; higher values give detail (see source "L-BFGS-B" trace levels). factr Convergence tolerance factor "L-BFGS-B"; converges objective reduction within factor machine tolerance (default 1e7, .e. ~1e-8). pgtol Tolerance projected gradient "L-BFGS-B"; 0 (default) suppresses check. abstol Absolute x-value tolerance "L-BFGS-B"; 0 (default) suppresses check. reltol Relative x-value tolerance "L-BFGS-B"; 0 (default) suppresses check. lmm Number BFGS updates retained "L-BFGS-B" (default 5). maxit maximum number iterations. returnLbfgsb3c return lbfgsb3c output instead nlmixr2 fit stickyRecalcN number bad ODE solves reducing atol/rtol rest problem. maxOdeRecalc Maximum number times reduce ODE tolerances try resolve system bad ODE solve. odeRecalcFactor ODE recalculation factor ODE solving goes bad, factor rtol/atol reduced indTolRelax `TRUE` (default), subjects whose ODE solve produced NaN/Inf tolerances relaxed, relaxed tolerance persists across optimizer calls (sticky). `FALSE`, subjects tolerances relaxed retry tolerances reset afterward. useColor Logical (`NULL`) emit ANSI bold/color escapes iteration print. `NULL` (default) defers [crayon::has_color()]. printNcol Integer (`NULL`) parameter columns per row wrapping. `NULL` (default) uses `floor((getOption("width") - 23) / 12)`. print Either scalar print-frequency (`0` = suppress, `1` (default) = every evaluation, `N` = every Nth), pre-built [iterPrintControl()] object. Equivalent `iterPrintControl(every = print, ncol = printNcol, useColor = useColor)`. normType Parameter normalization/scaling used get scaled initial values scaleType, form Vscaled = (Vunscaled-C1)/C2 (see Feature Scaling; rescale2 follows OptdesX manual): "rescale2" scales parameters (-1, 1); "rescale" (min-max) scales (0, 1); "mean" centers mean range (0, 1); "std" standardizes mean/sd; "len" scales unit (Euclidean) length; "constant" performs normalization (C1=0, C2=1). scaleType scaling scheme nlmixr2: "nlmixr2" (default) scales (current-init)*scaleC[] + scaleTo, scaleTo normType scales scaleC; "norm" uses simple scaling normType; "mult" scales multiplicatively current/init*scaleTo; "multAdd" scales linearly ((current-init)+scaleTo) parameters exponential block (e.g. exp(theta)) multiplicatively otherwise. scaleCmax Maximum value scaleC prevent overflow. scaleCmin Minimum value scaleC prevent underflow. scaleC Scaling constant used scaleType="nlmixr2"; specified, chosen parameter type keep gradient sizes similar log scale: `1` exp()-transformed/power/boxCox/ yeoJohnson parameters, `0.5*abs(est)` additive/proportional/ lognormal error parameters, `abs(1/digamma(est+1))` factorials, `log(abs(est))*abs(est)` log-scale parameters. May set explicitly per parameter defaults apply well. scaleTo Scale initial parameter estimate value. default 1.  zero , scaling performed. gradTo factor gradient scaled optimizing.  works scaleType="nlmixr2". rxControl `rxode2` ODE solving options fitting, created `rxControl()` optExpression Optimize rxode2 expression speed calculation. default turned . sumProd boolean indicating model change multiplication high precision multiplication sums high precision sums using PreciseSums package.  default FALSE. literalFix boolean, substitute fixed population values literals re-adjust ui parameter estimates optimization; Default `TRUE`. literalFixRes boolean, substitute fixed population values literals re-adjust ui parameter estimates optimization; Default `TRUE`. addProp Type additive-plus-proportional error: `"combined1"`, standard deviations add: $$y = f + (+ b\times f^c) \times \varepsilon$$; `"combined2"`, variances add: $$y = f + \sqrt{^2 + b^2\times f^{2\times c}} \times \varepsilon$$. y = observed, f = predicted, = additive sd, b = proportional/power sd, c = power exponent (1 proportional case). eventSens Controls dosing/event-parameter (`alag`, `F`, `rate`, `dur`) sensitivities computed THETA/ETA gradients: `"jump"` (default) uses rxode2's analytic event sensitivities; `"fd"` uses legacy finite-difference behavior. sensMethod Method used compute ODE parameter sensitivities: `"default"` (default) defers global option `getOption("nlmixr2est.adjoint")` (`"forward"` default); `"forward"` uses classic variational (forward) sensitivity ODEs; `"adjoint"` uses -engine discrete adjoint matching adjoint (`s`) method. calcTables boolean determine foceiFit calculate tables. default TRUE compress object compressed items covMethod Method calculating covariance.  "r" (default) uses nlmixr2's nlmixr2Hess() Hessian; "" skips covariance step. adjObf boolean indicate objective function adjusted closer NONMEM's default objective function.  default TRUE ci Confidence level tables.  default 0.95 95% confidence. sigdig Optimization significant digits; controls inner/outer optimization tolerance (10^-sigdig), boundary check tolerance (5*10^(-sigdig+1)), ODE solver tolerances.  solver tolerances split solver stiffness keep atol well rtol: stiff solver (lsoda/liblsoda – auto-switching method – plus indLin, default) uses rtol = 10^(-sigdig-3), atol = 10^(-sigdig-5), non-stiff explicit dop853 uses looser rtol = 10^-sigdig, atol = 10^(-sigdig-3).  sensitivity (atolSens/rtolSens) steady-state (ssAtol/ssRtol) tolerances run one order looser corresponding main tolerance.  default sigdig = 4 stiff solve atol = 1e-9, rtol = 1e-7. sigdigTable Significant digits final output table. specified, matches significant digits `sigdig` optimization algorithm.  `sigdig` NULL, use 3. ... Ignored parameters

title: Control for lbfgsb3c estimation method in nlmixr2 — lbfgsb3cControl
code: # \donttest{ # A logit regression example with emax model  dsn <- data.frame(i=1:1000) dsn$time <- exp(rnorm(1000)) dsn$DV=rbinom(1000,1,exp(-1+dsn$time)/(1+exp(-1+dsn$time)))  mod <- function() {  ini({    E0 <- 0.5    Em <- 0.5    E50 <- 2    g <- fix(2)  })  model({    v <- E0+Em*time^g/(E50^g+time^g)    ll(bin) ~ DV * v - log(1 + exp(v))  }) }  fit2 <- nlmixr(mod, dsn, est="lbfgsb3c") #>   #>   #>   #>   #> ℹ parameter labels from comments are typically ignored in non-interactive mode #> ℹ Need to run with the source intact to parse comments #> → loading into symengine environment... #> → pruning branches (`if`/`else`) of population log-likelihood model... #> ✔ done #> → calculate ∂(f)/∂(θ) #> → finding duplicate expressions in nlm llik gradient... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in nlm llik gradient... #> → finding duplicate expressions in nlm pred-only... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in nlm pred-only... #>   #>   #>   #>   #> → calculating covariance #> ✔ done #> → loading into symengine environment... #> → pruning branches (`if`/`else`) of full model... #> ✔ done #> → finding duplicate expressions in EBE model... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in EBE model... #> → compiling EBE model... #>   #>   #> ✔ done #> → Calculating residuals/tables #> ✔ done  print(fit2) #> ── nlmixr² log-likelihood lbfgsb3c ── #>  #>           OBJF      AIC      BIC Log-likelihood Condition#(Cov) Condition#(Cor) #> lPop -686.1115 1157.766 1172.489      -575.8828        11443.35        389.9271 #>  #> ── Time (sec $time): ── #>  #>            setup optimize covariance preprocess postprocess table compress #> elapsed 0.384275  1.51495   6.68e-06      0.044       0.007 0.031    0.001 #>             other #> elapsed 0.1107686 #>  #> ── ($parFixed or $parFixedDf): ── #>  #>        Est.     SE  %RSE   Back-transformed(95%CI) #> E0  -0.7181 0.2229 31.03 -0.7181 (-1.155, -0.2813) #> Em    10.73  14.11 131.5     10.73 (-16.93, 38.38) #> E50   4.995  4.447 89.02     4.995 (-3.721, 13.71) #> g     2.000  FIXED FIXED                     2.000 #>   #>   Covariance Type ($covMethod): r #>   Censoring ($censInformation): No censoring #>   Minimization message ($message):   #>     CONVERGENCE: REL_REDUCTION_OF_F_<=_FACTR*EPSMCH  #>  #> ── Fit Data (object is a modified tibble): ── #> # A tibble: 1,000 × 5 #>   ID      TIME    DV  IPRED      v #>   <fct>  <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1 1     0.0343     0 -0.397 -0.718 #> 2 1     0.0489     1 -1.11  -0.717 #> 3 1     0.0501     0 -0.398 -0.717 #> # ℹ 997 more rows  # you can also get the nlm output with fit2$lbfgsb3c  fit2$lbfgsb3c #> $par #>         E0         Em        E50  #> -0.7181425 10.7263638  4.9954149  #>  #> $grad #> [1]  0.0001900423 -0.0003470364  0.0005667704 #>  #> $value #> [1] 575.8828 #>  #> $counts #> [1] 17 17 #>  #> $convergence #> [1] 0 #>  #> $message #> [1] "CONVERGENCE: REL_REDUCTION_OF_F_<=_FACTR*EPSMCH" #>  #> $scaleC #> [1] 0.002595759 0.031364338 0.028740636 #>  #> $par.scaled #>        E0        Em       E50  #> -470.2817  325.0507  105.2223  #>  #> $hessian #>                E0            Em          E50 #> E0   0.0013515893  0.0008768856 -0.002965837 #> Em   0.0008768856  0.0014889114 -0.004570147 #> E50 -0.0029658371 -0.0045701474  0.014285006 #>  #> $cov.scaled #>            E0        Em      E50 #> E0   7371.897  19793.83  7863.11 #> Em  19793.833 202392.10 68860.10 #> E50  7863.110  68860.10 23942.69 #>  #> $r #>                E0            Em          E50 #> E0   0.0006757946  0.0004384428 -0.001482919 #> Em   0.0004384428  0.0007444557 -0.002285074 #> E50 -0.0014829185 -0.0022850737  0.007142503 #>   # The nlm control has been modified slightly to include # extra components and name the parameters # }

title: Control options for the magq estimation method — magqControl
text: sigdig Optimization significant digits; controls inner/outer optimization tolerance (10^-sigdig), boundary check tolerance (5*10^(-sigdig+1)), ODE solver tolerances.  solver tolerances split solver stiffness keep atol well rtol: stiff solver (lsoda/liblsoda – auto-switching method – plus indLin, default) uses rtol = 10^(-sigdig-3), atol = 10^(-sigdig-5), non-stiff explicit dop853 uses looser rtol = 10^-sigdig, atol = 10^(-sigdig-3).  sensitivity (atolSens/rtolSens) steady-state (ssAtol/ssRtol) tolerances run one order looser corresponding main tolerance.  default sigdig = 4 stiff solve atol = 1e-9, rtol = 1e-7. nAGQ Number Gauss-Hermite adaptive quadrature points. `0` disables AGQ; `1` equivalent Laplace. Cost grows quickly ETAs: EBE found, expect `nAGQ^neta` (even `nAGQ`) `(nAGQ^neta)-1` (odd `nAGQ`) additional evaluations per subject. ... Parameters used default `foceiControl()` interaction boolean, Interaction term model, case default `TRUE`; adaptive quadrature, normal distribution Hessian calculated foce() approximation agqLow lower bound adaptive quadrature log-likelihood. default -Inf; original nlmixr's gnlmm -700. agqHi upper bound adaptive quadrature log-likelihood.  default Inf; original nlmixr's gnlmm 400. muModel Selects regression variant; `magqControl()` always `"lin"` changed – use `iagqControl()` IRLS variant.

what: Control options for the mfoce estimation method — mfoceControl
title: Control options for the mfoce estimation method — mfoceControl
text: Mu-referenced-FOCEI-family closed-form-regression (`"lin"`) variant FOCE (interaction); see `foceiControl(muModel=)`.

title: Control options for the mfoce estimation method — mfoceControl
code: mfoceControl(   sigdig = 3,   ...,   interaction = FALSE,   muModel = c("lin", "irls", "none") )

title: Control options for the mfoce estimation method — mfoceControl
text: sigdig Optimization significant digits; controls inner/outer optimization tolerance (10^-sigdig), boundary check tolerance (5*10^(-sigdig+1)), ODE solver tolerances.  solver tolerances split solver stiffness keep atol well rtol: stiff solver (lsoda/liblsoda – auto-switching method – plus indLin, default) uses rtol = 10^(-sigdig-3), atol = 10^(-sigdig-5), non-stiff explicit dop853 uses looser rtol = 10^-sigdig, atol = 10^(-sigdig-3).  sensitivity (atolSens/rtolSens) steady-state (ssAtol/ssRtol) tolerances run one order looser corresponding main tolerance.  default sigdig = 4 stiff solve atol = 1e-9, rtol = 1e-7. ... Parameters used default `foceiControl()` interaction Interaction term model, case default `FALSE`; changed, use `mfocei` instead muModel Selects regression variant; `mfoceControl()` always `"lin"` changed – use `ifoceControl()` IRLS variant.

title: Control options for the mfoce estimation method — mfoceControl
text: mfoceControl object

title: Control options for the mfoce estimation method — mfoceControl
text: `mfocei`/`ifocei` (related) methods apply mu2+ covariate hooks, expand algebraic mu-referenced covariate expressions (e.g. `cl.wt*log(WT/70)`) estimable mu-referenced parameters split covariates non-time-varying (absorbed phi term) time-varying (kept `beta` regressors).  Calling `focei` directly apply hooks, methods can estimate mu-referenced models plain `focei` – genuine difference calling e.g. `est="mfocei"` `est="focei"`. mu-referenced population thetas – without covariates – profiled outer optimizer -C++ regression (intercept-covariate-free pairs), outer gradients calculated non-mu-referenced parameters (residual errors, omegas, non-mu thetas).  Bounded mu-referenced parameters regression-updated update clamped bounds (clamp reported fit note); user-fixed (`fix()`) mu thetas stay regression.

what: Control options for the mfocep (mu-referenced FOCE+) estimation method — mfocepControl
title: Control options for the mfocep (mu-referenced FOCE+) estimation method — mfocepControl
text: Mu-referenced-FOCEI-family closed-form-regression (`"lin"`) variant FOCE+ (interaction, live conditional residual variance R); `mfoce` `foce = "foce+"` forced (see `foceiControl(foce=)`).

title: Control options for the mfocep (mu-referenced FOCE+) estimation method — mfocepControl
code: mfocepControl(   sigdig = 3,   ...,   interaction = FALSE,   muModel = c("lin", "irls", "none"),   foce = "foce+" )

title: Control options for the mfocep (mu-referenced FOCE+) estimation method — mfocepControl
text: sigdig Optimization significant digits; controls inner/outer optimization tolerance (10^-sigdig), boundary check tolerance (5*10^(-sigdig+1)), ODE solver tolerances.  solver tolerances split solver stiffness keep atol well rtol: stiff solver (lsoda/liblsoda – auto-switching method – plus indLin, default) uses rtol = 10^(-sigdig-3), atol = 10^(-sigdig-5), non-stiff explicit dop853 uses looser rtol = 10^-sigdig, atol = 10^(-sigdig-3).  sensitivity (atolSens/rtolSens) steady-state (ssAtol/ssRtol) tolerances run one order looser corresponding main tolerance.  default sigdig = 4 stiff solve atol = 1e-9, rtol = 1e-7. ... Parameters used default `foceiControl()` interaction Interaction term model, case default `FALSE`; changed, use `mfocei` instead muModel Selects regression variant; `mfocepControl()` always `"lin"` changed – use `ifocepControl()` IRLS variant. foce FOCE residual-variance mode; `mfocepControl()` always `"foce+"` changed – use `mfoceControl()` `"nonmem"`

title: Control options for the mfocep (mu-referenced FOCE+) estimation method — mfocepControl
text: mfocepControl object

title: Control options for the mfocep (mu-referenced FOCE+) estimation method — mfocepControl
text: `mfocei`/`ifocei` (related) methods apply mu2+ covariate hooks, expand algebraic mu-referenced covariate expressions (e.g. `cl.wt*log(WT/70)`) estimable mu-referenced parameters split covariates non-time-varying (absorbed phi term) time-varying (kept `beta` regressors).  Calling `focei` directly apply hooks, methods can estimate mu-referenced models plain `focei` – genuine difference calling e.g. `est="mfocei"` `est="focei"`. mu-referenced population thetas – without covariates – profiled outer optimizer -C++ regression (intercept-covariate-free pairs), outer gradients calculated non-mu-referenced parameters (residual errors, omegas, non-mu thetas).  Bounded mu-referenced parameters regression-updated update clamped bounds (clamp reported fit note); user-fixed (`fix()`) mu thetas stay regression.

title: Control options for the mfocep (mu-referenced FOCE+) estimation method — mfocepControl
text: Matthew L. Fidler

what: Control options for the mlaplace estimation method — mlaplaceControl
title: Control options for the mlaplace estimation method — mlaplaceControl
text: Mu-referenced-FOCEI-family closed-form-regression (`"lin"`) variant Laplace method (`nAGQ=1`); see `foceiControl(muModel=)`.

title: Control options for the mlaplace estimation method — mlaplaceControl
code: mlaplaceControl(sigdig = 3, ..., nAGQ = 1, muModel = c("lin", "irls", "none"))

title: Control options for the mlaplace estimation method — mlaplaceControl
text: sigdig Optimization significant digits; controls inner/outer optimization tolerance (10^-sigdig), boundary check tolerance (5*10^(-sigdig+1)), ODE solver tolerances.  solver tolerances split solver stiffness keep atol well rtol: stiff solver (lsoda/liblsoda – auto-switching method – plus indLin, default) uses rtol = 10^(-sigdig-3), atol = 10^(-sigdig-5), non-stiff explicit dop853 uses looser rtol = 10^-sigdig, atol = 10^(-sigdig-3).  sensitivity (atolSens/rtolSens) steady-state (ssAtol/ssRtol) tolerances run one order looser corresponding main tolerance.  default sigdig = 4 stiff solve atol = 1e-9, rtol = 1e-7. ... Parameters used default `foceiControl()` nAGQ Number Gauss-Hermite adaptive quadrature points. `0` disables AGQ; `1` equivalent Laplace. Cost grows quickly ETAs: EBE found, expect `nAGQ^neta` (even `nAGQ`) `(nAGQ^neta)-1` (odd `nAGQ`) additional evaluations per subject. muModel Selects regression variant; `mlaplaceControl()` always `"lin"` changed – use `ilaplaceControl()` IRLS variant.

code: n1qn1Control(   epsilon = NULL,   max_iterations = 10000,   nsim = 10000,   imp = 0,   print.functions = FALSE,   returnN1qn1 = FALSE,   stickyRecalcN = 4,   maxOdeRecalc = 5,   odeRecalcFactor = 10^(0.5),   indTolRelax = TRUE,   useColor = NULL,   printNcol = NULL,   print = 1L,   normType = c("rescale2", "mean", "rescale", "std", "len", "constant"),   scaleType = c("nlmixr2", "norm", "mult", "multAdd"),   scaleCmax = 1e+05,   scaleCmin = 1e-05,   scaleC = NULL,   scaleTo = 1,   gradTo = 1,   rxControl = NULL,   optExpression = TRUE,   sumProd = FALSE,   literalFix = TRUE,   literalFixRes = TRUE,   addProp = c("combined2", "combined1"),   eventSens = c("jump", "fd"),   sensMethod = c("default", "forward", "adjoint"),   calcTables = TRUE,   compress = FALSE,   covMethod = c("r", "n1qn1", ""),   adjObf = TRUE,   ci = 0.95,   sigdig = 4,   sigdigTable = NULL,   boundedTransform = TRUE,   ... )

title: Control for n1qn1 estimation method in nlmixr2 — n1qn1Control
text: epsilon Precision estimate n1qn1 optimization. max_iterations Number iterations nsim Number function evaluations imp Verbosity messages. print.functions Boolean control function value parameter estimates echoed every time function called. returnN1qn1 return n1qn1 output instead nlmixr2 fit stickyRecalcN number bad ODE solves reducing atol/rtol rest problem. maxOdeRecalc Maximum number times reduce ODE tolerances try resolve system bad ODE solve. odeRecalcFactor ODE recalculation factor ODE solving goes bad, factor rtol/atol reduced indTolRelax `TRUE` (default), subjects whose ODE solve produced NaN/Inf tolerances relaxed, relaxed tolerance persists across optimizer calls (sticky). `FALSE`, subjects tolerances relaxed retry tolerances reset afterward. useColor Logical (`NULL`) emit ANSI bold/color escapes iteration print. `NULL` (default) defers [crayon::has_color()]. printNcol Integer (`NULL`) parameter columns per row wrapping. `NULL` (default) uses `floor((getOption("width") - 23) / 12)`. print Either scalar print-frequency (`0` = suppress, `1` (default) = every evaluation, `N` = every Nth), pre-built [iterPrintControl()] object. Equivalent `iterPrintControl(every = print, ncol = printNcol, useColor = useColor)`. normType Parameter normalization/scaling used get scaled initial values scaleType, form Vscaled = (Vunscaled-C1)/C2 (see Feature Scaling; rescale2 follows OptdesX manual): "rescale2" scales parameters (-1, 1); "rescale" (min-max) scales (0, 1); "mean" centers mean range (0, 1); "std" standardizes mean/sd; "len" scales unit (Euclidean) length; "constant" performs normalization (C1=0, C2=1). scaleType scaling scheme nlmixr2: "nlmixr2" (default) scales (current-init)*scaleC[] + scaleTo, scaleTo normType scales scaleC; "norm" uses simple scaling normType; "mult" scales multiplicatively current/init*scaleTo; "multAdd" scales linearly ((current-init)+scaleTo) parameters exponential block (e.g. exp(theta)) multiplicatively otherwise. scaleCmax Maximum value scaleC prevent overflow. scaleCmin Minimum value scaleC prevent underflow. scaleC Scaling constant used scaleType="nlmixr2"; specified, chosen parameter type keep gradient sizes similar log scale: `1` exp()-transformed/power/boxCox/ yeoJohnson parameters, `0.5*abs(est)` additive/proportional/ lognormal error parameters, `abs(1/digamma(est+1))` factorials, `log(abs(est))*abs(est)` log-scale parameters. May set explicitly per parameter defaults apply well. scaleTo Scale initial parameter estimate value. default 1.  zero , scaling performed. gradTo factor gradient scaled optimizing.  works scaleType="nlmixr2". rxControl `rxode2` ODE solving options fitting, created `rxControl()` optExpression Optimize rxode2 expression speed calculation. default turned . sumProd boolean indicating model change multiplication high precision multiplication sums high precision sums using PreciseSums package.  default FALSE. literalFix boolean, substitute fixed population values literals re-adjust ui parameter estimates optimization; Default `TRUE`. literalFixRes boolean, substitute fixed population values literals re-adjust ui parameter estimates optimization; Default `TRUE`. addProp Type additive-plus-proportional error: `"combined1"`, standard deviations add: $$y = f + (+ b\times f^c) \times \varepsilon$$; `"combined2"`, variances add: $$y = f + \sqrt{^2 + b^2\times f^{2\times c}} \times \varepsilon$$. y = observed, f = predicted, = additive sd, b = proportional/power sd, c = power exponent (1 proportional case). eventSens Controls dosing/event-parameter (`alag`, `F`, `rate`, `dur`) sensitivities computed THETA/ETA gradients: `"jump"` (default) uses rxode2's analytic event sensitivities; `"fd"` uses legacy finite-difference behavior. sensMethod Method used compute ODE parameter sensitivities: `"default"` (default) defers global option `getOption("nlmixr2est.adjoint")` (`"forward"` default); `"forward"` uses classic variational (forward) sensitivity ODEs; `"adjoint"` uses -engine discrete adjoint matching adjoint (`s`) method. calcTables boolean determine foceiFit calculate tables. default TRUE compress object compressed items covMethod Method calculating covariance.  "r" (default) uses nlmixr2's nlmixr2Hess() Hessian; "n1qn1" uses optimizer's Hessian; "" skips covariance step. adjObf boolean indicate objective function adjusted closer NONMEM's default objective function.  default TRUE ci Confidence level tables.  default 0.95 95% confidence. sigdig Optimization significant digits; controls inner/outer optimization tolerance (10^-sigdig), boundary check tolerance (5*10^(-sigdig+1)), ODE solver tolerances.  solver tolerances split solver stiffness keep atol well rtol: stiff solver (lsoda/liblsoda – auto-switching method – plus indLin, default) uses rtol = 10^(-sigdig-3), atol = 10^(-sigdig-5), non-stiff explicit dop853 uses looser rtol = 10^-sigdig, atol = 10^(-sigdig-3).  sensitivity (atolSens/rtolSens) steady-state (ssAtol/ssRtol) tolerances run one order looser corresponding main tolerance.  default sigdig = 4 stiff solve atol = 1e-9, rtol = 1e-7. sigdigTable Significant digits final output table. specified, matches significant digits `sigdig` optimization algorithm.  `sigdig` NULL, use 3. boundedTransform `TRUE` (default), bounded parameters transformed unbounded optimization methods back-transformed final estimates. `FALSE` optimizes original scale bounds passed optimizer. `NA` transforms optimization skips final back-transform. ... title: Control for n1qn1 estimation method in nlmixr2 — n1qn1Control
code: # \donttest{ # A logit regression example with emax model  dsn <- data.frame(i=1:1000) dsn$time <- exp(rnorm(1000)) dsn$DV=rbinom(1000,1,exp(-1+dsn$time)/(1+exp(-1+dsn$time)))  mod <- function() {  ini({    E0 <- 0.5    Em <- 0.5    E50 <- 2    g <- fix(2)  })  model({    v <- E0+Em*time^g/(E50^g+time^g)    ll(bin) ~ DV * v - log(1 + exp(v))  }) }  fit2 <- nlmixr(mod, dsn, est="n1qn1") #>   #>   #>   #>   #> ℹ parameter labels from comments are typically ignored in non-interactive mode #> ℹ Need to run with the source intact to parse comments #> → loading into symengine environment... #> → pruning branches (`if`/`else`) of population log-likelihood model... #> ✔ done #> → calculate ∂(f)/∂(θ) #> → finding duplicate expressions in nlm llik gradient... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in nlm llik gradient... #> → finding duplicate expressions in nlm pred-only... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in nlm pred-only... #>   #>   #>   #>   #> → calculating covariance #> ✔ done #> → loading into symengine environment... #> → pruning branches (`if`/`else`) of full model... #> ✔ done #> → finding duplicate expressions in EBE model... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in EBE model... #> → compiling EBE model... #>   #>   #> ✔ done #> → Calculating residuals/tables #> ✔ done  print(fit2) #> ── nlmixr² log-likelihood n1qn1 ── #>  #>           OBJF      AIC      BIC Log-likelihood Condition#(Cov) Condition#(Cor) #> lPop -715.2361 1128.641 1143.364      -561.3205        493.1802         71.7887 #>  #> ── Time (sec $time): ── #>  #>             setup  optimize covariance preprocess postprocess table compress #> elapsed 0.3128889 0.5428134  5.718e-06      0.048       0.005 0.024    0.001 #>            other #> elapsed 0.108292 #>  #> ── ($parFixed or $parFixedDf): ── #>  #>        Est.     SE  %RSE   Back-transformed(95%CI) #> E0  -0.7540 0.2468 32.73 -0.7540 (-1.238, -0.2703) #> Em    6.000  2.701 45.01     6.000 (0.7071, 11.29) #> E50   2.931  1.257 42.87     2.931 (0.4682, 5.395) #> g     2.000  FIXED FIXED                     2.000 #>   #>   Covariance Type ($covMethod): r #>   Censoring ($censInformation): No censoring #>  #> ── Fit Data (object is a modified tibble): ── #> # A tibble: 1,000 × 5 #>   ID      TIME    DV  IPRED      v #>   <fct>  <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1 1     0.0591     0 -0.386 -0.752 #> 2 1     0.0591     0 -0.386 -0.752 #> 3 1     0.0628     0 -0.386 -0.751 #> # ℹ 997 more rows  # you can also get the nlm output with fit2$n1qn1  fit2$n1qn1 #> $value #> [1] 561.3205 #>  #> $par #>        E0        Em       E50  #> -0.753981  6.000383  2.931381  #>  #> $H #>              [,1]         [,2]         [,3] #> [1,]  0.001615830  0.002649943 -0.007050603 #> [2,]  0.002649943  0.009531677 -0.020904357 #> [3,] -0.007050603 -0.020904357  0.050493848 #>  #> $c.hess #>  [1]  0.001615830  0.002649943 -0.007050603  0.009531677 -0.020904357 #>  [6]  0.050493848  0.000000000  0.000000000  0.000000000  0.000000000 #> [11]  0.000000000  0.000000000  0.000000000  0.000000000  0.000000000 #> [16]  0.000000000  0.000000000  0.000000000  0.000000000  0.000000000 #> [21]  0.000000000  0.000000000  0.000000000  0.000000000 #>  #> $n.fn #> [1] 38 #>  #> $n.gr #> [1] 38 #>  #> $scaleC #> [1] 0.002875081 0.036929293 0.033369905 #>  #> $par.scaled #>        E0        Em       E50  #> -437.1549  147.9436   28.9108  #>  #> $hessian #>               E0           Em          E50 #> E0   0.001602122  0.002609465 -0.006942852 #> Em   0.002609465  0.009410840 -0.020574667 #> E50 -0.006942852 -0.020574667  0.049546010 #>  #> $cov.scaled #>           E0       Em      E50 #> E0  7368.594 2325.943 1998.437 #> Em  2325.943 5348.209 2546.851 #> E50 1998.437 2546.851 1418.388 #>  #> $r #>                E0           Em          E50 #> E0   0.0008010608  0.001304733 -0.003471426 #> Em   0.0013047326  0.004705420 -0.010287333 #> E50 -0.0034714262 -0.010287333  0.024773005 #>   # The nlm control has been modified slightly to include # extra components and name the parameters # }

occurs, value 1     means initial final details printed value     2 means full tracing information printed. ndigit number significant digits function f. gradtol positive scalar giving tolerance     scaled gradient considered close enough zero     terminate algorithm.  scaled gradient     measure relative change f direction     p[] divided relative change p[]. stepmax positive scalar gives algorithm. stepmax chosen     small enough prevent first two occurrences,     larger anticipated reasonable step. steptol positive scalar providing minimum allowable     relative step length. iterlim positive integer specifying maximum number     iterations performed program terminated. check.analyticals logical scalar specifying whether     analytic gradients Hessians, supplied,     checked numerical derivatives initial parameter     values. can help detect incorrectly formulated gradients     Hessians. returnNlm logical allows return `nlm` object solveType controls whether `nlm` uses nlmixr2's analytical gradients (event-related parameters like lag time/duration/rate/F use Shi2021 finite differences instead): `"hessian"` builds Hessian analytical gradient via finite differences, `"combined2"`, variances add: $$y = f + \sqrt{^2 + b^2\times f^{2\times c}} \times \varepsilon$$. y = observed, f = predicted, = additive sd, b = proportional/power sd, c = power exponent (1 proportional case). calcTables boolean determine foceiFit calculate tables. default TRUE compress object compressed items covMethod "r" uses nlmixr2's `nlmixr2Hess()` hessian, "nlm" uses hessian `stats::nlm(.., hessian=TRUE)`; controls inner/outer optimization tolerance (10^-sigdig), boundary check tolerance (5*10^(-sigdig+1)), ODE solver tolerances.  solver tolerances split solver stiffness keep atol well rtol: stiff solver (lsoda/liblsoda – auto-switching method – plus indLin, default) uses rtol = 10^(-sigdig-3), atol = 10^(-sigdig-5), non-stiff explicit dop853 uses looser rtol = 10^-sigdig, atol = 10^(-sigdig-3).  sensitivity (atolSens/rtolSens) steady-state (ssAtol/ssRtol) tolerances run one order looser corresponding main tolerance.  default sigdig = 4 stiff solve atol = 1e-9, rtol = 1e-7. sigdigTable Significant digits final output table. specified, matches significant digits `sigdig` optimization algorithm.  `sigdig` NULL, use 3. boundedTransform `TRUE` (default), bounded parameters transformed unbounded optimization methods back-transformed final estimates. `FALSE` optimizes original scale bounds passed optimizer. `NA` transforms optimization skips final back-transform. ... additional arguments passed f.

code: # \donttest{ # A logit regression example with emax model  dsn <- data.frame(i=1:1000) dsn$time <- exp(rnorm(1000)) dsn$DV=rbinom(1000,1,exp(-1+dsn$time)/(1+exp(-1+dsn$time)))  mod <- function() {  ini({    E0 <- 0.5    Em <- 0.5    E50 <- 2    g <- fix(2)  })  model({    v <- E0+Em*time^g/(E50^g+time^g)    ll(bin) ~ DV * v - log(1 + exp(v))  }) }  fit2 <- nlmixr(mod, dsn, est="nlm") #>   #>   #>   #>   #> ℹ parameter labels from comments are typically ignored in non-interactive mode #> ℹ Need to run with the source intact to parse comments #> → loading into symengine environment... #> → pruning branches (`if`/`else`) of population log-likelihood model... #> ✔ done #> → calculate ∂(f)/∂(θ) #> → finding duplicate expressions in nlm llik gradient... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in nlm llik gradient... #> → finding duplicate expressions in nlm pred-only... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in nlm pred-only... #>   #>   #>   #>   #> → calculating covariance #> ✔ done #> → loading into symengine environment... #> → pruning branches (`if`/`else`) of full model... #> ✔ done #> → finding duplicate expressions in EBE model... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in EBE model... #> → compiling EBE model... #>   #>   #> ✔ done #> → Calculating residuals/tables #> ✔ done  print(fit2) #> ── nlmixr² log-likelihood nlm ── #>  #>           OBJF      AIC      BIC Log-likelihood Condition#(Cov) Condition#(Cor) #> lPop -688.1882 1155.689 1170.412      -574.8444         2549011        156229.8 #>  #> ── Time (sec $time): ── #>  #>             setup  optimize covariance preprocess postprocess table compress #> elapsed 0.3240652 0.5531521  6.599e-06      0.044       0.006 0.024    0.001 #>              other #> elapsed 0.09477612 #>  #> ── ($parFixed or $parFixedDf): ── #>  #>        Est.    SE  %RSE Back-transformed(95%CI) #> E0  -0.6131 4.608 751.6 -0.6131 (-9.645, 8.419) #> Em    9.016 203.0  2251   9.016 (-388.8, 406.8) #> E50   4.661 86.21  1849   4.661 (-164.3, 173.6) #> g     2.000 FIXED FIXED                   2.000 #>   #>   Covariance Type ($covMethod): r (nlm) #>   Censoring ($censInformation): No censoring #>   Minimization message ($message):   #>     relative gradient is close to zero, current iterate is probably solution  #>  #> ── Fit Data (object is a modified tibble): ── #> # A tibble: 1,000 × 5 #>   ID      TIME    DV  IPRED      v #>   <fct>  <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1 1     0.0358     0 -0.433 -0.613 #> 2 1     0.0521     0 -0.433 -0.612 #> 3 1     0.0610     0 -0.433 -0.612 #> # ℹ 997 more rows  # you can also get the nlm output with fit2$nlm  fit2$nlm #> $minimum #> [1] 574.8444 #>  #> $estimate #>         E0         Em        E50  #> -0.6130711  9.0163993  4.6613852  #>  #> $gradient #> [1] -5.406938e-07 -1.822863e-06  3.341858e-06 #>  #> $hessian #>               E0           Em          E50 #> E0   0.001540707  0.001102975 -0.003277943 #> Em   0.001102975  0.002027278 -0.005561242 #> E50 -0.003277943 -0.005561242  0.013715325 #>  #> $code #> [1] 1 #>  #> $iterations #> [1] 17 #>  #> $scaleC #> [1] 0.002771929 0.032196427 0.029707265 #>  #> $estimate.scaled #>         E0         Em        E50  #> -402.55106  263.51380   90.58702  #>  #> $cov.scaled #>           E0       Em      E50 #> E0   2763643 10470186  4820805 #> Em  10470186 39741385 18293564 #> E50  4820805 18293564  8421379 #>  #> $r #>                E0            Em          E50 #> E0   0.0007703535  0.0005514877 -0.001638971 #> Em   0.0005514877  0.0010136390 -0.002780621 #> E50 -0.0016389714 -0.0027806211  0.006857663 #>   # The nlm control has been modified slightly to include # extra components and name the parameters # }

title: nlmixr2 nlminb defaults — nlminbControl
text: eval.max Maximum number evaluations objective function allowed.  Defaults 200. iter.max Maximum number iterations allowed.  Defaults 150. trace value objective function parameters printed every trace'th iteration.  0 trace information printed abs.tol Absolute tolerance.  Defaults 0 absolute convergence test used.  objective function known non-negative, previous default `1e-20` appropriate rel.tol Relative tolerance.  `NULL` (default) derived `sigdig` way `foceiControl()` (`10^(-sigdig-1)`). x.tol X tolerance.  `NULL` (default) derived `sigdig` (`10^(-sigdig-1)`). xf.tol false convergence tolerance.  Defaults `2.2e-14`. step.min Minimum step size.  Default `1.`. step.max Maximum step size.  Default `1.`. sing.tol singular convergence tolerance; defaults `rel.tol;. scale See PORT documentation (leave alone). scale.init ... probably need check PORT documentation diff.g estimated bound relative error objective function value rxControl `rxode2` ODE solving options fitting, created `rxControl()` optExpression Optimize rxode2 expression speed calculation. default turned . sumProd boolean indicating model change multiplication high precision multiplication sums high precision sums using PreciseSums package.  default FALSE. literalFix boolean, substitute fixed population values literals re-adjust ui parameter estimates optimization; Default `TRUE`. literalFixRes boolean, substitute fixed population values literals re-adjust ui parameter estimates optimization; Default `TRUE`. returnNlminb logical; TRUE return nlminb result instead nlmixr2 fit object solveType controls whether `nlm` uses nlmixr2's analytical gradients (event-related parameters like lag time/duration/rate/F use Shi2021 finite differences instead): `"hessian"` builds Hessian analytical gradient via finite differences, `"gradient"` supplies gradient lets `nlm` compute finite-difference Hessian, `"fun"` lets `nlm` compute finite differences. stickyRecalcN number bad ODE solves reducing atol/rtol rest problem. maxOdeRecalc Maximum number times reduce ODE tolerances try resolve system bad ODE solve. odeRecalcFactor ODE recalculation factor ODE solving goes bad, factor rtol/atol reduced indTolRelax `TRUE` (default), subjects whose ODE solve produced NaN/Inf tolerances relaxed, relaxed tolerance persists across optimizer calls (sticky). `FALSE`, subjects tolerances relaxed retry tolerances reset afterward. eventType Event gradient type dosing events; Can "central" "forward" shiErr represents epsilon optimizing ideal step size numeric differentiation using Shi2021 method shi21maxFD maximum number steps optimization forward difference step size using dosing events (lag time, modeled duration/rate bioavailability) optimHessType Hessian type numeric-difference individual Hessians generalized log-likelihood estimation: "central" (matches R's `optimHess()`, default) "forward" (faster). hessErr represents epsilon optimizing Hessian step size using Shi2021 method. shi21maxHess Maximum number times optimize best step size hessian calculation useColor Logical (`NULL`) emit ANSI bold/color escapes iteration print. `NULL` (default) defers [crayon::has_color()]. printNcol Integer (`NULL`) parameter columns per row wrapping. `NULL` (default) uses `floor((getOption("width") - 23) / 12)`. print Either scalar print-frequency (`0` = suppress, `1` (default) = every evaluation, `N` = every Nth), pre-built [iterPrintControl()] object. Equivalent `iterPrintControl(every = print, ncol = printNcol, useColor = useColor)`. normType Parameter normalization/scaling used get scaled initial values scaleType, form Vscaled = (Vunscaled-C1)/C2 (see Feature Scaling; rescale2 follows OptdesX manual): "rescale2" scales parameters (-1, 1); "rescale" (min-max) scales (0, 1); "mean" centers mean range (0, 1); "std" standardizes mean/sd; "len" scales unit (Euclidean) length; "constant" performs normalization (C1=0, C2=1). scaleType scaling scheme nlmixr2: "nlmixr2" (default) scales (current-init)*scaleC[] + scaleTo, scaleTo normType scales scaleC; "norm" uses simple scaling normType; "mult" scales multiplicatively current/init*scaleTo; "multAdd" scales linearly ((current-init)+scaleTo) parameters exponential block (e.g. exp(theta)) multiplicatively otherwise. scaleCmax Maximum value scaleC prevent overflow. scaleCmin Minimum value scaleC prevent underflow. scaleC Scaling constant used scaleType="nlmixr2"; specified, chosen parameter type keep gradient sizes similar log scale: `1` exp()-transformed/power/boxCox/ yeoJohnson parameters, `0.5*abs(est)` additive/proportional/ lognormal error parameters, `abs(1/digamma(est+1))` factorials, `log(abs(est))*abs(est)` log-scale parameters. May set explicitly per parameter defaults apply well. scaleTo Scale initial parameter estimate value. default 1.  zero , scaling performed. gradTo factor gradient scaled optimizing.  works scaleType="nlmixr2". addProp Type additive-plus-proportional error: `"combined1"`, standard deviations add: $$y = f + (+ b\times f^c) \times \varepsilon$$; `"combined2"`, variances add: $$y = f + \sqrt{^2 + b^2\times f^{2\times c}} \times \varepsilon$$. y = observed, f = predicted, = additive sd, b = proportional/power sd, c = power exponent (1 proportional case). eventSens Controls dosing/event-parameter (`alag`, `F`, `rate`, `dur`) sensitivities computed THETA/ETA gradients: `"jump"` (default) uses rxode2's analytic event sensitivities; `"fd"` uses legacy finite-difference behavior. sensMethod Method used compute ODE parameter sensitivities: `"default"` (default) defers global option `getOption("nlmixr2est.adjoint")` (`"forward"` default); `"forward"` uses classic variational (forward) sensitivity ODEs; `"adjoint"` uses -engine discrete adjoint matching adjoint (`s`) method. calcTables boolean determine foceiFit calculate tables. default TRUE compress object compressed items covMethod Method calculating covariance.  "r" (default) uses nlmixr2's nlmixr2Hess() Hessian; "nlminb" uses optimizer's Hessian; "" skips covariance step. adjObf boolean indicate objective function adjusted closer NONMEM's default objective function.  default TRUE ci Confidence level tables.  default 0.95 95% confidence. sigdig Optimization significant digits; controls inner/outer optimization tolerance (10^-sigdig), boundary check tolerance (5*10^(-sigdig+1)), ODE solver tolerances.  solver tolerances split solver stiffness keep atol well rtol: stiff solver (lsoda/liblsoda – auto-switching method – plus indLin, default) uses rtol = 10^(-sigdig-3), atol = 10^(-sigdig-5), non-stiff explicit dop853 uses looser rtol = 10^-sigdig, atol = 10^(-sigdig-3).  sensitivity (atolSens/rtolSens) steady-state (ssAtol/ssRtol) tolerances run one order looser corresponding main tolerance.  default sigdig = 4 stiff solve atol = 1e-9, rtol = 1e-7. sigdigTable Significant digits final output table. specified, matches significant digits `sigdig` optimization algorithm.  `sigdig` NULL, use 3. ... arguments supplied objective.

Currently can use either add(parameter)      additive error,  prop(parameter) proportional error add(parameter1) + prop(parameter2)      additive plus proportional error.  can also specify norm(parameter) additive error,      since follows normal distribution. routines, like saem require  parameters terms Pop.Parameter + Individual.Deviation.Parameter + Covariate*Covariate.Parameter.      order parameters matter.  similar NONMEM's mu-referencing, though      quite restrictive. type parameter model determined initial block;  Covariates used      model missing ini block.  variables need present modeling      dataset model run. code: f <- function(){ ## Note the arguments to the function are currently                  ## ignored by nlmixr     ini({         ## Initial conditions for population parameters (sometimes         ## called theta parameters) are defined by either `<-` or '='         lCl <- 1.6      #log Cl (L/hr)         ## Note that simple expressions that evaluate to a number are         ## OK for defining initial conditions (like in R)         lVc = log(90)  #log V (L)         ## Also a comment on a parameter is captured as a parameter label         lKa <- 1 #log Ka (1/hr)         ## Bounds may be specified by c(lower, est, upper), like NONMEM:         ## Residuals errors are assumed to be population parameters         prop.err <- c(0, 0.2, 1)     })     ## The model block will be discussed later     model({}) } f <- function(){     ini({         lCl <- 1.6      #log Cl (L/hr)         lVc = log(90)  #log V (L)         lKa <- 1 #log Ka (1/hr)         prop.err <- c(0, 0.2, 1)         ## Initial estimate for ka IIV variance         ## Labels work for single parameters         eta.ka ~ 0.1 # BSV Ka          ## For correlated parameters, you specify the names of each         ## correlated parameter separated by a addition operator `+`         ## and the left handed side specifies the lower triangular         ## matrix initial of the covariance matrix.         eta.cl + eta.vc ~ c(0.1,                             0.005, 0.1)         ## Note that labels do not currently work for correlated         ## parameters.  Also do not put comments inside the lower         ## triangular matrix as this will currently break the model.     })     ## The model block will be discussed later     model({}) } f <- function(){     ini({         lCl <- 1.6      #log Cl (L/hr)         lVc <- log(90)   #log Vc (L)         lKA <- 0.1      #log Ka (1/hr)         prop.err <- c(0, 0.2, 1)         eta.Cl ~ 0.1 ## BSV Cl         eta.Vc ~ 0.1 ## BSV Vc         eta.KA ~ 0.1 ## BSV Ka     })     model({         ## First parameters are defined in terms of the initial estimates         ## parameter names.         Cl <- exp(lCl + eta.Cl)         Vc = exp(lVc + eta.Vc)         KA <- exp(lKA + eta.KA)         ## After the differential equations are defined         kel <- Cl / Vc;         d/dt(depot)    = -KA*depot;         d/dt(centr)  =  KA*depot-kel*centr;         ## And the concentration is then calculated         cp = centr / Vc;         ## Last, nlmixr is told that the plasma concentration follows         ## a proportional error (estimated by the parameter prop.err)         cp ~ prop(prop.err)     }) } f <- function(){     ini({         lCl <- 1.6      #log Cl (L/hr)         lVc <- log(90)   #log Vc (L)         lKA <- 0.1      #log Ka (1/hr)         prop.err <- c(0, 0.2, 1)         eta.Cl ~ 0.1 ## BSV Cl         eta.Vc ~ 0.1 ## BSV Vc         eta.KA ~ 0.1 ## BSV Ka     })     model({         Cl <- exp(lCl + eta.Cl)         Vc = exp(lVc + eta.Vc)         KA <- exp(lKA + eta.KA)         ## Instead of specifying the ODEs, you can use         ## the linCmt() function to use the solved system.         ##         ## This function determines the type of PK solved system         ## to use by the parameters that are defined.  In this case         ## it knows that this is a one-compartment model with first-order         ## absorption.         linCmt() ~ prop(prop.err)     }) } > nlmixr(f) ## 1-compartment model with first-order absorption in terms of Cl ## Initialization: ################################################################################ Fixed Effects ($theta):     lCl     lVc     lKA 1.60000 4.49981 0.10000  Omega ($omega):      [,1] [,2] [,3] [1,]  0.1  0.0  0.0 [2,]  0.0  0.1  0.0 [3,]  0.0  0.0  0.1  ## Model: ################################################################################ Cl <- exp(lCl + eta.Cl) Vc = exp(lVc + eta.Vc) KA <- exp(lKA + eta.KA) ## Instead of specifying the ODEs, you can use ## the linCmt() function to use the solved system. ## ## This function determines the type of PK solved system ## to use by the parameters that are defined.  In this case ## it knows that this is a one-compartment model with first-order ## absorption. linCmt() ~ prop(prop.err) fit <- nlmixr(model.function, dataset, est="est", control=estControl(options)) > f <- function(){     ini({         lCl <- 1.6      #log Cl (L/hr)         lVc <- log(90)   #log Vc (L)         lKA <- 0.1      #log Ka (1/hr)         prop.err <- c(0, 0.2, 1)         eta.Cl ~ 0.1 ## BSV Cl         eta.Vc ~ 0.1 ## BSV Vc         eta.KA ~ 0.1 ## BSV Ka     })     model({         ## First parameters are defined in terms of the initial estimates         ## parameter names.         Cl <- exp(lCl + eta.Cl)         Vc = exp(lVc + eta.Vc)         KA <- exp(lKA + eta.KA)         ## After the differential equations are defined         kel <- Cl / Vc;         d/dt(depot)    = -KA*depot;         d/dt(centr)  =  KA*depot-kel*centr;         ## And the concentration is then calculated         cp = centr / Vc;         ## Last, nlmixr is told that the plasma concentration follows         ## a proportional error (estimated by the parameter prop.err)         cp ~ prop(prop.err)     }) } > fit.s <- nlmixr(f,d,est="saem",control=saemControl(n.burn=50,n.em=100,print=50)); text: fit Nlmixr2 fit object covsInterpolation specifies interpolation method time-varying covariates. solving ODEs often samples times outside sampling time specified events. happens, time varying covariates interpolated.  Currently can : "linear" interpolation, interpolates covariate solving line observed covariates extrapolating new covariate value. "locf" – Last observation carried forward (default). "nocb" – Next Observation Carried Backward.  method NONMEM uses. "midpoint" Last observation carried forward midpoint; Next observation carried backward midpoint. time-varying covariates missing value present, interpolation method use either "locf" "nocb" throughout type covariate interpolation selected. using linear midpoint interpolation, lower point interpolation use locf interpolate missing covariates upper point use nocb interpolate missing covariates. minimum optional lower limit primary    covariate. Defaults min(primary). maximum optional upper limit primary    covariate. Defaults max(primary). length.optional integer number primary    covariate values evaluate predictions.  Defaults    51. ... methods generic may require additional    arguments. object fitted model object predictions can    extracted, using predict method. primary optional one-sided formula specifying primary    covariate used generate augmented predictions.    default,  covariate can extracted data used generate    object (using getCovariate), used    primary.

text: ui UI used translation data data control focei control data creation table Table options env Environment setup needs following: - `$table` table options - `$origData` – Original Data - `$dataSav` – Processed data .foceiPreProcessData - `$idLvl` – Level information ID factor added - `$covLvl` – Level information items convert factor - `$ui` ui object - `$fullTheta` Full theta information - `$etaObf` data frame ID, etas OBJI - `$cov` covariance - `$covMethod` method calculating covariance - `$adjObf` objective function value adjusted - `$objective` objective function value - `$extra` Extra print information - `$method` Estimation method (printing) - `$omega` Omega matrix - `$theta` theta data frame - `$model` list model information table generation.  Needs `predOnly` model - `$message` Message display - `$est` estimation method - `$ofvType` (optional) tells type ofv currently use details need described est Estimation method

text: either function non-empty character string naming     function called. args list arguments function call.      names attribute args gives argument names. envir environment within evaluate call.      useful character string     arguments symbols quoted expressions. parameters calculate forward difference optimal forward difference interval gillRtol relative tolerance used Gill 1983 determination optimal step size. gillK Max steps determine optimal forward/central difference step size per parameter (Gill 1983). `0` = optimal step size determined. gillStep looking optimal forward difference step size, step size increase initial estimate .  iteration new step size = (prior step size)*gillStep gillFtol gillFtol gradient error tolerance acceptable issuing warning/error gradient estimates.

title: Get the optimal forward difference interval by Gill83 method — nlmixr2Gill83
text: data frame following columns: - info Gradient evaluation/forward difference information - hf Forward difference final estimate - df Derivative estimate - df2 2nd Derivative Estimate - err Error final estimate derivative - aEps Absolute difference forward numerical differences - rEps Relative Difference backward numerical differences - aEpsC Absolute difference central numerical differences - rEpsC Relative difference central numerical differences info returns one following: - "Assessed" Gradient assessed - "Good Success" Estimating optimal forward difference interval - "High Grad Error" Large error; Derivative estimate error fTol derivative - "Constant Grad" Function constant nearly constant parameter - "Odd/Linear Grad" Function odd nearly linear, df = K, df2 ~ 0 - "Grad changes quickly" df2 increases rapidly h decreases

Default 7. msMaxIter maximum number iterations nlminb    (iter.max) nlm (iterlim,    10-th step) optimization step inside nlme    optimization.  Default 50 (may small e.g.    overparametrized cases). minScale minimum factor shrink default step size    attempt decrease sum squares PNLS step.    Default 0.001. tolerance tolerance convergence criterion    nlme algorithm.  Default 1e-6. niterEM number iterations EM algorithm used refine    initial estimates random effects variance-covariance    coefficients.  Default 25. pnlsTol tolerance convergence criterion PNLS    step.  Default 1e-3. msTol tolerance convergence criterion nlm,    passed gradtol argument function (see    documentation nlm).  Default 1e-7. returnObject logical value indicating whether fitted    object returned maximum number iterations    reached without convergence algorithm.  Default    FALSE. msVerbose logical value passed trace    nlminb(.., control= list(trace = *, ..))    argument print.level nlm().  Default    FALSE. msWarnNoConv logical indicating warning    signalled whenever minimization (opt)    LME step converge; defaults TRUE. gradHess logical value indicating whether numerical gradient    vectors Hessian matrices log-likelihood function    used nlm optimization. option available    correlation structure (corStruct) variance    function structure (varFunc) "varying" parameters    pdMat classes used random effects structure    pdSymm (general positive-definite), pdDiag (diagonal),    pdIdent (multiple identity),     pdCompSymm (compound symmetry).  Default TRUE. apVar logical value indicating whether approximate    covariance matrix variance-covariance parameters    calculated.  Default TRUE. .relStep relative step numerical derivatives    calculations.  Default .Machine$double.eps^(1/3). minAbsParApVar numeric value - minimum absolute parameter value    approximate variance calculation.  default 0.05. opt optimizer used, either "nlminb" (   default) "nlm". natural logical value indicating whether pdNatural    parametrization used general positive-definite matrices    (pdSymm) reStruct, approximate covariance    matrix estimators calculated.  Default TRUE. sigma optionally positive number fix residual error .    NULL, default, 0, sigma estimated. optExpression Optimize rxode2 expression speed calculation. default turned . literalFix boolean, substitute fixed population values literals re-adjust ui parameter estimates optimization; Default `TRUE`. sumProd boolean indicating model change multiplication high precision multiplication sums high precision sums using PreciseSums package.  default FALSE. rxControl `rxode2` ODE solving options fitting, created `rxControl()` method character string.  "REML" model fit    maximizing restricted log-likelihood.  "ML"    log-likelihood maximized.  Defaults "ML". random optionally, following: () two-sided formula    form r1+...+rn~x1+...+xm | g1/.../gQ,    r1,...,rn naming parameters included right    hand side model, x1+...+xm specifying    random-effects model    parameters g1/.../gQ grouping structure    (Q may equal 1, case /    required). random effects formula repeated    levels grouping, case multiple levels    grouping; (ii) two-sided formula form    r1+...+rn~x1+..+xm, list two-sided formulas form    r1~x1+...+xm, possibly different random-effects models    different parameters, pdMat object two-sided    formula, list two-sided formulas (.e. non-NULL value    formula(random)), list pdMat objects two-sided    formulas, lists two-sided formulas. case, grouping    structure formula given groups, derived    data used fit nonlinear mixed-effects model,    inherit class  groupedData,; (iii) named list    formulas, lists formulas, pdMat objects (ii),    grouping factors names. order nesting    assumed order order elements    list; (iv) reStruct object. See documentation    pdClasses description available pdMat    classes. Defaults fixed,    resulting fixed effects also random effects. fixed two-sided linear formula form    f1+...+fn~x1+...+xm, list two-sided formulas form    f1~x1+...+xm, possibly different models different    parameters. f1,...,fn names parameters included    right hand side model x1+...+xm    expressions define linear models parameters (left    hand side formula contains several parameters,    assumed follow linear model, described right hand    side expression).    1 right hand side formula(s) indicates single    fixed effects corresponding parameter(s). weights optional varFunc object one-sided formula    describing within-group heteroscedasticity structure. given    formula, used argument varFixed,    corresponding fixed variance weights. See documentation    varClasses description available varFunc    classes. Defaults NULL, corresponding homoscedastic    within-group errors. verbose optional logical value. TRUE information    evolution iterative algorithm printed. Default    FALSE. returnNlme Returns nlme object instead nlmixr object (default FALSE).  nlme specific options `random`, `fixed`, `sens`, nlme object returned addProp Type additive-plus-proportional error: `"combined1"`, standard deviations add: $$y = f + (+ b\times f^c) \times \varepsilon$$; `"combined2"`, variances add: $$y = f + \sqrt{^2 + b^2\times f^{2\times c}} \times \varepsilon$$. y = observed, f = predicted, = additive sd, b = proportional/power sd, c = power exponent (1 proportional case). calcTables boolean determine foceiFit calculate tables. default TRUE compress object compressed items adjObf boolean indicate objective function adjusted closer NONMEM's default objective function.  default TRUE ci Confidence level tables.  default 0.95 95% confidence. sigdig Optimization significant digits; controls inner/outer optimization tolerance (10^-sigdig), boundary check tolerance (5*10^(-sigdig+1)), ODE solver tolerances.  solver tolerances split solver stiffness keep atol well rtol: stiff solver (lsoda/liblsoda – auto-switching method – plus indLin, default) uses rtol = 10^(-sigdig-3), atol = 10^(-sigdig-5), non-stiff explicit dop853 uses looser rtol = 10^-sigdig, atol = 10^(-sigdig-3).  sensitivity (atolSens/rtolSens) steady-state (ssAtol/ssRtol) tolerances run one order looser corresponding main tolerance.  default sigdig = 4 stiff solve atol = 1e-9, rtol = 1e-7. sigdigTable Significant digits final output table. specified, matches significant digits `sigdig` optimization algorithm.  `sigdig` NULL, use 3. muRefCovAlg `TRUE` (default), algebraic expressions can mu-referenced internally rewritten mu-referenced covariates restored optimization. Mirrors saemControl(muRefCovAlg=)/nlmeControl(muRefCovAlg=); foceiControl() takes effect muModel != "none". eventSens Controls dosing/event-parameter (`alag`, `F`, `rate`, `dur`) sensitivities computed THETA/ETA gradients: `"jump"` (default) uses rxode2's analytic event sensitivities; `"fd"` uses legacy finite-difference behavior. print Convenience alias shared nlmixr `print` control. `nlme` prints progress `verbose` option, `print` maps : `print=0` runs quietly (`verbose=FALSE`) positive value verbose (`verbose=TRUE`).  `print` supplied explicit `verbose` used given. covMethod Covariance method: `"analytic"` (default) computes focei observed-information covariance converged nlme estimates post-fit (falling back finite-difference `"r,s"` -> `"r"`/`"s"` chain analytic scope); `"r,s"`, `"r"`, `"s"` request finite-difference forms directly; `"nlme"` `""` skip recompute keep nlme's standard errors.  recompute fails `"nlme"` covariance kept. ... , named control arguments passed    nlminb (apart trace iter.max    mentioned ), used (eval.max    abs.tol ).
