title: Manually add time to a nlmixr2 object — nlmixrAddTiming
code: # \donttest{  one.cmt <- function() {  ini({    ## You may label each parameter with a comment    tka <- 0.45 # Ka    tcl <- log(c(0, 2.7, 100)) # Log Cl    ## This works with interactive models    ## You may also label the preceding line with label("label text")    tv <- 3.45; label("log V")    ## the label("Label name") works with all models    eta.ka ~ 0.6    eta.cl ~ 0.3    eta.v ~ 0.1    add.sd <- 0.7  })  model({    ka <- exp(tka + eta.ka)    cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)    v <- exp(tv + eta.v)    linCmt() ~ add(add.sd)  }) }  fit <- nlmixr(one.cmt, theo_sd, est="saem") #>   #>   #>   #>   #> ℹ parameter labels from comments are typically ignored in non-interactive mode #> ℹ Need to run with the source intact to parse comments #>   #>   #> → loading into symengine environment... #> → pruning branches (`if`/`else`) of saem model... #> ✔ done #> → finding duplicate expressions in saem model... #> ✔ done #> ℹ calculate uninformed etas #> ℹ done #> covMethod="sa" could not be computed; using the linearized FIM #> Calculating covariance matrix #> → loading into symengine environment... #> → pruning branches (`if`/`else`) of saem model... #> ✔ done #> → finding duplicate expressions in saem predOnly model 0... #> → finding duplicate expressions in saem predOnly model 1... #> → finding duplicate expressions in saem predOnly model 2... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in saem predOnly model 2... #> ✔ done #>   #>   #> → Calculating residuals/tables #> ✔ done #> → compress origData in nlmixr2 object, save 6584 #> → compress parHistData in nlmixr2 object, save 8592 #> → compress phiM in nlmixr2 object, save 446912  # will add to the current setup nlmixrAddTiming(fit, "setup", 3)  # Add a new item to the timing dataframe nlmixrAddTiming(fit, "new", 3)  # }

code: # \donttest{  one.cmt <- function() {  ini({    ## You may label each parameter with a comment    tka <- 0.45 # Ka    tcl <- log(c(0, 2.7, 100)) # Log Cl    ## This works with interactive models    ## You may also label the preceding line with label("label text")    tv <- 3.45; label("log V")    ## the label("Label name") works with all models    eta.ka ~ 0.6    eta.cl ~ 0.3    eta.v ~ 0.1    add.sd <- 0.7  })  model({    ka <- exp(tka + eta.ka)    cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)    v <- exp(tv + eta.v)    linCmt() ~ add(add.sd)  }) } fit <- nlmixr(one.cmt, theo_sd, est="saem") #>   #>   #>   #>   #> ℹ parameter labels from comments are typically ignored in non-interactive mode #> ℹ Need to run with the source intact to parse comments #>   #>   #> → loading into symengine environment... #> → pruning branches (`if`/`else`) of saem model... #> ✔ done #> → finding duplicate expressions in saem model... #> ✔ done #> ℹ calculate uninformed etas #> ℹ done #> covMethod="sa" could not be computed; using the linearized FIM #> Calculating covariance matrix #> → loading into symengine environment... #> → pruning branches (`if`/`else`) of saem model... #> ✔ done #> → finding duplicate expressions in saem predOnly model 0... #> → finding duplicate expressions in saem predOnly model 1... #> → finding duplicate expressions in saem predOnly model 2... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in saem predOnly model 2... #> ✔ done #>   #>   #> → Calculating residuals/tables #> ✔ done #> → compress origData in nlmixr2 object, save 6584 #> → compress parHistData in nlmixr2 object, save 8592 #> → compress phiM in nlmixr2 object, save 446912  nlmixrWithTiming("time1", {    Sys.sleep(1)    # note this can be nested, time1 will exclude the timing from time2    nlmixrWithTiming("time2", {       Sys.sleep(1)    }, envir=fit) }, envir=fit)  print(fit) #> ── nlmixr² SAEM OBJF by FOCEi approximation ── #>  #>  Gaussian/Laplacian Likelihoods: AIC() or $objf etc.  #>  FOCEi CWRES & Likelihoods: addCwres()  #>  #> ── Time (sec $time): ── #>  #>             setup   optimize covariance preprocess configure  saem postprocess #> elapsed 0.1043944 2.7621e-05 0.01100513      0.069      0.28 7.074       0.448 #>         table compress     other time2 time1 #> elapsed 0.048    0.136 0.1445729 1.002 1.002 #>  #> ── Population Parameters ($parFixed or $parFixedDf): ── #>  #>        Parameter  Est.     SE %RSE Back-transformed(95%CI) BSV(CV%) Shrink(SD)% #> tka              0.454  0.192 42.3       1.57 (1.08, 2.29)     69.6      -1.53  #> tcl               1.01 0.0851 8.40       2.75 (2.33, 3.25)     27.5       3.98  #> tv         log V  3.45 0.0451 1.31       31.6 (28.9, 34.5)     13.2       9.72  #> add.sd           0.700      0    0    0.700 (0.700, 0.700)                      #>   #>   Covariance Type ($covMethod): linFim #>   No correlations in between subject variability (BSV) matrix #>   Full BSV covariance ($omega) or correlation ($omegaR; diagonals=SDs)  #>   Distribution stats (mean/skewness/kurtosis/p-value) available in $shrink  #>   Censoring ($censInformation): No censoring #>  #> ── Fit Data (object is a modified tibble): ── #> # A tibble: 132 × 18 #>   ID     TIME    DV  PRED    RES IPRED    IRES   IWRES eta.ka eta.cl   eta.v #>   <fct> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1 1      0     0.74  0     0.74   0     0.74    1.06    0.107 -0.484 -0.0782 #> 2 1      0.25  2.84  3.21 -0.371  3.82 -0.977  -1.40    0.107 -0.484 -0.0782 #> 3 1      0.57  6.57  5.62  0.946  6.62 -0.0514 -0.0734  0.107 -0.484 -0.0782 #> # ℹ 129 more rows #> # ℹ 7 more variables: depot <dbl>, central <dbl>, ka <dbl>, cl <dbl>, v <dbl>, #> #   tad <dbl>, dosenum <dbl>  # }

text: maxiter positive integer specifying maximum number     iterations allowed. tol positive numeric value specifying tolerance level     relative offset convergence criterion. minFactor positive numeric value specifying minimum     step-size factor allowed step iteration.      increment calculated Gauss-Newton algorithm     successively halved residual sum squares     decreased step-size factor reduced     limit. printEval logical specifying whether number evaluations     (steps gradient direction taken iteration) printed. warnOnly logical specifying whether nls()     return instead signalling error case termination     convergence.     Termination convergence happens upon completion maxiter     iterations, case singular gradient, case     step-size factor reduced minFactor. scaleOffset constant added denominator relative     offset convergence criterion calculation avoid zero divide case     fit model data close.  default value     0 keeps legacy behaviour nls().  value     1 seems work problems reasonable scale small     residuals. nDcentral numerical derivatives used:     logical indicating central differences     employed, .e., numericDeriv(*, central=TRUE)     used. algorithm character string specifying algorithm use.     default algorithm Gauss-Newton algorithm.  possible     values "plinear" Golub-Pereyra algorithm     partially linear least-squares models "port"     ‘nl2sol’ algorithm Port library – see     references.  Can abbreviated. ftol non-negative numeric. Termination occurs       actual predicted relative reductions sum       squares ftol. Therefore, ftol measures       relative error desired sum squares. ptol non-negative numeric. Termination occurs       relative error two consecutive iterates       ptol. Therefore, ptol measures relative error       desired approximate solution. gtol non-negative numeric. Termination occurs       cosine angle result fn evaluation       \(fvec\) column Jacobian gtol       absolute value. Therefore, gtol measures       orthogonality desired function vector       columns Jacobian. diag list numeric vector containing positive       entries serve multiplicative scale factors       parameters. Length diag equal       par. , user-provided diag ignored       diag internally set. epsfcn (used jac provided)       numeric used determining suitable step       forward-difference approximation. approximation assumes       relative errors functions order       epsfcn. epsfcn less machine       precision, assumed relative errors       functions order machine precision. factor positive numeric, used determining       initial step bound.  bound set product       factor \(|\code{diag}*\code{par}|\) nonzero,       else factor . cases factor       lie interval (0.1,100). 100 generally       recommended value. maxfev integer; termination occurs       number calls fn reached maxfev.       Note nls.lm sets value maxfev       100*(length(par) + 1)       maxfev = integer(), par list       vector parameters optimized. nprint integer; set nprint positive       enable printing iterates solveType controls whether `nlm` uses nlmixr2's analytical gradients (event-related parameters like lag time/duration/rate/F use Shi2021 finite differences instead): `"hessian"` builds Hessian analytical gradient via finite differences, `"gradient"` supplies gradient lets `nlm` compute finite-difference Hessian, `"fun"` lets `nlm` compute finite differences. stickyRecalcN number bad ODE solves reducing atol/rtol rest problem. maxOdeRecalc Maximum number times reduce ODE tolerances try resolve system bad ODE solve. odeRecalcFactor ODE recalculation factor ODE solving goes bad, factor rtol/atol reduced indTolRelax `TRUE` (default), subjects whose ODE solve produced NaN/Inf tolerances relaxed, relaxed tolerance persists across optimizer calls (sticky). `FALSE`, subjects tolerances relaxed retry tolerances reset afterward. eventType Event gradient type dosing events; Can "central" "forward" shiErr represents epsilon optimizing ideal step size numeric differentiation using Shi2021 method shi21maxFD maximum number steps optimization forward difference step size using dosing events (lag time, modeled duration/rate bioavailability) useColor Logical (`NULL`) emit ANSI bold/color escapes iteration print. `NULL` (default) defers [crayon::has_color()]. printNcol Integer (`NULL`) parameter columns per row wrapping. `NULL` (default) uses `floor((getOption("width") - 23) / 12)`. print Either scalar print-frequency (`0` = suppress, `1` (default) = every evaluation, `N` = every Nth), pre-built [iterPrintControl()] object. Equivalent `iterPrintControl(every = print, ncol = printNcol, useColor = useColor)`. normType Parameter normalization/scaling used get scaled initial values scaleType, form Vscaled = (Vunscaled-C1)/C2 (see Feature Scaling; rescale2 follows OptdesX manual): "rescale2" scales parameters (-1, 1); "rescale" (min-max) scales (0, 1); "mean" centers mean range (0, 1); "std" standardizes mean/sd; "len" scales unit (Euclidean) length; "constant" performs normalization (C1=0, C2=1). scaleType scaling scheme nlmixr2: "nlmixr2" (default) scales (current-init)*scaleC[] + scaleTo, scaleTo normType scales scaleC; "norm" uses simple scaling normType; "mult" scales multiplicatively current/init*scaleTo; "multAdd" scales linearly ((current-init)+scaleTo) parameters exponential block (e.g. exp(theta)) multiplicatively otherwise. scaleCmax Maximum value scaleC prevent overflow. scaleCmin Minimum value scaleC prevent underflow. scaleC Scaling constant used scaleType="nlmixr2"; specified, chosen parameter type keep gradient sizes similar log scale: `1` exp()-transformed/power/boxCox/ yeoJohnson parameters, `0.5*abs(est)` additive/proportional/ lognormal error parameters, `abs(1/digamma(est+1))` factorials, `log(abs(est))*abs(est)` log-scale parameters. May set explicitly per parameter defaults apply well. scaleTo Scale initial parameter estimate value. default 1.  zero , scaling performed. gradTo factor gradient scaled optimizing.  works scaleType="nlmixr2". trace logical value indicating trace iteration     progress printed.  Default FALSE.      TRUE residual (weighted) sum--squares, convergence     criterion parameter values printed conclusion     iteration.  Note format() used,     mostly depend getOption("digits").     "plinear" algorithm used, conditional     estimates linear parameters printed nonlinear     parameters.  "port" algorithm used     objective function value printed half residual (weighted)     sum--squares. rxControl `rxode2` ODE solving options fitting, created `rxControl()` optExpression Optimize rxode2 expression speed calculation. default turned . sumProd boolean indicating model change multiplication high precision multiplication sums high precision sums using PreciseSums package.  default FALSE. literalFix boolean, substitute fixed population values literals re-adjust ui parameter estimates optimization; Default `TRUE`. returnNls logical; TRUE, return nls object instead nlmixr object addProp Type additive-plus-proportional error: `"combined1"`, standard deviations add: $$y = f + (+ b\times f^c) \times \varepsilon$$; `"combined2"`, variances add: $$y = f + \sqrt{^2 + b^2\times f^{2\times c}} \times \varepsilon$$. y = observed, f = predicted, = additive sd, b = proportional/power sd, c = power exponent (1 proportional case). eventSens Controls dosing/event-parameter (`alag`, `F`, `rate`, `dur`) sensitivities computed THETA/ETA gradients: `"jump"` (default) uses rxode2's analytic event sensitivities; `"fd"` uses legacy finite-difference behavior. calcTables boolean determine foceiFit calculate tables. default TRUE compress object compressed items adjObf boolean indicate objective function adjusted closer NONMEM's default objective function.  default TRUE ci Confidence level tables.  default 0.95 95% confidence. sigdig Optimization significant digits; controls inner/outer optimization tolerance (10^-sigdig), boundary check tolerance (5*10^(-sigdig+1)), ODE solver tolerances.  solver tolerances split solver stiffness keep atol well rtol: stiff solver (lsoda/liblsoda – auto-switching method – plus indLin, default) uses rtol = 10^(-sigdig-3), atol = 10^(-sigdig-5), non-stiff explicit dop853 uses looser rtol = 10^-sigdig, atol = 10^(-sigdig-3).  sensitivity (atolSens/rtolSens) steady-state (ssAtol/ssRtol) tolerances run one order looser corresponding main tolerance.  default sigdig = 4 stiff solve atol = 1e-9, rtol = 1e-7. sigdigTable Significant digits final output table. specified, matches significant digits `sigdig` optimization algorithm.  `sigdig` NULL, use 3. boundedTransform `TRUE` (default), bounded parameters transformed unbounded optimization methods back-transformed final estimates. `FALSE` optimizes original scale bounds passed optimizer. `NA` transforms optimization skips final back-transform. ... Additional optional arguments.  None used present.

title: nlmixr2 defaults controls for nls — nlsControl
code: # \donttest{  one.cmt <- function() {   ini({    tka <- 0.45    tcl <- log(c(0, 2.7, 100))    tv <- 3.45    add.sd <- 0.7  })  model({    ka <- exp(tka)    cl <- exp(tcl)    v <- exp(tv)    linCmt() ~ add(add.sd)  }) }  # Uses nlsLM from minpack.lm if available  fit1 <- nlmixr(one.cmt, nlmixr2data::theo_sd, est="nls", nlsControl(algorithm="LM")) #>   #>   #>   #>   #> ℹ parameter labels from comments are typically ignored in non-interactive mode #> ℹ Need to run with the source intact to parse comments #> → loading into symengine environment... #> → pruning branches (`if`/`else`) of nls model... #> ✔ done #> → calculate ∂(f)/∂(θ) #> → finding duplicate expressions in nls gradient... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in nls gradient... #> → finding duplicate expressions in nls pred-only... #>   #>   #>   #>   #> → calculating covariance #> ✔ done #> → loading into symengine environment... #> → pruning branches (`if`/`else`) of full model... #> ✔ done #> → finding duplicate expressions in EBE model... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in EBE model... #> → compiling EBE model... #>   #>   #> ✔ done #> → Calculating residuals/tables #> ✔ done #> → compress origData in nlmixr2 object, save 6584 #> → compress parHistData in nlmixr2 object, save 2280  # Uses port and respect parameter boundaries fit2 <- nlmixr(one.cmt, nlmixr2data::theo_sd, est="nls", nlsControl(algorithm="port")) #>   #>   #>   #>   #> ℹ parameter labels from comments are typically ignored in non-interactive mode #> ℹ Need to run with the source intact to parse comments #> → loading into symengine environment... #> → pruning branches (`if`/`else`) of nls model... #> ✔ done #> → calculate ∂(f)/∂(θ) #> → finding duplicate expressions in nls gradient... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in nls gradient... #> → finding duplicate expressions in nls pred-only... #>   #>   #>   #>   #> → loading into symengine environment... #> → pruning branches (`if`/`else`) of full model... #> ✔ done #> → finding duplicate expressions in EBE model... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in EBE model... #> → compiling EBE model... #>   #>   #> ✔ done #> → Calculating residuals/tables #> ✔ done #> → compress origData in nlmixr2 object, save 6584 #> → compress parHistData in nlmixr2 object, save 2576  # You can access the underlying nls object with `$nls` fit2$nls #> Nonlinear regression model #>   model: 0 ~ nlmixr2est::.nlmixrNlsFunValGrad(DV, tka, rxBoundedTr.tcl,     tv) #>    data: nlmixr2est::.nlmixrNlsData() #>             tka rxBoundedTr.tcl              tv  #>         -1.0097         -0.4351          1.0423  #>  residual sum-of-squares: 249.7 #>  #> Algorithm "port", convergence message: relative convergence (4)  # }

title: C++ implementation of Matrix's nearPD — nmNearPD
text: `ensureSymmetry` makes sure symmetric applying 0.5*(t(x) + x) using nmNearPD

title: C++ implementation of Matrix's nearPD — nmNearPD
code: nmNearPD(   x,   keepDiag = FALSE,   do2eigen = TRUE,   doDykstra = TRUE,   only.values = FALSE,   ensureSymmetry = !isSymmetric(x),   eig.tol = 1e-06,   conv.tol = 1e-07,   posd.tol = 1e-08,   maxit = 100L,   trace = FALSE )

title: C++ implementation of Matrix's nearPD — nmNearPD
text: x numeric \(n \times n\) approximately positive     definite matrix, typically approximation correlation     covariance matrix.  x symmetric (    ensureSymmetry false), symmpart(x) used. keepDiag logical, generalizing corr: TRUE,     resulting matrix diagonal     (diag(x)) input matrix. do2eigen logical indicating     posdefify() eigen step applied     result Higham algorithm. doDykstra logical indicating Dykstra's correction     used; true default.  false, algorithm basically     direct fixpoint iteration     \(Y_k = P_U(P_S(Y_{k-1}))\). .values logical; TRUE, result just     vector eigenvalues approximating matrix. ensureSymmetry logical; symmetrizes `x` via symmpart unless already symmetric. Beware: setting FALSE asymmetric input typically nonsense. eig.tol defines relative positiveness eigenvalues compared     largest one, \(\lambda_1\). Eigenvalues \(\lambda_k\)     treated zero \(\lambda_k / \lambda_1 \le eig.tol\). conv.tol convergence tolerance Higham algorithm. posd.tol tolerance enforcing positive definiteness (    final posdefify step do2eigen TRUE). maxit maximum number iterations allowed. trace logical integer specifying convergence monitoring     traced.

title: C++ implementation of Matrix's nearPD — nmNearPD
text: unlike matrix package, simply returns nearest   positive definite matrix

title: C++ implementation of Matrix's nearPD — nmNearPD
text: implements algorithm Higham (2002), (  do2eigen true) forces positive definiteness using code   posdefify.  algorithm Knol ten   Berge (1989) (implemented ) general   allows constraints (1) fix rows (columns) matrix   (2) force smallest eigenvalue certain value. Note setting corr = TRUE just sets diag(.) <- 1   within algorithm. Higham (2002) uses Dykstra's correction, version Jens   Oehlschlägel use (accidentally),   still gave reasonable results; simplification, now   used doDykstra = FALSE,   active nearPD() Matrix version 0.999375-40.

title: C++ implementation of Matrix's nearPD — nmNearPD
text: Cheng, Sheung Hun Higham, Nick (1998)   Modified Cholesky Algorithm Based Symmetric Indefinite Factorization;   SIAM J. Matrix Anal.\ Appl., 19, 1097–1110. Knol DL, ten Berge JMF (1989)   Least-squares approximation improper correlation matrix   proper one.   Psychometrika 54, 53–61. Higham, Nick (2002)   Computing nearest correlation matrix - problem finance;   IMA Journal Numerical Analysis 22, 329–343.

title: C++ implementation of Matrix's nearPD — nmNearPD
text: Jens Oehlschlägel donated first version.   Subsequent changes Matrix package authors.

what: Control for the npag (nonparametric adaptive grid) method — npagControl

text: wrapper around [impmapControl()] reuses shared FOCEI family plumbing nonparametric adaptive grid engine.  nonparametric support-point knobs added later milestone.

title: Control for the npag (nonparametric adaptive grid) method — npagControl
code: npagControl(   points = NULL,   cycles = 100L,   gammaOptimize = TRUE,   residOptimize = c("alternate", "final", "none"),   muExpand = FALSE,   gridWidth = 4,   gridBounds = c("auto", "ini", "both"),   dfScan = -1L,   cores = NULL,   ... )

title: Control for the npag (nonparametric adaptive grid) method — npagControl
text: points Initial Sobol grid size (support points).  `NULL` (default) picks automatically number support-point dimensions (etas): `max(2028, 512 * n_eta)` – fixed grid (Pmetrics uses 2028) covers low-dimensional model grows sparse can collapse high dimensions, auto size floors 2028 scales per added eta.  Supply integer override. cycles Maximum adaptive-grid cycles. gammaOptimize Use global assay-error multiplier (gamma) per-cycle warm start overall residual magnitude, folded variance-scale coefficients (`add`/`prop`/`lnorm`).  per-endpoint values, add/prop ratio, transform/autocorrelation parameters come residOptimize.  valid normal endpoints; censoring transform--sides supported. residOptimize estimate residual-error thetas (every endpoint's `add`/`prop`/`lnorm`, transform `lambda`, `ar`) support points weights held fixed, using bounded minqa::bobyqa EXTENDED LEAST SQUARES objective `sum_obs((f-dv)^2/r + log(r))` posterior-mean etas.  `log(r)` term keeps residual drifting zero flexible support (marginal likelihood reward), giving saem/focei residual; variance scale warm-started per-endpoint moment (additive SD `sqrt(mean(err^2))`, proportional `sqrt(mean((err/f)^2))`, transform- -sides scale).  "alternate" (default) optimizes every cycle (block- coordinate ascent); "final" optimizes converged support; "none" holds initial values.  Fixed residual parameters always held.  residual thetas converge, final adaptive-grid pass re-optimizes support held constant support remains nonparametric MLE (D(F) ~ 0) fitted residual. muExpand estimate non-mu structural fixed-effect parameters (theta eta, e.g. `ke <- exp(tke)`; npag's grid otherwise covers mu-referenced residual/likelihood parameters).  `FALSE` (default) optimizes directly "regressors" residual step – `bobyqa` moves alongside residual parameters, re-deriving posterior-mean etas candidate (eta grid stale-absorb structural shift) – identifies well (e.g. recovering clearance poor start).  available mix() models (ELS step mixture-aware; component parameters held).  `TRUE` instead uses saem-style mu-expansion: inject pseudo-eta (`ke <- exp(tke + eta.tke)`), grid-estimate, recover fixed effect finalization (support-mean folded theta, injected random effect collapsed).  regressor default usually identifies parameters sharply grid. gridWidth support-point box half-width, initial-eta SDs, `gridBounds="auto"` grid (default 4).  narrower box focuses initial Sobol grid plausible region – useful high-dimensional models wide box wastes points near-zero-density support (can collapse fit). gridBounds set initial support-point box: `"auto"` (default) uses `+/- gridWidth * initial eta SD`; `"ini"` uses mu-referenced parameter's ini-block lower/upper bounds finite (else auto); `""` uses ini bounds present auto otherwise.  high-dimensional model, bounded ini estimates + `"ini"` keep grid range. dfScan Size Sobol scan used D(F) global-optimality certificate: `-1` (default) auto-sizes `max(2048, 2 * points)`, `0` skips certificate (`npagDF` `NA`), positive value sets explicit scan size.  scan affect fit, reported certificate; smaller scan faster. cores Number threads used parallel per-subject conditional- likelihood solves.  `NULL` (default) uses current `rxode2` thread count (`rxode2::getRxThreads()`); integer sets thread count fit (restored afterwards).  Results independent thread count. ... Parameters passed [impmapControl()].

title: Control for the npag (nonparametric adaptive grid) method — npagControl
text: `impmapControl` object tagged npag engine.

title: Control for the npag (nonparametric adaptive grid) method — npagControl
text: Note: npag objective nonparametric marginal log-likelihood uses different constant convention NONMEM/FOCEI, `-2LL` comparable nlmixr2's FOCEI/SAEM/FOCE `-2LL`.  Compare npag runs Pmetrics NPAG. Note residual error flexible support distribution: residual parameters estimated nonparametric objective (see residOptimize) support-point distribution held fixed.  distribution flexible, can absorb variability parametric model (FOCEI/SAEM) attribute residual error – especially additive term combined additive+proportional model low concentrations.  result additive coefficient combined error model may estimated smaller (sometimes toward zero) corresponding parametric fit, proportional term per-endpoint magnitudes recovered well.  expected property nonparametric estimation, convergence failure; use residOptimize = "none" hold residual parameters initial values fixed error model desired.

what: Run the NPAG adaptive-grid cycle on a set-up inner problem — npagCycle_
title: Run the NPAG adaptive-grid cycle on a set-up inner problem — npagCycle_
text: Requires FOCEi inner problem set (.npInnerSetup).  Runs full Yamada adaptive-grid cycle (Sobol grid -> Psi -> Burke IPM -> condensation -> expansion -> convergence) returns discrete mixing distribution.  Exposed testing ahead full fit-object wiring.

title: Run the NPAG adaptive-grid cycle on a set-up inner problem — npagCycle_
code: npagCycle_(   lower,   upper,   points = 2028L,   cycles = 100L,   cores = 1L,   gammaOptimize = FALSE )

title: Run the NPAG adaptive-grid cycle on a set-up inner problem — npagCycle_
text: lower, upper Numeric vectors, per-eta support-point box. points Initial Sobol grid size. cycles Maximum cycles. cores OpenMP threads. gammaOptimize Optimize residual-error magnitude (gamma) cycle (valid uncensored normal endpoints).

title: Run the NPAG adaptive-grid cycle on a set-up inner problem — npagCycle_
text: list support (support points, eta space; one per row),   weights, objf (log-likelihood), gamma, cycles,   converged.

what: Control for the npb (nonparametric Bayes) method — npbControl
title: Control for the npb (nonparametric Bayes) method — npbControl
text: wrapper around [impmapControl()] reuses shared FOCEI family plumbing nonparametric Bayes engine.  stick-breaking sampler knobs added later milestone.

title: Control for the npb (nonparametric Bayes) method — npbControl
code: npbControl(   points = 50L,   alpha = 1,   burnin = 500L,   nsamp = 500L,   nchains = 1L,   propSd = 0.2,   seed = 42L,   residOptimize = c("alternate", "final", "none"),   cycles = 100L,   gammaOptimize = FALSE,   muExpand = FALSE,   cores = NULL,   ... )

title: Control for the npb (nonparametric Bayes) method — npbControl
text: points Stick-breaking truncation level K (number support points). alpha Dirichlet-process concentration parameter. burnin Number burn-Gibbs sweeps. nsamp Number post-burn-Gibbs samples collected. nchains Number independent chains (Gelman-Rubin R-hat convergence reported nchains > 1). propSd Standard deviation Gaussian random-walk MH proposal support-point locations (eta space). seed Random seed sampler. residOptimize estimate residual-error thetas (every endpoint's `add`/`prop`/`lnorm`, transform `lambda`, `ar`) non-mu structural "regressor" theta, sampled mixing distribution held fixed, using bounded `bobyqa` EXTENDED LEAST SQUARES objective (see [npagControl()]; `log(r)` term keeps residual collapsing zero moment warm-start gives saem-style SD).  "alternate" (default) re-fits burn-holds fixed sampling phase (every collected draw shares converged residual scale); "final" holds initial values sampling fits converged draw; "none" holds initial values throughout.  Fixed residual parameters always held.  Unlike npag, npb optimize assay-error multiplier (gamma); residual thetas fit directly. cycles Unused npb (kept control compatibility). gammaOptimize Unused npb (kept control compatibility). muExpand `TRUE`, mu-expand non-mu structural fixed-effect thetas (theta eta) grid-estimable pseudo-etas fit; `FALSE` (default) leaves residual step. cores Number threads used parallel per-subject conditional- likelihood solves Gibbs sweeps.  `NULL` (default) uses current `rxode2` thread count (`rxode2::getRxThreads()`); integer sets thread count fit (restored afterwards).  fixed `seed` fit bit--bit identical regardless thread count. ... Parameters passed [impmapControl()].

title: Control for the npb (nonparametric Bayes) method — npbControl
text: `impmapControl` object tagged npb engine.

title: Control for the npb (nonparametric Bayes) method — npbControl
text: Note: npb objective nonparametric marginal log-likelihood uses different constant convention NONMEM/FOCEI, `-2LL` comparable nlmixr2's FOCEI/SAEM/FOCE `-2LL`.  Compare npb runs Pmetrics NPAG.

title: Control for the npb (nonparametric Bayes) method — npbControl
text: Matthew L. Fidler

title: nlmixr2 optim defaults — optimControl
text: method method used. See ‘Details’.  Can abbreviated. trace Non-negative integer. positive, tracing information progress optimization produced. Higher values may produce tracing information: method `"L-BFGS-B"`, six levels tracing. See `optim()` information fnscale overall scaling applied value `fn` `gr` optimization. negative, turns problem maximization problem. Optimization performed `fn(par)/fnscale` parscale vector scaling values parameters. Optimization performed `par/parscale` comparable sense unit change element produces unit change scaled value.  used (needed) `method = "Brent"` ndeps vector step sizes finite-difference approximation gradient, `par/parscale` scale.  Defaults `1e-3` maxit maximum number iterations. Defaults `100` derivative-based methods, `500` `"Nelder-Mead"`. abstol absolute convergence tolerance. useful non-negative functions, tolerance reaching zero. reltol Relative convergence tolerance.  algorithm stops unable reduce value factor `reltol * (abs(val) + reltol)` step alpha Reflection factor `"Nelder-Mead"` method. beta Contraction factor `"Nelder-Mead"` method gamma Expansion  factor `"Nelder-Mead"` method REPORT frequency reports `"BFGS"`, `"L-BFGS-B"` `"SANN"` methods `control$trace` positive. Defaults every 10 iterations `"BFGS"` `"L-BFGS-B"`, every 100 temperatures `"SANN"` warn.1d.NelderMead logical indicating (default) `"Nelder-Mead"` method signal warning used one-dimensional minimization.  warning sometimes inappropriate, can suppress setting option `FALSE` type conjugate-gradients method.  Takes value `1` Fletcher-Reeves update, `2` Polak-Ribiere `3` Beale-Sorenson. lmm integer giving number BFGS updates retained `"L-BFGS-B"` method, defaults `5` factr controls convergence `"L-BFGS-B"` method. Convergence occurs reduction objective within factor machine tolerance. Default `1e7`, tolerance `1e-8`. pgtol helps control convergence `"L-BFGS-B"` method.  tolerance projected gradient current search direction. defaults zero, check suppressed temp controls `"SANN"` method. starting temperature cooling schedule. Defaults `10`. tmax number function evaluations temperature `"SANN"` method. Defaults `10`. stickyRecalcN number bad ODE solves reducing atol/rtol rest problem. maxOdeRecalc Maximum number times reduce ODE tolerances try resolve system bad ODE solve. odeRecalcFactor ODE recalculation factor ODE solving goes bad, factor rtol/atol reduced indTolRelax `TRUE` (default), subjects whose ODE solve produced NaN/Inf tolerances relaxed, relaxed tolerance persists across optimizer calls (sticky). `FALSE`, subjects tolerances relaxed retry tolerances reset afterward. eventType Event gradient type dosing events; Can "central" "forward" shiErr represents epsilon optimizing ideal step size numeric differentiation using Shi2021 method shi21maxFD maximum number steps optimization forward difference step size using dosing events (lag time, modeled duration/rate bioavailability) solveType controls whether `optim` uses nlmixr2's analytical gradients (event-related parameters like lag time/duration/rate/F use Shi2021 finite differences instead). `"gradient"` supplies gradient lets `optim` compute finite-difference Hessian; `"fun"` lets `optim` compute finite differences. applies gradient-based methods: "BFGS", "CG", "L-BFGS-B". useColor Logical (`NULL`) emit ANSI bold/color escapes iteration print. `NULL` (default) defers [crayon::has_color()]. printNcol Integer (`NULL`) parameter columns per row wrapping. `NULL` (default) uses `floor((getOption("width") - 23) / 12)`. print Either scalar print-frequency (`0` = suppress, `1` (default) = every evaluation, `N` = every Nth), pre-built [iterPrintControl()] object. Equivalent `iterPrintControl(every = print, ncol = printNcol, useColor = useColor)`. normType Parameter normalization/scaling used get scaled initial values scaleType, form Vscaled = (Vunscaled-C1)/C2 (see Feature Scaling; rescale2 follows OptdesX manual): "rescale2" scales parameters (-1, 1); "rescale" (min-max) scales (0, 1); "mean" centers mean range (0, 1); "std" standardizes mean/sd; "len" scales unit (Euclidean) length; "constant" performs normalization (C1=0, C2=1). scaleType scaling scheme nlmixr2: "nlmixr2" (default) scales (current-init)*scaleC[] + scaleTo, scaleTo normType scales scaleC; "norm" uses simple scaling normType; "mult" scales multiplicatively current/init*scaleTo; "multAdd" scales linearly ((current-init)+scaleTo) parameters exponential block (e.g. exp(theta)) multiplicatively otherwise. scaleCmax Maximum value scaleC prevent overflow. scaleCmin Minimum value scaleC prevent underflow. scaleC Scaling constant used scaleType="nlmixr2"; specified, chosen parameter type keep gradient sizes similar log scale: `1` exp()-transformed/power/boxCox/ yeoJohnson parameters, `0.5*abs(est)` additive/proportional/ lognormal error parameters, `abs(1/digamma(est+1))` factorials, `log(abs(est))*abs(est)` log-scale parameters. May set explicitly per parameter defaults apply well. scaleTo Scale initial parameter estimate value. default 1.  zero , scaling performed. gradTo factor gradient scaled optimizing.  works scaleType="nlmixr2". rxControl `rxode2` ODE solving options fitting, created `rxControl()` optExpression Optimize rxode2 expression speed calculation. default turned . sumProd boolean indicating model change multiplication high precision multiplication sums high precision sums using PreciseSums package.  default FALSE. literalFix boolean, substitute fixed population values literals re-adjust ui parameter estimates optimization; Default `TRUE`. literalFixRes boolean, substitute fixed population values literals re-adjust ui parameter estimates optimization; Default `TRUE`. returnOptim logical; TRUE return optim list instead nlmixr2 fit object addProp Type additive-plus-proportional error: `"combined1"`, standard deviations add: $$y = f + (+ b\times f^c) \times \varepsilon$$; `"combined2"`, variances add: $$y = f + \sqrt{^2 + b^2\times f^{2\times c}} \times \varepsilon$$. y = observed, f = predicted, = additive sd, b = proportional/power sd, c = power exponent (1 proportional case). eventSens Controls dosing/event-parameter (`alag`, `F`, `rate`, `dur`) sensitivities computed THETA/ETA gradients: `"jump"` (default) uses rxode2's analytic event sensitivities; `"fd"` uses legacy finite-difference behavior. sensMethod Method used compute ODE parameter sensitivities: `"default"` (default) defers global option `getOption("nlmixr2est.adjoint")` (`"forward"` default); `"forward"` uses classic variational (forward) sensitivity ODEs; `"adjoint"` uses -engine discrete adjoint matching adjoint (`s`) method. calcTables boolean determine foceiFit calculate tables. default TRUE compress object compressed items covMethod allows selection "r", uses nlmixr2's `nlmixr2Hess()` hessian calculation "optim" uses hessian `stats::optim(.., hessian=TRUE)` adjObf boolean indicate objective function adjusted closer NONMEM's default objective function.  default TRUE ci Confidence level tables.  default 0.95 95% confidence. sigdig Optimization significant digits; controls inner/outer optimization tolerance (10^-sigdig), boundary check tolerance (5*10^(-sigdig+1)), ODE solver tolerances.  solver tolerances split solver stiffness keep atol well rtol: stiff solver (lsoda/liblsoda – auto-switching method – plus indLin, default) uses rtol = 10^(-sigdig-3), atol = 10^(-sigdig-5), non-stiff explicit dop853 uses looser rtol = 10^-sigdig, atol = 10^(-sigdig-3).  sensitivity (atolSens/rtolSens) steady-state (ssAtol/ssRtol) tolerances run one order looser corresponding main tolerance.  default sigdig = 4 stiff solve atol = 1e-9, rtol = 1e-7. sigdigTable Significant digits final output table. specified, matches significant digits `sigdig` optimization algorithm.  `sigdig` NULL, use 3. boundedTransform `TRUE` (default), bounded parameters transformed unbounded optimization methods back-transformed final estimates. `FALSE` optimizes original scale bounds passed optimizer. `NA` transforms optimization skips final back-transform. ... arguments passed fn gr.

code: # \donttest{ # A logit regression example with emax model  dsn <- data.frame(i=1:1000) dsn$time <- exp(rnorm(1000)) dsn$DV=rbinom(1000,1,exp(-1+dsn$time)/(1+exp(-1+dsn$time)))  mod <- function() {  ini({    E0 <- 0.5    Em <- 0.5    E50 <- 2    g <- fix(2)  })  model({    v <- E0+Em*time^g/(E50^g+time^g)    ll(bin) ~ DV * v - log(1 + exp(v))  }) }  fit2 <- nlmixr(mod, dsn, est="optim", optimControl(method="BFGS")) #>   #>   #>   #>   #> ℹ parameter labels from comments are typically ignored in non-interactive mode #> ℹ Need to run with the source intact to parse comments #> → loading into symengine environment... #> → pruning branches (`if`/`else`) of population log-likelihood model... #> ✔ done #> → calculate ∂(f)/∂(θ) #> → finding duplicate expressions in nlm llik gradient... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in nlm llik gradient... #> → finding duplicate expressions in nlm pred-only... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in nlm pred-only... #>   #>   #>   #>   #> → calculating covariance #> ✔ done #> → loading into symengine environment... #> → pruning branches (`if`/`else`) of full model... #> ✔ done #> → finding duplicate expressions in EBE model... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in EBE model... #> → compiling EBE model... #>   #>   #> ✔ done #> → Calculating residuals/tables #> ✔ done fit2 #> ── nlmixr² log-likelihood optim with BFGS method ── #>  #>           OBJF      AIC      BIC Log-likelihood Condition#(Cov) Condition#(Cor) #> lPop -684.2964 1159.581 1174.304      -576.7903        56372.43        479.4776 #>  #> ── Time (sec value$time): ── #>  #>             setup  optimize covariance preprocess postprocess table compress #> elapsed 0.3350544 0.5232404   6.58e-06      0.047       0.006 0.025    0.001 #>             other #> elapsed 0.1156987 #>  #> ── (value$parFixed or value$parFixedDf): ── #>  #>        Est.     SE  %RSE   Back-transformed(95%CI) #> E0  -0.2947 0.2131 72.33 -0.2947 (-0.7124, 0.1231) #> Em    17.22  31.65 183.8     17.22 (-44.81, 79.26) #> E50   7.688  8.845 115.1     7.688 (-9.649, 25.02) #> g     2.000  FIXED FIXED                     2.000 #>   #>   Covariance Type (value$covMethod): r (optim) #>   Censoring (value$censInformation): No censoring #>  #> ── Fit Data (object value is a modified tibble): ── #> # A tibble: 1,000 × 5 #>   ID      TIME    DV  IPRED      v #>   <fct>  <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1 1     0.0429     0 -0.557 -0.294 #> 2 1     0.0693     1 -0.850 -0.293 #> 3 1     0.0743     0 -0.557 -0.293 #> # ℹ 997 more rows # }

title: Predict method for nlmixr2 fit core objects — predict.nlmixr2FitCore
code: # \donttest{  one.compartment <- function() {  ini({   tka <- log(1)   tcl <- log(10)   tv <- log(35)   eta.ka ~ 0.1   eta.cl ~ 0.1   eta.v ~ 0.1   add.sd <- 0.1  })  model({   ka <- exp(tka + eta.ka)   cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)   v <- exp(tv + eta.v)   d/dt(depot) = -ka * depot   d/dt(center) = ka * depot - cl / v * center   cp = center / v   cp ~ add(add.sd)  }) }  # The fit is performed by the function nlmixr/nlmix2 specifying # the model, data and estimate fit <- nlmixr2(one.compartment, theo_sd, est = "focei",                foceiControl(maxOuterIterations = 0L)) #>   #>   #>   #>   #> ℹ parameter labels from comments are typically ignored in non-interactive mode #> ℹ Need to run with the source intact to parse comments #> ℹ moved zero initial estimate(s) off 0 (foceiControl(zeroTheta)): tka #> → loading into symengine environment... #> → pruning branches (`if`/`else`) of full model... #> ✔ done #> → calculate sensitivities #> → calculate ∂(f)/∂(η) #> → calculate ∂(R²)/∂(η) #> → finding duplicate expressions in inner model... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in inner model... #> → finding duplicate expressions in EBE model... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in EBE model... #> → compiling inner model... #>   #>   #> ✔ done #> → finding duplicate expressions in FD model... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in FD model... #> → compiling EBE model... #>   #>   #> ✔ done #> → compiling events FD model... #>   #>   #> ✔ done #> → Calculating residuals/tables #> ✔ done  # Population predictions ppred <- predict(fit, theo_sd, level="population") #> ℹ population predictions requested (`level="population"`) #> ℹ using new data for predictions #>   #>   #>   #>   #>   #>    # Individual predictions ipred <- predict(fit, theo_sd, level="individual") #> ℹ individual predictions requested (`level="individual"`) #> ℹ using new data for predictions #>   #>    # }

what: Control for the qrpem (quasi-random parametric EM) estimation method — qrpemControl
title: Control for the qrpem (quasi-random parametric EM) estimation method — qrpemControl
text: convenience wrapper around [impmapControl()] defaulting `qr=TRUE` (Sobol quasi-random importance samples) `sir=TRUE` (SIR-accelerated non-mu / residual-error M-step); explicitly supplied arguments win.  See [impmapControl()] full parameter list.

title: Control for the qrpem (quasi-random parametric EM) estimation method — qrpemControl
code: qrpemControl(..., qr = TRUE, sir = TRUE)

title: Control for the qrpem (quasi-random parametric EM) estimation method — qrpemControl
text: ... Parameters passed [impmapControl()]. qr `TRUE`, draw quasi-random (Sobol low-discrepancy) importance samples instead pseudo-random Gaussian samples (QRPEM, Leary & Dunlavey PAGE 2012); E-step integrals converge O(1/N) instead O(1/sqrt(N)). sir `TRUE`, accelerate non-mu / residual-error M-step SIR (sampling-importance-resampling): theta-sensitivity Newton step uses `sirSample` equal-weight resampled points per subject instead `isample` weighted samples.

title: Control for the qrpem (quasi-random parametric EM) estimation method — qrpemControl
text: `impmapControl` object QRPEM defaults.

title: Control for the qrpem (quasi-random parametric EM) estimation method — qrpemControl
text: Note know QRPEM implementation Phoenix NLME since details method public. However, matches QRPEM method using quasi-random parametric EM SIR accelerated parameter convergence described Leary & Dunlavey (2012) PAGE 2012, 19(1): 1-6.

title: Remove an eta from the model — rmEta
code: mod <- function ()  {  description <- "One compartment PK model with linear clearance"  ini({    lka <- 0.45    lcl <- 1    lvc <- 3.45     propSd <- c(0, 0.5)     etaKa ~ 0.1   })  model({    ka <- exp(lka + etaKa)    cl <- exp(lcl)    vc <- exp(lvc)    Cc <- linCmt()    Cc ~ prop(propSd)  }) }  mod |> rmEta("etaKa") #>   #>   #> ℹ parameter labels from comments are typically ignored in non-interactive mode #> ℹ Need to run with the source intact to parse comments #>  ── rxode2-based solved PK 1-compartment model ──────────────────────────────────  #>  ── Initalization: ──   #> Fixed Effects ($theta):  #>    lka    lcl    lvc propSd  #>   0.45   1.00   3.45   0.50  #>  #> States ($state or $stateDf):  #>   Compartment Number Compartment Name Rate   Off Internal # #> 1                  1            depot TRUE FALSE          1 #> 2                  2          central TRUE FALSE          2 #>  ── Model (Normalized Syntax): ──  #> function() { #>     description <- "One compartment PK model with linear clearance" #>     ini({ #>         lka <- 0.45 #>         lcl <- 1 #>         lvc <- 3.45 #>         propSd <- c(0, 0.5) #>     }) #>     model({ #>         ka <- exp(lka) #>         cl <- exp(lcl) #>         vc <- exp(lvc) #>         Cc <- linCmt() #>         Cc ~ prop(propSd) #>     }) #> }  # This can also remove more than one eta  mod <- function ()  {  description <- "One compartment PK model with linear clearance"  ini({    lka <- 0.45    lcl <- 1    lvc <- 3.45    propSd <- c(0, 0.5)    etaKa ~ 0.1    etaCl ~ 0.2    etaVc ~ 0.3   })  model({    ka <- exp(lka + etaKa)    cl <- exp(lcl + etaCl)    vc <- exp(lvc + etaVc)    Cc <- linCmt()    Cc ~ prop(propSd)  }) }  mod |> rmEta(c("etaKa", "etaCl")) #>   #>   #> ℹ parameter labels from comments are typically ignored in non-interactive mode #> ℹ Need to run with the source intact to parse comments #>  ── rxode2-based solved PK 1-compartment model ──────────────────────────────────  #>  ── Initalization: ──   #> Fixed Effects ($theta):  #>    lka    lcl    lvc propSd  #>   0.45   1.00   3.45   0.50  #>  #> Omega ($omega):  #>       etaVc #> etaVc   0.3 #>  #> States ($state or $stateDf):  #>   Compartment Number Compartment Name Rate   Off Internal # #> 1                  1            depot TRUE FALSE          1 #> 2                  2          central TRUE FALSE          2 #>  ── μ-referencing ($muRefTable): ──   #>   theta   eta level #> 1   lvc etaVc    id #>  #>  ── Model (Normalized Syntax): ──  #> function() { #>     description <- "One compartment PK model with linear clearance" #>     ini({ #>         lka <- 0.45 #>         lcl <- 1 #>         lvc <- 3.45 #>         propSd <- c(0, 0.5) #>         etaVc ~ 0.3 #>     }) #>     model({ #>         ka <- exp(lka) #>         cl <- exp(lcl) #>         vc <- exp(lvc + etaVc) #>         Cc <- linCmt() #>         Cc ~ prop(propSd) #>     }) #> }

title: Control Options for SAEM — saemControl
code: saemControl(   seed = 99,   nBurn = 200,   nEm = 300,   nmc = 3,   nu = c(2, 2, 2),   print = 1L,   trace = 0,   covMethod = c("sa", "analytic", "linFim", "fim", "r,s", "r", "s", "imp", ""),   covMethodDeferred = NA_character_,   covFull = TRUE,   nSaCov = 500L,   calcTables = TRUE,   logLik = FALSE,   nnodesGq = 3,   nsdGq = 1.6,   optExpression = TRUE,   literalFix = FALSE,   adjObf = TRUE,   sumProd = FALSE,   addProp = c("combined2", "combined1"),   tol = NULL,   itmax = 30,   type = c("newuoa", "nelder-mead"),   powRange = 10,   lambdaRange = 3,   odeRecalcFactor = 10^(0.5),   maxOdeRecalc = 5L,   indTolRelax = TRUE,   perSa = 0.75,   perNoCor = 0.75,   perFixOmega = 0.1,   perFixResid = 0.1,   compress = TRUE,   rxControl = NULL,   sigdig = NULL,   sigdigTable = NULL,   ci = 0.95,   muRefCov = TRUE,   muRefCovAlg = TRUE,   handleUninformativeEtas = TRUE,   iovXform = c("sd", "var", "logsd", "logvar"),   boundedTransform = TRUE,   eventSens = c("jump", "fd"),   mixProbMethod = c("regress", "regularized", "annealed"),   mixProbStepExp = 1,   mixProbPriorN = 20,   mixSampleMethod = c("parallel", "msaem"),   nonMuTheta = c("regress", "eta"),   residWarmStart = TRUE,   censOption = c("gauss", "laplace"),   lbfgsLmm = 5L,   lbfgsFactr = NULL,   lbfgsPgtol = NULL,   lbfgsMaxIter = 20L,   ... )

title: Control Options for SAEM — saemControl
text: seed Random Seed SAEM step.  (Needs set reproducibility.)  default 99. nBurn Number iterations first phase, ie  MCMC/Stochastic Approximation steps. equivalent Monolix's K_0 K_b. nEm Number iterations Expectation-Maximization (EM) Step. equivalent Monolix's K_1. nmc Number Markov Chains. default 3.  increase number chains numerical integration MC method accurate cost computation.  Monolix equivalent L. nu vector 3 integers. represent     numbers transitions three different kernels used     Hasting-Metropolis algorithm.  default value c(2,2,2),     representing 40 transition initially (value     multiplied 20). first value represents initial number multi-variate     Gibbs samples taken normal distribution. second value represents number uni-variate, multi-     dimensional random walk Gibbs samples taken. third value represents number bootstrap/reshuffling     uni-dimensional random samples taken. print Either scalar print-frequency (`0` = suppress, `1` (default) = every evaluation, `N` = every Nth), pre-built [iterPrintControl()] object. Equivalent `iterPrintControl(every = print, ncol = printNcol, useColor = useColor)`. trace integer indicating want trace(1) SAEM algorithm process.  Useful debugging, typical fitting. covMethod Method calculating covariance.      discussion, R Hessian matrix objective     function. S matrix sum individual's     gradient cross-product (evaluated individual empirical     Bayes estimates). "sa" (default) Use stochastic-approximation Fisher Information  Matrix.  estimation, dedicated covariance phase (nSaCov  iterations) holds parameters converged estimate keeps  resimulating individual parameters, Monte-Carlo averaging Louis  observed-information integrand converged FIM decoupled cooling  schedule (approach used Monolix; Kuhn & Lavielle 2005).  Always includes  every estimated population parameter (theta, Omega diagonal  variances, residual). "analytic" Compute FOCEI analytic observed-information covariance  converged SAEM estimates.  model analytic-covariance  scope (e.g. linCmt(), non-normal likelihood, non-SD IOV  parameterization) result positive definite, falls back  linearized Fisher information (linFim) message. "linFim" Use Linearized Fisher Information Matrix calculate covariance. "fim" Use Fisher Information Matrix accumulated SAEM  estimation calculate covariance.  Like sa inverts observed  information full theta + Omega diagonal + residual covariance,  uses (noisier) estimation-phase matrix rather dedicated cov phase. fim sa simulation-based Fisher information covers  structural theta, Omega diagonal variances, additive residual error.  -diagonal Omega covariances proportional/combined residual error  estimated reliably simulation FIM (complete-data correction unstable  -subject variability dominates residual), variance-block standard  errors spliced linearized FIM (linFim). "r,s" Uses sandwich matrix calculate covariance, : \(R^-1 \times S \times R^-1\) "r" Uses Hessian matrix calculate covariance \(2\times R^-1\) "s" Uses crossproduct matrix calculate covariance \(4\times S^-1\) "" calculate covariance step. covMethodDeferred Internal.  foreign covariance ("sa"/"imp") requested, stashed computed post-fit converged estimates decoupled recompute engine (setCov() uses path); NA otherwise. covFull Boolean (default TRUE) indicating covariance include every estimated population parameter – structural residual thetas plus Omega variance/covariance elements – named om.<eta> / cov.<eta>.<eta>.  FALSE legacy structural-theta-covariance reported.  Ignored covMethod="sa", always full. nSaCov Number iterations dedicated stochastic-approximation covariance phase used covMethod="sa" (default 500).  iterations run converged estimate (parameters frozen) resimulate individual parameters build observed Fisher information; larger value gives less noisy covariance.  Ignored covariance methods. calcTables boolean determine foceiFit calculate tables. default TRUE logLik boolean indicating log-likelihood calculate Gaussian quadrature. nnodesGq number nodes use Gaussian quadrature computing likelihood method (defaults 1, equivalent Laplacian likelihood) nsdGq span (SD) integrate computing likelihood Gaussian quadrature. Defaults 3 (eg 3 times SD) optExpression Optimize rxode2 expression speed calculation. default turned . literalFix boolean, substitute fixed population values literals re-adjust ui parameter estimates optimization; Default `TRUE`. adjObf boolean indicate objective function adjusted closer NONMEM's default objective function.  default TRUE sumProd boolean indicating model change multiplication high precision multiplication sums high precision sums using PreciseSums package.  default FALSE. addProp Type additive-plus-proportional error: `"combined1"`, standard deviations add: $$y = f + (+ b\times f^c) \times \varepsilon$$; `"combined2"`, variances add: $$y = f + \sqrt{^2 + b^2\times f^{2\times c}} \times \varepsilon$$. y = observed, f = predicted, = additive sd, b = proportional/power sd, c = power exponent (1 proportional case). tol tolerance regression models used complex residual errors (ie add+prop etc) itmax maximum number iterations regression models used complex residual errors.  number iterations itmax*number parameters type indicates type optimization residuals; Can one c("nelder-mead", "newuoa") powRange indicates range powers can take residual errors;  default 10 indicating range c(-10, 10) lambdaRange indicates range Box-Cox Yeo-Johnson parameters constrained ;  default 3 indicating range c(-3,3) odeRecalcFactor ODE recalculation factor ODE solving goes bad, factor rtol/atol reduced maxOdeRecalc Maximum number times reduce ODE tolerances try resolve system bad ODE solve. indTolRelax `TRUE` (default), subjects whose ODE solve produced NaN/Inf tolerances relaxed, relaxed tolerance persists across optimizer calls (sticky). `FALSE`, subjects tolerances relaxed retry tolerances reset afterward. perSa percent time `nBurn` iterations phase runs runs simulated annealing. perNoCor percentage MCMC phase SAEM algorithm variance/covariance matrix correlations. default 0.75 75 Monte-carlo iteration. perFixOmega percentage `nBurn` phase omega values unfixed allow better exploration likelihood surface.  time, omegas fixed optimization. perFixResid percentage `nBurn` phase residual components unfixed allow better exploration likelihood surface. compress object compressed items rxControl `rxode2` ODE solving options fitting, created `rxControl()` sigdig Specifies "significant digits" ode solving requests.  specified controls relative absolute tolerances ODE solvers.  default tolerance 0.5*10^(-sigdig-2) regular ODEs. sensitivity equations default 0.5*10\^(-sigdig-1.5) (sensitivity changes applicable liblsoda).  also controls atol/rtol steady state solutions. ssAtol/ssRtol 0.5*10\^(-sigdig) sensitivities 0.5*10\^(-sigdig+0.625).  default unspecified (NULL) uses standard atol/rtol. sigdigTable Significant digits final output table. specified, matches significant digits `sigdig` optimization algorithm.  `sigdig` NULL, use 3. ci Confidence level tables.  default 0.95 95% confidence. muRefCov controls mu-referenced covariates `saem` handled differently non mu-referenced covariates.  `TRUE`, mu-referenced covariates special handling.  `FALSE` mu-referenced covariates treated input parameter. muRefCovAlg controls algebraic expressions can   mu-referenced treated mu-referenced covariates : 1. Creating internal data-variable `nlmixrMuDerCov#`      algebraic mu-referenced expression 2. Change algebraic expression `nlmixrMuDerCov# * mu_cov_theta` 3. Use internal mu-referenced covariate saem 4. optimization completed, replace `model()` old   `model()` expression 5. Remove `nlmixrMuDerCov#` nlmix2 output general, covariates accurate since changes system linear compartment model.  Therefore, default `TRUE`. handleUninformativeEtas boolean tells nlmixr2's saem calculate uninformative etas handle specially (default `TRUE`). iovXform Transformation used diagonal IOV: one "sd", "var", "logsd", "logvar". boundedTransform `TRUE` (default), bounded parameters transformed unbounded optimization methods back-transformed final estimates. `FALSE` optimizes original scale bounds passed optimizer. `NA` transforms optimization skips final back-transform. eventSens Controls dosing/event-parameter (`alag`, `F`, `rate`, `dur`) sensitivities computed THETA/ETA gradients: `"jump"` (default) uses rxode2's analytic event sensitivities; `"fd"` uses legacy finite-difference behavior. mixProbMethod mixture models (`mix()`, one   component), stabilizes mixing-probability estimate   collapsing onto single component (responsibility used   update weighted current mixing probability,   can create runaway feedback loop). Three options: * `"regress"` (default): treat per-subject mixture membership     fixed regressor.  subject hard-classified component     front, held fixed, fed solve (via existing     mixture-index regressor), skipping per-iteration soft-EM     responsibility step entirely.  Avoids responsibility feedback     loop / collapse construction lower-bias; heavily     overlapping components higher-variance (early     misclassification revisited), prefer `"regularized"`     membership genuinely uncertain. * `"regularized"`: blend `mixProbPriorN` pseudo-subjects,     distributed per initial mixing probability,     responsibility average iteration (Dirichlet/MAP-EM-style).     Prevents collapse even difficult cases, cost     bias toward initial guess; may need larger `nBurn`/`nEm`. * `"annealed"`: give mixing-probability update decaying     step-size schedule (`mixProbStepExp`) instead     full-replacement step used `nBurn`. Lower bias,     fix systematic (non-noise-driven) collapse. mixProbStepExp used `mixProbMethod="annealed"`. Decay exponent mixing-probability step size (`1/iteration^mixProbStepExp`), applied iteration 1. Default 1; smaller values decay slowly. mixProbPriorN used `mixProbMethod="regularized"`. Number pseudo-subjects blended responsibility average iteration. Larger values robust collapse bias estimate need `nBurn`/`nEm`. Default 20. mixSampleMethod mixture models per-component etas   (split-ETA, e.g. `cl <- mix(tcl1 + eta.cl1, p1, tcl2 + eta.cl2)`),   controls MCMC/sufficient-statistic architecture   individual random effects, independent `mixProbMethod`. BSV   (`$omega`) split components unreliable `"parallel"`   regardless `mixProbMethod`. * `"parallel"` (default): one full MCMC chain per component per     subject per iteration, blended post hoc responsibility. Mirrors     NONMEM's `$MIX` correctly estimates BSV shared across     components, cleanly separate per-component BSV     split-ETA models ("wrong-hypothesis" chain still explores     non-owned column(s) unconstrained prior noise). * `"msaem"` (experimental): MSAEM algorithm (Lavielle & Mbogning     2014), used Monolix. Simulates one random-effects trajectory     per subject per iteration (label marginalized via     closed-form responsibility) instead parallel per-component     chains, post-hoc blending needed. compute-matched     `"parallel"` equal `nmc` – set `nmc` roughly `nMix` times     default fair comparison. Uses model-aware stratified     initialization split-ETA components reliably achieves     full theta/fixed-effect separation. Split-ETA BSV recovery     improved (two numerical bugs fixed: `IGamma2_phi1` blowup     locked variance exactly zero, inverted responsibility     sign) still reliable – often settles safety-floor     value rather true variance. Prefer `"parallel"` unless     specifically evaluating method. nonMuTheta Controls population `theta`   mu-referenced (appear linearly eta – SAEM `phi0`   fixed effects) estimated. * `"regress"` (default): keep parameter plain directly-estimated     `theta` regressor.  iteration `phi0` estimated bounded     direct optimization observation likelihood (robust coordinate     descent within local trust region, honoring `ini` bounds), held     fixed rather drawn stochastically shrinking variance.      recovers population parameters associated random effect     accurately, extra runtime (objective re-solves     ODE). * `"eta"`: historic SAEM treatment (parameter carried     stochastic `phi0` block). residWarmStart Boolean (default `TRUE`); warm-start residual-error parameters observed per-endpoint moments initial predictions (additive SD `sqrt(mean(err^2))`, proportional SD `sqrt(mean((err/f)^2))`), moment estimate `est="npag"` uses.  Gives stochastic step better starting residual scale.  Set `FALSE` start `ini`-block residual values instead. censOption Treatment second derivative censored (M2/M3/M4/BLQ) observations FOCEI family.  "gauss" (default) keeps historic uncensored Gauss-Newton curvature, matching common PMx tools; "laplace" uses exact censored second derivative objective (proper Laplace inner Hessian analytic covariance).  Accepted saemControl/nlmControl uniform interface inert – SAEM (stochastic EM) Laplace inner Hessian, NLM uses finite-difference Hessian already reflects censoring exactly. lbfgsLmm Integer number BFGS corrections (L-BFGS-B `lmm` memory) used refining fixed-effect-parameters general log-likelihood model (`ll(name) ~ <expr>`) direct L-BFGS-B optimization observation likelihood.  Default 5. lbfgsFactr Convergence tolerance relative reduction objective L-BFGS-B refinement (`factr` control, units machine epsilon).  `NULL` (default) derived `sigdig` way `foceiControl()` (`10^(-sigdig - 1) / .Machine$double.eps`). lbfgsPgtol Convergence tolerance projected gradient L-BFGS-B refinement (`pgtol` control).  `NULL` (default) derived `sigdig` (`10^(-sigdig - 1)`). lbfgsMaxIter Integer maximum number iterations L-BFGS-B refinement.  Default 20. ... arguments control SAEM.

title: Control for uobyqa estimation method in nlmixr2 — uobyqaControl
text: npt Number points bobyqa's quadratic approximation objective; must `[n+2, (n+1)(n+2)/2]`. Defaults `2*n + 1`. (bobyqa) rhobeg Initial trust region radius bobyqa outer optimizer (`rhoend`, must satisfy `0 < rhoend < rhobeg`). Default `0.2` (20 `abs(upper-lower)/2`. (bobyqa) rhoend Final trust region radius. defined, `10^(-sigdig-1)` used. (bobyqa) iprint Controls amount printing (`0`=none, `1`=start/end , `2`=new rho, `3`=every function evaluation, `>3`=every `iprint` evaluations). Default `0`. maxfun maximum allowed number function evaluations. exceeded, method terminate. returnUobyqa return uobyqa output instead nlmixr2 fit stickyRecalcN number bad ODE solves reducing atol/rtol rest problem. maxOdeRecalc Maximum number times reduce ODE tolerances try resolve system bad ODE solve. odeRecalcFactor ODE recalculation factor ODE solving goes bad, factor rtol/atol reduced indTolRelax `TRUE` (default), subjects whose ODE solve produced NaN/Inf tolerances relaxed, relaxed tolerance persists across optimizer calls (sticky). `FALSE`, subjects tolerances relaxed retry tolerances reset afterward. useColor Logical (`NULL`) emit ANSI bold/color escapes iteration print. `NULL` (default) defers [crayon::has_color()]. printNcol Integer (`NULL`) parameter columns per row wrapping. `NULL` (default) uses `floor((getOption("width") - 23) / 12)`. print Either scalar print-frequency (`0` = suppress, `1` (default) = every evaluation, `N` = every Nth), pre-built [iterPrintControl()] object. Equivalent `iterPrintControl(every = print, ncol = printNcol, useColor = useColor)`. normType Parameter normalization/scaling used get scaled initial values scaleType, form Vscaled = (Vunscaled-C1)/C2 (see Feature Scaling; rescale2 follows OptdesX manual): "rescale2" scales parameters (-1, 1); "rescale" (min-max) scales (0, 1); "mean" centers mean range (0, 1); "std" standardizes mean/sd; "len" scales unit (Euclidean) length; "constant" performs normalization (C1=0, C2=1). scaleType scaling scheme nlmixr2: "nlmixr2" (default) scales (current-init)*scaleC[] + scaleTo, scaleTo normType scales scaleC; "norm" uses simple scaling normType; "mult" scales multiplicatively current/init*scaleTo; "multAdd" scales linearly ((current-init)+scaleTo) parameters exponential block (e.g. exp(theta)) multiplicatively otherwise. scaleCmax Maximum value scaleC prevent overflow. scaleCmin Minimum value scaleC prevent underflow. scaleC Scaling constant used scaleType="nlmixr2"; specified, chosen parameter type keep gradient sizes similar log scale: `1` exp()-transformed/power/boxCox/ yeoJohnson parameters, `0.5*abs(est)` additive/proportional/ lognormal error parameters, `abs(1/digamma(est+1))` factorials, `log(abs(est))*abs(est)` log-scale parameters. May set explicitly per parameter defaults apply well. scaleTo Scale initial parameter estimate value. default 1.  zero , scaling performed. rxControl `rxode2` ODE solving options fitting, created `rxControl()` optExpression Optimize rxode2 expression speed calculation. default turned . sumProd boolean indicating model change multiplication high precision multiplication sums high precision sums using PreciseSums package.  default FALSE. literalFix boolean, substitute fixed population values literals re-adjust ui parameter estimates optimization; Default `TRUE`. literalFixRes boolean, substitute fixed population values literals re-adjust ui parameter estimates optimization; Default `TRUE`. addProp Type additive-plus-proportional error: `"combined1"`, standard deviations add: $$y = f + (+ b\times f^c) \times \varepsilon$$; `"combined2"`, variances add: $$y = f + \sqrt{^2 + b^2\times f^{2\times c}} \times \varepsilon$$. y = observed, f = predicted, = additive sd, b = proportional/power sd, c = power exponent (1 proportional case). calcTables boolean determine foceiFit calculate tables. default TRUE compress object compressed items covMethod Method calculating covariance.  "r" (default) uses nlmixr2's nlmixr2Hess() Hessian; "" skips covariance step. adjObf boolean indicate objective function adjusted closer NONMEM's default objective function.  default TRUE ci Confidence level tables.  default 0.95 95% confidence. sigdig Optimization significant digits; controls inner/outer optimization tolerance (10^-sigdig), boundary check tolerance (5*10^(-sigdig+1)), ODE solver tolerances.  solver tolerances split solver stiffness keep atol well rtol: stiff solver (lsoda/liblsoda – auto-switching method – plus indLin, default) uses rtol = 10^(-sigdig-3), atol = 10^(-sigdig-5), non-stiff explicit dop853 uses looser rtol = 10^-sigdig, atol = 10^(-sigdig-3).  sensitivity (atolSens/rtolSens) steady-state (ssAtol/ssRtol) tolerances run one order looser corresponding main tolerance.  default sigdig = 4 stiff solve atol = 1e-9, rtol = 1e-7. sigdigTable Significant digits final output table. specified, matches significant digits `sigdig` optimization algorithm.  `sigdig` NULL, use 3. boundedTransform `TRUE` (default), bounded parameters transformed unbounded optimization methods back-transformed final estimates. `FALSE` optimizes original scale bounds passed optimizer. `NA` transforms optimization skips final back-transform. eventSens Controls dosing/event-parameter (`alag`, `F`, `rate`, `dur`) sensitivities computed THETA/ETA gradients: `"jump"` (default) uses rxode2's analytic event sensitivities; `"fd"` uses legacy finite-difference behavior. ... title: Control for uobyqa estimation method in nlmixr2 — uobyqaControl
code: # \donttest{ # A logit regression example with emax model  dsn <- data.frame(i=1:1000) dsn$time <- exp(rnorm(1000)) dsn$DV=rbinom(1000,1,exp(-1+dsn$time)/(1+exp(-1+dsn$time)))  mod <- function() {  ini({    E0 <- 0.5    Em <- 0.5    E50 <- 2    g <- fix(2)  })  model({    v <- E0+Em*time^g/(E50^g+time^g)    ll(bin) ~ DV * v - log(1 + exp(v))  }) }  fit2 <- nlmixr(mod, dsn, est="uobyqa") #>   #>   #>   #>   #> ℹ parameter labels from comments are typically ignored in non-interactive mode #> ℹ Need to run with the source intact to parse comments #> → pruning branches (`if`/`else`) of population log-likelihood model... #> ✔ done #> → loading llik model into symengine environment... #> → finding duplicate expressions in population log-likelihood model... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in population log-likelihood model... #> ✔ done #>   #>   #> → calculating covariance #> ✔ done #> → loading into symengine environment... #> → pruning branches (`if`/`else`) of full model... #> ✔ done #> → finding duplicate expressions in EBE model... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in EBE model... #> → compiling EBE model... #>   #>   #> ✔ done #> → Calculating residuals/tables #> ✔ done  print(fit2) #> ── nlmixr² log-likelihood uobyqa ── #>  #>           OBJF     AIC      BIC Log-likelihood Condition#(Cov) Condition#(Cor) #> lPop -679.7168 1164.16 1178.884      -579.0801         407.093        67.95288 #>  #> ── Time (sec $time): ── #>  #>             setup  optimize covariance preprocess postprocess table compress #> elapsed 0.3771115 0.3718097  6.269e-06       0.05       0.006 0.026        0 #>             other #> elapsed 0.1000726 #>  #> ── ($parFixed or $parFixedDf): ── #>  #>        Est.     SE  %RSE   Back-transformed(95%CI) #> E0  -0.8062 0.2470 30.64 -0.8062 (-1.290, -0.3221) #> Em    5.487  2.368 43.16     5.487 (0.8456, 10.13) #> E50   2.898  1.244 42.94     2.898 (0.4592, 5.337) #> g     2.000  FIXED FIXED                     2.000 #>   #>   Covariance Type ($covMethod): r #>   Censoring ($censInformation): No censoring #>  #> ── Fit Data (object is a modified tibble): ── #> # A tibble: 1,000 × 5 #>   ID      TIME    DV  IPRED      v #>   <fct>  <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1 1     0.0383     0 -0.369 -0.805 #> 2 1     0.0509     0 -0.370 -0.805 #> 3 1     0.0543     1 -1.17  -0.804 #> # ℹ 997 more rows  # you can also get the nlm output with fit2$nlm  fit2$uobyqa #> parameter estimates: -0.806218086692849, 5.48662208163687, 2.89816154446765  #> objective: 579.080149823444  #> number of function evaluations: 61   # The nlm control has been modified slightly to include # extra components and name the parameters # }

title: Control for vae (variational autoencoder) estimation method in nlmixr2 — vaeControl
text: seed Random seed VAE training (encoder init, Adam, reparameterization sampling); default 42. Training stochastic, fixed seed makes every fit reproducible. itersBurnIn Number burn-iterations (encoder-, tiny KL weight) main EM phase. klWarmup Number KL-annealing iterations KL weight ramped small value 1 (prevents posterior collapse). gammaIter Number main iterations EMA-smoothing phase population-parameter update begins. iters Total number main-loop iterations (burn-). nGradStep Number Adam gradient steps per EM outer iteration (reference `L_iter`). hiddenDim LSTM hidden dimension (reference `h_dim`). learningRate Adam learning rate used main training phase. burnInLearningRate Adam learning rate used burn-. sigma0 Encoder prior standard deviation(s) initialization (small value giving sharp initial posterior). `NULL` uses small default per individual parameter. distinct `ini()` omega. covariateSelection `TRUE` (default) perform automated BICc-ELBO covariate selection training; `FALSE` fit covariate structure written model.  `FALSE` case model-declared covariate coefficients (linear `beta*WT` effects transformed ones `beta*log(WT/70)`) estimated place regress M-step regardless `nonMuTheta`; `ini(... ~ fix())` coefficient stays fixed. covSelectAlpha Starting multiplier covariate-selection L0 penalty, ramped linearly `covSelectAlpha` `1` `klWarmup` warmup iterations held `1` afterward (matching reference implementation's `linspace(alpha, 1, kl_iter)`).  Values `> 1` penalize covariate entry heavily early training; `1` disables ramp. bnbStrategy Frontier discipline exact branch--bound covariate selection: `"lifo"` (default, last--first-depth-first search), `"fifo"` (first--first-) `"lc"` (least cost / best-first).  solver exact, selected covariates identical every strategy; search order (thus efficiency) differs. parEncoderBackward Parallelize encoder backward (gradient) pass subjects.  Defaults `TRUE` unless `options(nlmixr2.identical = TRUE)` set (flips default `FALSE`); explicit value always wins. encoder forward pass covariate branch--bound already run multi-threaded bit-identical serial run.  backward gradient continuous cross-subject sum, parallelizing (per-thread partials reduced thread order) makes result deterministic fixed number `cores` longer bit-identical serial path: per-step gradient differs ~1e-12, compounds iterative SGD/EM training small final difference (well estimation tolerance), results may differ across different `cores`.  active (`cores > 1`) note added fit's `$runInfo`.  Set `FALSE` – globally `options(nlmixr2.identical = TRUE)` – bit-identical, fully reproducible results. nonMuTheta treat structural population `theta`   random effect (mu-referenced) can still estimated VAE   (estimates parameters occupy latent space).    eta-injection modes small eta injected parameter enters   latent space, reported fixed effect `theta + mean(eta)`   temporary eta dropped output model. * `"regress"` (default, matching `saemControl(nonMuTheta=)`): eta     injected; instead theta estimated directly, re-optimized every     M-step bounded `bobyqa` regression FOCEi inner likelihood     (bounds `ini()` lower/upper), blended M-step gain.      recovers -random-effect population parameter without adding spurious     random effect.  `nonMuEtaOmega` unused mode.   * `"eta"`: inject eta ESTIMATED omega (starting     `nonMuEtaOmega`); typical value estimated appears     iteration table.   * `"fix"`: inject eta omega held FIXED `nonMuEtaOmega` hold     typical-value theta fixed `ini()` value.  Nothing     parameter estimated, shown iteration table (    reported `ini()` value, marked fixed, injected eta dropped).   * `"none"`: leave non-mu-referenced thetas frozen `ini()` value (    historic behavior). nonMuEtaOmega Variance eta injected non-mu-referenced theta (starting value `nonMuTheta="eta"`, fixed value `nonMuTheta="fix"`; unused `"regress"`). likelihood Inner likelihood used objective, EBEs, gradients, run FOCEi inner interface: `"focei"` (default, eta-epsilon interaction), `"foce"` (interaction, NONMEM FOCE R frozen population prediction), `"focep"` (FOCE+, interaction R evaluated live conditional eta), `"laplace"`. objf objective-function value active AIC/BIC/BICc. linearization importance-sampling -2LL always computed stored; selects default active one. nIsSample Number importance-sampling draws -2LL. returnVae `TRUE` return raw VAE training object instead nlmixr2 fit. print Either scalar print-frequency (`0` = suppress, `1` (default) = every evaluation, `N` = every Nth), pre-built [iterPrintControl()] object. Equivalent `iterPrintControl(every = print, ncol = printNcol, useColor = useColor)`. useColor Logical (`NULL`) emit ANSI bold/color escapes iteration print. `NULL` (default) defers [crayon::has_color()]. printNcol Integer (`NULL`) parameter columns per row wrapping. `NULL` (default) uses `floor((getOption("width") - 23) / 12)`. covMethod Method calculating covariance VAE estimates, run FOCEi covariance step; choices foceiControl(): "analytic" (default), "r,s", "r", "s", "" skip. optExpression Optimize rxode2 expression speed calculation. default turned . sumProd boolean indicating model change multiplication high precision multiplication sums high precision sums using PreciseSums package.  default FALSE. literalFix boolean, substitute fixed population values literals re-adjust ui parameter estimates optimization; Default `TRUE`. literalFixRes boolean, substitute fixed population values literals re-adjust ui parameter estimates optimization; Default `TRUE`. addProp Type additive-plus-proportional error: `"combined1"`, standard deviations add: $$y = f + (+ b\times f^c) \times \varepsilon$$; `"combined2"`, variances add: $$y = f + \sqrt{^2 + b^2\times f^{2\times c}} \times \varepsilon$$. y = observed, f = predicted, = additive sd, b = proportional/power sd, c = power exponent (1 proportional case). calcTables boolean determine foceiFit calculate tables. default TRUE compress object compressed items adjObf boolean indicate objective function adjusted closer NONMEM's default objective function.  default TRUE ci Confidence level tables.  default 0.95 95% confidence. sigdig Specifies "significant digits" ode solving requests.  specified controls relative absolute tolerances ODE solvers.  default tolerance 0.5*10^(-sigdig-2) regular ODEs. sensitivity equations default 0.5*10\^(-sigdig-1.5) (sensitivity changes applicable liblsoda).  also controls atol/rtol steady state solutions. ssAtol/ssRtol 0.5*10\^(-sigdig) sensitivities 0.5*10\^(-sigdig+0.625).  default unspecified (NULL) uses standard atol/rtol. sigdigTable Significant digits final output table. specified, matches significant digits `sigdig` optimization algorithm.  `sigdig` NULL, use 3. stickyRecalcN number bad ODE solves reducing atol/rtol rest problem. maxOdeRecalc Maximum number times reduce ODE tolerances try resolve system bad ODE solve. odeRecalcFactor ODE recalculation factor ODE solving goes bad, factor rtol/atol reduced indTolRelax `TRUE` (default), subjects whose ODE solve produced NaN/Inf tolerances relaxed, relaxed tolerance persists across optimizer calls (sticky). `FALSE`, subjects tolerances relaxed retry tolerances reset afterward. eventSens Controls dosing/event-parameter (`alag`, `F`, `rate`, `dur`) sensitivities computed THETA/ETA gradients: `"jump"` (default) uses rxode2's analytic event sensitivities; `"fd"` uses legacy finite-difference behavior. rxControl `rxode2` ODE solving options fitting, created `rxControl()` ... arguments control SAEM.
