text: vae control structure (class `vaeControl`)

title: Control for vae (variational autoencoder) estimation method in nlmixr2 — vaeControl
text: Covariate selection – MIQP vs. branch--bound.  Per latent parameter selection step minimizes L0/BIC objective `RSS_S/omega + log(N)*|S|` subsets `S` candidate covariates (`RSS_S` residual sum squares ordinary-least-squares fit intercept plus `S`).  reference implementation (Rohleff et al.) writes Mixed-Integer Quadratic Program (MIQP) – binary include/exclude indicators big-M constraints – solves commercial Gurobi solver `cvxpy`.  MIQP-capable solver freely available R: Gurobi commercial/licensed, open QP solvers CRAN (e.g. `osqp`) continuous-represent binary selection.  continuous convex relaxation (L1 / lasso) solvable approximates best subset. package instead solves identical L0/BIC objective EXACTLY self-contained branch--bound: candidate support's coefficients closed-form OLS fit branches pruned valid lower bound (RSS OLS fit using still-free covariates).  therefore returns optimum MIQP – commercial dependency relaxation/accuracy loss – scales dozen covariates.  search worst-case exponential number covariates, pruning makes practical (sparse) case fast (e.g. 32 candidate covariates fraction second).

title: Control for vae (variational autoencoder) estimation method in nlmixr2 — vaeControl
text: Matthew L. Fidler

what: Covariates explored by the VAE covariate search — vaeCovariates
title: Covariates explored by the VAE covariate search — vaeCovariates
text: Returns subject-level covariates `nlmixr2(..., est = "vae")` explore automated covariate selection, using discovery rules fit: every non-reserved numeric data column constant within subject candidate; candidate two unique values (positive) treated continuous (encoded `log(value/mean)`), anything else categorical (mean-centered).  Time-varying numeric columns searched excluded warning.

title: Covariates explored by the VAE covariate search — vaeCovariates
code: vaeCovariates(data, warn = TRUE)

title: Covariates explored by the VAE covariate search — vaeCovariates
text: data estimation dataset containing least `ID` column; column names matched case-insensitively, VAE fit warn `TRUE` (default) warn time-varying numeric columns excluded search; `FALSE` exclude silently

title: Covariates explored by the VAE covariate search — vaeCovariates
text: data frame one row per explored covariate columns   `covariate` (upper-cased column name), `type` (`"continuous"`   `"categorical"`) `center` (population value covariate   centered ); zero rows covariates qualify

title: Covariates explored by the VAE covariate search — vaeCovariates
text: Matthew L. Fidler

title: Covariates explored by the VAE covariate search — vaeCovariates
code: d <- data.frame(id = rep(1:3, each = 2), time = rep(0:1, 3), dv = rnorm(6),                 wt = rep(c(70, 80, 60), each = 2),                 sex = rep(c(0, 1, 0), each = 2)) vaeCovariates(d) #>   covariate        type     center #> 1        WT  continuous 70.0000000 #> 2       SEX categorical  0.3333333

title: VPC simulation — vpcSim
code: # \donttest{  one.cmt <- function() {  ini({    ## You may label each parameter with a comment    tka <- 0.45 # Log Ka    tcl <- log(c(0, 2.7, 100)) # Log Cl    ## This works with interactive models    ## You may also label the preceding line with label("label text")    tv <- 3.45; label("log V")    ## the label("Label name") works with all models    eta.ka ~ 0.6    eta.cl ~ 0.3    eta.v ~ 0.1    add.sd <- 0.7  })  model({    ka <- exp(tka + eta.ka)    cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)    v <- exp(tv + eta.v)    linCmt() ~ add(add.sd)  }) }  fit <- nlmixr(one.cmt, theo_sd, est="focei") #>   #>   #>   #>   #> ℹ parameter labels from comments are typically ignored in non-interactive mode #> ℹ Need to run with the source intact to parse comments #> → Calculating residuals/tables #> ✔ done  head(vpcSim(fit, pred=TRUE)) #>   #>   #>   sim.id id time     ipred        sim  tad        depot  central nlmixrRowNums #> 1      1  1 0.00  0.000000  0.9524208 0.00 3.199920e+02   0.0000             2 #> 2      1  1 0.25 10.011961 10.1707034 0.25 4.548213e+01 269.7910             3 #> 3      1  1 0.57 11.190039 11.3882033 0.57 3.743682e+00 301.5365             4 #> 4      1  1 1.12 10.685218 10.2928740 1.12 5.119677e-02 287.9331             5 #> 5      1  1 2.02  9.711679 10.6667194 2.02 4.560039e-05 261.6993             6 #> 6      1  1 3.82  8.019731  8.4815026 3.82 3.617600e-11 216.1066             7 #>   rxLambda rxYj rxLow rxHi     pred #> 1        1    2     0    1 0.000000 #> 2        1    2     0    1 3.262300 #> 3        1    2     0    1 5.829162 #> 4        1    2     0    1 7.864057 #> 5        1    2     0    1 8.504603 #> 6        1    2     0    1 7.620448  # }

title: nlmixr2est 7.0.0
text: optimization sigdig now sets ODE solver tolerances common ODE convention – atol well rtol, split solver stiffness – every estimation method (sigdig routed focei/foce/fo/laplace, saem, advi, vae, nlme, nls, nlm family). Stiffness classified rxode2::rxIsNonStiff(): stiff-solver (ros4, cvode, bdf, …), auto-switching solver (lsoda/liblsoda, default) stiff-secondary composite (dop853+ros4) uses rtol = 10^(-sigdig-3), atol = 10^(-sigdig-5); purely non-stiff solver (dop853, dop5, Runge-Kutta / Verner methods, …) uses looser rtol = 10^-sigdig, atol = 10^(-sigdig-3). Sensitivity (atolSens/rtolSens) steady-state (ssAtol/ssRtol) solves run one order looser. default sigdig = 4 stiff solve atol = 1e-9, rtol = 1e-7 (symmetric atol = rtol = 5e-7). explicit atol/rtol passed rxControl (list(...) full rxode2::rxControl() object) still overrides sigdig-derived value. sigdig still drives optimizer tolerances . optimization sigdig now also sets estimation method’s optimizer convergence tolerances (previously FOCEi SAEM derived sigdig). FOCEi-family optimizers methods reuse take FOCEi formula 10^(-sigdig-1) (n1qn1 epsilon; bobyqa/newuoa/uobyqa rhoend; nlminb rel.tol/x.tol; lbfgsb3c factr = 10^(-sigdig-1)/eps; optim abstol/reltol/factr matching closest FOCEi optimizer; saem’s inner residual-regression tol). Methods tuned defaults keep default sigdig = 4 scale one order per significant digit: nlm gradtol/steptol (1e-6 4), nls tol (1e-5) / ftol/ptol (sqrt(eps)), nlme tolerance (1e-5), msTol (1e-6), pnlsTol (1e-3) – e.g. nlme sigdig = 5 tolerance = 1e-6, msTol = 1e-7, pnlsTol = 1e-4. explicitly supplied tolerance always overrides sigdig-derived value. FOCEi guards theta’s scaling constant per transform, keeping derivative-based scaleC well-behaved falling back transform’s singular / --range region. parameter keeps 1/|init| (linear/additive), 1 (log-normal), transform-specific formula value stays inside band tailored transform (linear band foceiControl(scaleCband=), default c(0.1, 10)). Outside band falls back parameter’s native magnitude |init| (NONMEM7 Appendix K, eq 15.2); bounded transform (logit/expit/probit/probitInv), |init| also range uses geometric middle band. fixes singular cases froze destabilized fit – 1/|init| blowing small covariate initial estimate (issue-641 large-additive case, whose special handling subsumes), log() init 1, logit interval midpoint, factorial/gamma digamma zero – leaving well-scaled common case, results, unchanged. bounded-transform (logit/expit/probit/probitInv) scaleC band now built parameter’s low high bound instead fixed cutoff. derivative-based scaleC factors N * M, N per-parameter scale using distance bound ((x-low)(hi-x)/(hi-low) logit/probit, E/(hi-low) expit/probitInv) M bounds-invariant factor carries singularity. Guarding scaleC N * [lo, hi] applies dimensionless band every bound, logit(x, 0, 1) logit(x, 1, 100) guarded identically equal fractional position. Previously wide interval (e.g. logit(x, 1, 100)) healthy large scaleC clipped fixed c(1e-4, 10) band slammed midpoint; (0, 1) results unchanged. Fixed FOCEi scaleC gamma()-transformed population parameter: rxode2 reports curEval="lgammafn", scaling setup recognize, silently received linear 1/|init| default instead 1/digamma scaling. FOCEi family nudges structural population parameter (theta) initialized exactly 0 zero estimation, controlled foceiControl(zeroTheta=) (default 0.001), since zero initial estimate native scale scale . +zeroTheta used within parameter’s bounds, otherwise -zeroTheta; neither within bounds errors. Fixed parameters (including fixed 0) left untouched. Residual error parameters also left untouched: carry scaleC, error sd set exactly 0 still disables component combined error model reduces smaller model . foceiControl() gains shi21hMax shi21hMin (defaults 2.0 1e-4), upper lower bounds adaptive shi21 finite-difference step used FOCEi gradients (inner eta , shi21maxOuter != 0, outer theta/covariate finite differences). larger upper bound lets gradient flat, small-magnitude parameter clear ODE-solver noise floor. NLM family keeps fixed bounds. imp / impmap / qrpem importance-sampling family faster: theta-score M-step, Monte-Carlo covariance (covMethod="imp", default) per-subject proposal build E-step now parallelized subjects, using cores set control’s rxControl (defaulting rxode2::getRxThreads()), joining already-threaded E-step weight loop. bit-identical single-threaded run thread count. also fixes latent bug theta-sensitivity M-step d(V)/d(theta) read -sized per-thread lhs buffer, residual-error variance depends structural parameter now contributes correct M-step gradient. est="vae" now runs multi-threaded. per-subject encoder forward pass exact branch--bound covariate M-step (previously serial, dominating EM covariate-selection phases) parallelized cores set vaeControl(rxControl=rxode2::rxControl(cores=)) (defaulting rxode2::getRxThreads()), joining already-threaded decoder solve. encoder forward pass covariate branch--bound bit-identical single-threaded run. encoder backward (gradient) pass also parallelized default (vaeControl(parEncoderBackward=TRUE)); cross-subject sum reduced parallel bit-identically, deterministic fixed cores differs slightly serial path. note added fit’s $runInfo active. bit-identical, fully reproducible results set options(nlmixr2.identical=TRUE) (flips default serial) vaeControl(parEncoderBackward=FALSE). SAEM Louis stochastic-approximation FIM (covMethod="sa") importance-sampling Monte-Carlo observed information (covMethod="imp") longer tied est="saem"/est="imp". can now requested covMethod mixed-effects estimation method (computed post-fit converged estimates) switched onto completed fit setCov(fit, "sa") / setCov(fit, "imp"). (population-NLM family random effects, sa/imp apply .) Several estimation families changed default covMethod now covariance can applied mixed-effects method: FOCEI family (focei/foce/laplace/agq) now defaults "r,s" sandwich ("analytic"); est="vae" now defaults "r,s" ("analytic"); est="nlme" now keeps nlme’s covariance ("nlme") default; nonparametric family (npag/npb) now defaults importance-sampling covariance ("imp"). est="saem" ("sa"), importance-sampling family ("imp"), NLM family ("r"/optimizer Hessian), est="advi" ("advi") fo/foi (covariance) keep previous defaults. vaeControl(bnbStrategy=) selects frontier discipline exact branch--bound covariate selection est="vae": "lifo" (default, existing last--first-depth-first search), "fifo" (first--first-) "lc" (least cost / best-first). solver exact, selected covariates identical every strategy; search order differs. est="vae" can now estimate structural population parameters random effect (mu-referenced). Previously theta frozen ini() value VAE estimates parameters latent space. vaeControl(nonMuTheta=) selects treatment: "regress" (default, matching saemControl(nonMuTheta=)) injects eta estimates theta directly bounded bobyqa regression FOCEi inner likelihood every M-step (bounds ini() lower/upper, blended M-step gain), recovering -random-effect population parameter without adding spurious random effect. eta-injection alternatives estimate theta + mean(eta) (temporary eta dropped output model): "eta" estimates injected omega typical value; "fix" holds injected omega typical-value theta fixed ini() values (nothing parameter estimated, appear iteration table); "none" keeps old freeze behavior. $runInfo note lists parameters converted. analytic observed-information covariance now preferred covMethod across mixed-model estimation methods, falling back method’s previous default model analytic scope: est="saem" keeps stochastic-approximation FIM ("sa") default covMethod, now followed "analytic" "linFim". covMethod="analytic" computes FOCEI analytic covariance converged SAEM estimates falls back linearized FIM ("linFim") message scope positive definite; "linFim" covariance stays selectable via setCov(fit, "linFim"). est="nlme" gains covMethod argument (c("analytic", "r,s", "r", "s", "nlme", ""), default "analytic") recomputes covariance converged nlme estimates; "nlme" keeps nlme’s standard errors (also available via setCov(fit, "nlme")). est="npag"/"npb" (m/variants), previously reported covariance, now compute one post-fit converged estimates (default "analytic" finite-difference fallback chain). est="imp"/"impmap"/"qrpem" gain covMethod argument (c("imp", "analytic", "r,s", "r", "s", ""), default "imp"). "imp" Monte-Carlo importance-sampling covariance old impCov=TRUE selected (impCov argument removed); tokens compute post-fit FOCEI covariance. est="advi" keeps variational covariance ("advi") default now honors explicit covMethod (e.g. "analytic") without overwriting variational covariance. setCov()/getVarCov() accept covMethod="analytic" post-fit. general FOCE-family per-subject log-likelihood can now built rxode2 UI model used outside fit, MCMC/SAMBA-style algorithms (issue #414). foceiLikLoad() compiles inner model sets problem (including data) memory, foceiLikRun() evaluates individual log-likelihood supplied population parameter vector eta matrix – parallel per subject – foceiLikUnload() frees . likelihood type may "focei" (interaction), "focep" (FOCE+) "foce" (NONMEM-style), foceiLikRun(type=) selects individual joint density log p(y_i, eta_i) ("joint", default) conditional data log-likelihood log p(y_i | eta_i) alone ("cond"). one likelihood system may loaded time; loading second errors first unloaded. fit$etaCI returns per-subject confidence intervals individual’s eta, complementing existing fit$etaSE fit$etaRSE. intervals empirical-Bayes estimate plus/minus normal quantile times eta standard error, using fit’s ci level (default 0.95). Like etaSE, requires CWRES fit (add addCwres() non-focei methods). est="agq" now supports analytic outer gradient (agqControl(fast=TRUE)), previously available FOCEi family. AGQ objective FOCEi objective one term swapped – l(etahat) becomes log(sum_k a_k) quadrature nodes, log det, Omega tbs terms unchanged – gradient reuses sensitivity solve adds node terms plus tr(Ht^-1 dHt/dp) node placement. FOCEi replaces finite-difference outer gradient, exact rather difference approximation costs one augmented solve instead one extra solve per parameter. quadrature nodes solve cheaper 1st-order model eta-hat point needs (never read 2nd-order block), node cost goes grid grows. Requires interaction=TRUE; fit use falls back finite differences rather failing. covType="analytic" now covers est="agq" well (previously declined nAGQ > 1 fell back finite-difference covariance). AGQ observed information FOCEi one single term swapped, log det half reused unchanged data half becomes expectation quadrature nodes plus covariance score contributions. nAGQ=1 reduces FOCEi observed information exactly, FOCEi Laplace results unchanged. Validated finite-difference oracle (tight ODE tolerance, Richardson extrapolation): AGQ standard errors agree oracle’s noise floor. gradient, model outside scope – general multi-endpoint residual variance, censoring, IOV, finite agqLow/agqHi clamp, cholSECov=TRUE, interaction=FALSE – reports keeps finite-difference covariance. Requesting unsupported est= method (e.g. typo) now prints available estimation methods grouped category (Linearized, Integral approximation, Stochastic EM, Nonparametric, Machine learning, Optimizer (NLM family)) short description , instead single flat list. Calling nlmixr2() arguments prints grouped list (invisibly returns ). new nlmixr2AllEstType() returns information data frame, built-method carries type description attributes (e.g. attr(nlmixr2Est.focei, "type")) third-party methods can set join list. est="npag"/est="npb" now PIN current ODE solve residual-error (err) parameter optimization instead re-integrating. parameters change prediction f, subject’s states cached posterior etas ODE frozen (op_focei.freezeOde) r recomputed – mixture frozen recompute reuses component’s cached states rather re-solving . structural regressor (move f, including estimated per-component clearance) still re-solves. Results unchanged. SAEM mixture models now fix per-subject membership default (saemControl(mixProbMethod="regress"), new default): subject hard-classified best component , held fixed, soft-EM responsibility step skipped (reusing existing responsibility-weighted machinery via 0/1 mixWeights). avoids soft-EM collapse (component running away degenerate value) lower-bias well-separated overlapping component evaluations; heavily overlapping components can higher-variance, previous soft-EM behavior remains available mixProbMethod="regularized". membership fixed, S-step solves subject component (per-subject mixest regressor) instead running one MCMC chain per component – roughly nMix-fold reduction ODE solves per iteration. Split-ETA mixtures (separate eta per component, start symmetric must differentiate fit) automatically fall back soft-EM (regularized). SAEM warm-starts residual-error parameters observed per-endpoint moments initial predictions (additive SD sqrt(mean(err^2)), proportional SD sqrt(mean((err/f)^2))), moment estimate est="npag"/est="npb" use – saemControl(residWarmStart=TRUE), default. SAEM forms unconverged population prediction, proportional moment excludes near-zero predictions (-subject variability dominates) warm-started value clamped sane multiple ini value. Set residWarmStart=FALSE start ini residual values. mixture models warm-start disabled (poor population initial fit inflate residual stop components separating). proportional residual moment used warm-start est="npag"/est="npb" (now SAEM) guards near-zero prediction: ratio err / (abs(f) <= 1e-6 ? 1 : f), f (near) zero longer blows proportional moment. SAEM now estimates population theta parameters associated random effect (SAEM phi0 fixed effects) bounded direct optimization observation likelihood iteration – saemControl(nonMuTheta="regress"), now DEFAULT – keeping plain directly-estimated regressors instead stochastic phi0 draws shrinking variance. optimization uses robust coordinate descent within local trust region, honoring theta’s ini-block bounds, holds phi0 fixed optimizer owns . simulated model three -eta thetas (ka, V, Hill power) recovered far accurately old handling (e.g. absorption theta RMSE dropped ~16x), extra runtime (objective re-solves ODE). previous behavior available saemControl(nonMuTheta="eta"). mixture models falls back stochastic phi0 block (direct optimizer partition per-component structural theta subject membership). est="npag"/est="npb" now ESTIMATE mixture (mix()) model’s component structural parameters (e.g. per-subpopulation clearance) instead holding initial values. residual/regressor step optimizes exact mixture negative log-likelihood -sum_i log(sum_m a_m exp(cll_m)) (NONMEM7 eq 1.182), marginalizing components current proportions a_m (proportion update step moves); per-component conditional log-likelihood carries -0.5*log(r) penalty, additive residual collapse. Verified: two-subpopulation clearance model recovers component clearances mixing proportion, non-zero additive SD. per-endpoint residual moment warm start now attributes observation endpoint via new rxode2 accessor (getIndCmt, reading CMT time-varying covariate), multi-endpoint model warm-starts endpoint’s residual moment. Requires matching rxode2 (function-pointer table index 82). est="npag"/est="npb" now estimate residual-error parameters EXTENDED LEAST SQUARES individual predictions instead marginal likelihood. marginal likelihood flexible nonparametric support rewards vanishing residual (support point can fit subjects arbitrarily well), residual drift toward zero. residual step now minimizes exact conditional normal negative log-likelihood sum_obs(0.5*(f-dv)^2/r + 0.5*log(r) + 0.5*log(2*pi)) posterior-mean etas (equivalently extended least squares – minimizer) – 0.5*log(r) term penalizes r -> 0, giving saem/focei residual (e.g. theophylline add.sd ~ 0.73, prop.sd ~ 0.15) rather collapsed one. variance-scale parameter warm-started (, single scale per endpoint, set) saem-style per-endpoint moment: additive SD sqrt(mean(err^2)), proportional SD sqrt(mean((err/f)^2)), transform--sides scale (lognormal / box-cox handled transformed residual). non-mu structural “regressor” optimized step, posterior-mean etas re-derived per candidate eta grid stale-absorb structural shift (identifies , e.g. recovering theophylline’s clearance deliberately-wrong start). residual + regressor thetas converge, final adaptive-grid pass re-optimizes support thetas held CONSTANT, support nonparametric MLE mixing distribution fitted residual D(F) global-optimality certificate restored (~0). npb runs residual/regressor step inside sampler. (mix() model’s structural component parameters held initial values – ELS step mixture-aware; components handled mixture marginalization proportion update.) est="npag" now picks initial grid size automatically model’s dimensionality npagControl(points=) supplied: max(2028, 512 * n_eta) (2028 Pmetrics NPAG default, covers low-dimensional model grows sparse can collapse high dimensions). Theophylline (3 etas) resolves 2028 (matching Pmetrics); warfarin (8 etas) 4096. Supply points override. est="npag" robust high-dimensional models (many etas), validated golden comparison Pmetrics NPAG Warfarin PK/PD model (transit absorption + Emax turnover, 8 parameters): per-cycle Psi build now per-row log-sum-exp normalized non-gamma path , hard subject’s conditional density underflow whole row zero (aborted condensation); Burke interior-point solve ridges Newton matrix retries instead aborting ill-conditioned; npagControl() exposes gridWidth gridBounds ("auto"/"ini"/"") bounded, high-dimensional grid can focused plausible region (unbounded box collapses support). numerically transparent well-conditioned fits (normalization restores exact objective; Burke weights scale-invariant). est="npag"/est="npb" now estimate non-mu structural fixed-effect parameters (theta eta, e.g. ke <- exp(tke), npag’s grid otherwise cover – covers mu-referenced residual/likelihood parameters). default optimized “regressors” residual step: bounded bobyqa moves alongside residual parameters, re-solving ODE per candidate (feed states, ODE freeze turned step). identifies sharply – e.g. recovering theophylline’s clearance deliberately-wrong start, bimodal mixture proportion (p1 = 0.70) grid alternative recovered weakly. opt-npagControl(muExpand=TRUE) instead uses saem-style mu-expansion: inject pseudo-eta (ke <- exp(tke + eta.tke)), grid-estimate, recover fixed effect finalization (support-mean folded theta, injected random effect collapsed; injected eta carries FIXED omega, excluded free omega objective like IOV, also works mixture models). residOptimize="none" holds structural regressors together residual parameters. (non-mu-referenced ETA – eta paired theta – needs neither: npag box already covers every eta, grid dimension estimated pure random effect.) est="npag" now supports generalized (non-normal) / user-ll() likelihoods. nonparametric objective sums inner per-observation llikObs, non-normal endpoint exactly user’s log-likelihood, objective already correct; residual/likelihood parameters (e.g. Student-t’s degrees freedom, iniDf$err non-NA) estimated frozen-ODE bounded step residual parameters. Freezing ODE step valid every optimized parameter feeds post-solve f/r alone (err-tagged) – non-err parameter ever enters optimized set step re-solves instead. gamma forced (non-normal endpoint r == 1). non-mu-referenced structural fixed-effect parameter placed grid held initial value, reported fit’s $runInfo. est="npb" handles non-normal endpoints (Gibbs sweep sums llikObs). est="npb" now runs residual/regressor optimization (previously held residual-error non-mu structural “regressor” thetas initial values sampled mixing distribution). sampled mixing distribution held fixed, bounded bobyqa step npag uses fits residual thetas (add/prop/ lnorm/lambda/ar) structural regressor – recovering, e.g., theophylline’s clearance deliberately-wrong start. npbControl(residOptimize=) selects : "alternate" (default) re-fits burn-holds thetas fixed sampling phase (every collected draw shares converged residual scale), "final" fits converged draw, "none" holds initial values. Unlike npag, npb optimize assay-error multiplier (gamma) – residual thetas fit directly. est="npag" est="npb" now support mixture (sub-population) mix() models. subject split per-component pseudo-subjects conditional likelihood marginalized components using mixture proportions (p(y_i | phi) = sum_m mixProb_m * p(y_i | phi, component m)). npag updates estimated proportion cycle EM step (support points weights held fixed); npb samples proportions inside blocked Gibbs sweep – subject draws component posterior responsibility proportions drawn Dirichlet(1 + component counts), posterior-mean proportions reported $env$npbMixProb. fix()ed proportion held ini value . est="npb" now supports multiple independent chains (npbControl(nchains=)): stick-breaking Gibbs sampler runs per chain (seed offset per chain), posterior-mean draws pooled, Gelman-Rubin R-hat per eta reported $env$npbRhat (~1 convergence; > ~1.1 flags non-convergence). est="npb" faster: two per-sweep loops re-solve ODE serially (support-location Metropolis-Hastings step, mixture-proportion Gibbs step mix() models) now solve per-subject conditional likelihoods parallel subjects, matching already-parallel Psi build. proposal accept/reject draws stay serial original order, fixed-seed fit bit--bit identical regardless thread count. est="npag" faster: longer redundant full conditional-density build first cycle (degeneracy check now reads working build’s per-subject maxima), one-time D(F) global-optimality scan smaller default configurable via npagControl(dfScan=) (-1 auto, 0 skip certificate, explicit scan size). Neither change affects fitted support, Omega, thetas, objective. npagControl(cores=) npbControl(cores=) set number threads used parallel per-subject conditional-likelihood solves. default (NULL) uses current rxode2 thread count (rxode2::getRxThreads()); integer sets thread count fit restores afterwards. est="saem" now fits general log-likelihood (ll() ~ expr) models saemix way (model returns per-observation loglik; standard MCMC kernels use -ll observation loss). solve event data keeps DV model references (previously dropped, likelihood solve errored “parameter(s) required solving: DV”); fixed-effect-(phi0) parameters optimized bounded bobyqa honoring ini-block bounds (likelihood SD stays non-negative). Normal-endpoint saem unchanged. Nonparametric engines (cont.): est="npag" optimizes residual parameters bounded minqa::bobyqa, honoring ini-block lower/upper bounds residual parameter (e.g. additive SD stays >= 0, AR correlation (-1,1)). unbounded optimizer wander invalid region, newuoa / nelder-mead longer used residual step (residType control removed). SAEM general log-likelihood: fixed-effect-(phi0) refinement step (saemix “ind.fix10”, distribution=general) now optimized derivative-free optimizers residual step (nelder-mead / newuoa, selected type) instead L-BFGS-B – model emits analytic d(ll)/d(phi0), previous finite-difference-gradient L-BFGS pure overhead. phi0 enter ODE, states solved held fixed phi0 optimized (ODE-freeze), evaluation recomputing log-likelihood. SAEM-side L-BFGS plumbing (phi0 gradient, trampolines, lbfgs* config) removed; FOCEI’s outerOpt="lbfgsb" unaffected. Nonparametric engines (cont.): npag residual-parameter optimization now freezes ODE states – inner likelihood solves (support point, subject) re-evaluates output f/r candidate residual theta, skipping (costly) re-integration. Results identical full re-solve; combined-error theo fit ~35% faster, much models expensive ODEs. Exposed general freezeOde option inner likelihood (default, engines bit-identical). Nonparametric engines (cont.): est="npag" now estimates residual-error parameters generally. single variance-scale parameter (pure additive proportional) handled fast gamma /search folded theta; anything else – combined additive+proportional (add/prop ratio single gamma recover), multiple endpoints (add.sd/prop.sd), transform (boxCox/yeoJohnson lambda) autocorrelation (ar) parameters – optimized nonparametric objective support points weights held fixed, using optimizers SAEM (residType: "newuoa" default, "nelder-mead"), gamma warm start. residOptimize control selects "alternate" (default, every cycle), "final" (converged support), "none" (hold ini). simulated two-endpoint model npag recovers add.sd1=0.20 add.sd2=1.47 (truth 0.20 / 1.50), matching FOCEI, single global gamma forced equal; simulated AR(1) data (true ar1.cor=0.6) recovers ~0.54 0 start gradient FOCEI stalls ar1.cor=0 saddle. reported residual reflects estimate. Note: support distribution flexible can absorb additive residual scatter, additive term combined error model may smaller parametric fit (documented ?npagControl). Nonparametric engines (cont.): npag/npb conditional likelihood now folds transform--sides (dTBS) per-observation Jacobian, lnorm, boxCox, yeoJohnson residual models handled correctly lambda-type transform parameters estimable. Proportional combined additive + proportional error supported, global-optimality certificate D(F) now evaluated fitted gamma (reaches ~0 proportional/combined models gamma optimization ). model whose transform link sees non-positive prediction (e.g. lnorm observation prediction now raises clear error instead Armadillo empty-matrix crash. Nonparametric engines (cont.): npag/npb engines now support fixed parameters. Fixed population thetas (including fixed residual parameters add.sd <- fix(0.7)) held ini value. Fixed-Omega etas – example fixed inter-occasion variance iov.ka ~ fix(0.05) | occ – remain support-point dimensions keep variance held fixed value instead estimated, IOV models fit. Nonparametric engines (cont.): est="npag" now reports global-optimality certificate D(F) ($env$npagDF; ~0 certifies nonparametric maximum likelihood), records per-cycle parameter-history trace shared scale.h printer ($parHistData), installs reported Omega masked model’s sparsity correlated-eta models keep -diagonal terms. AR(1) transform--sides / structured residual models supported (residual enters f + sqrt(r)*eps, structure carried r flows conditional likelihood). Validation: bimodal-recovery test confirms est="npag" recovers two-subpopulation (fast/slow absorption) parameter distribution – modes carry substantial weight recovered cluster means land near simulated truth – defining nonparametric capability single-mode parametric random-effect model reproduce. est="npb" (nonparametric Bayes) now usable engine: truncated stick-breaking Dirichlet-process mixture sampled blocked Metropolis-within-Gibbs sampler (cluster assignments, stick weights, MH support locations). reuses conditional-likelihood primitive npag returns nlmixr2FitData posterior mixing distribution ($env$npbSupport/npbWeights), per-subject posterior-mean etas, posterior draws population mean (npbMeanDraws) Bayesian credible intervals. npbControl() exposes points (truncation K), alpha, burnin, nsamp, propSd, seed. (Gelman-Rubin multi-chain convergence follow-.) est="npag" now usable engine: returns standard nlmixr2FitData object nonparametric population summary (mean + variance mapped reported theta/Omega), per-subject posterior-mean etas, discrete support-point distribution attached fit ($env$npagSupport, npagWeights, npagPosteriorEta, npagGamma, npagNspp). npagControl() exposes points, cycles, gammaOptimize. (reported Omega uses support-point variances; correlated-Omega models global-optimality certificate follow-ups.) Nonparametric engines (cont.): added residual-error magnitude (gamma) optimization inside NPAG cycle (per-cycle /search). Gamma scales residual variance inside FOCEi inner likelihood, censoring (BLQ/ALQ via M3 censored likelihood – normal tail probability /limit) transform--sides handled correctly scaled error. objective uses log-sum-exp row normalization numerical stability. Generalized (non-normal) likelihoods supported rejected error. Note: npag/npb objective nonparametric marginal log-likelihood comparable NONMEM/FOCEI -2LL. Nonparametric engines (cont.): assembled NPAG adaptive-grid cycle (Yamada Alg 1) – Sobol grid, Psi, Burke IPM, weight/QR condensation, adaptive-grid expansion (npExpandGrid), eps/F convergence controller. Runs end--end Theophylline (exposed npagCycle_ ahead full fit-object wiring). Nonparametric engines (cont.): added Sobol initial grid (npSobolGrid), weight-threshold QR rank-revealing condensation (npCondenseWeights / npCondenseQR), eta-space support-point box (.npEtaBox, control-selectable via gridBounds/gridWidth). Nonparametric engines (cont.): added conditional-likelihood primitive (npEvalCondLik) parallel Psi-matrix builder (npBuildPsi), reusing FOCEi inner solve residual-error models, transform--sides censoring carry unchanged. Scaffolding two native nonparametric estimation engines, est="npag" (nonparametric adaptive grid) est="npb" (nonparametric Bayes), plus mu-referenced sugar variants mnpag/inpag mnpb/inpb (OLS IRLS covariate M-step). reuse FOCEI inner likelihood machinery; estimation loop runs C++. algorithm added subsequent releases (drivers currently report estimation yet implemented). Fix covariance matrix ($cov) bounded-parameter fit run unbounded method (e.g. saem): internal rxBoundedTr.<name> name leaked $cov back-transform Jacobian applied , reported standard errors internal (transformed) scale. $cov (stashed full theta+Omega covariance) now renamed original parameter names Jacobian-corrected; Omega residual terms untransformed pass unchanged. nlm parameter-history machinery can now driven external optimizer. nlmerSolveGrad() gains record argument logs evaluation’s population parameter estimate (per-subject mean phi columns) resident scale, nlmGetParHist() now exported externally-optimized engine (e.g. babelmixr2’s nlmer, driven lme4::nlmer) can recover accumulated parameter history .nlmFreeEnv(). new optional showOfv field nlm solve control hides objective column engines (record parameters ).

title: nlmixr2est 7.0.0
text: est = "advi" (adviControl()): automatic differentiation variational inference (Kucukelbir et al. 2017), mean-field block full-rank family, point-estimate full-Bayes mode. variational gradient comes FOCEi forward sensitivities whole optimization runs one C++ call, reproducibly independent thread count. est = "impmap" est = "imp" (impmapControl() / impControl()): importance-sampling EM style NONMEM METHOD=IMP, E-step proposal subject’s MAP mode (impmap) running conditional mean (imp). Supports mu-referenced, mixture, bounded fix()ed models; reported objective FOCEi evaluation EM estimate. Quasi-random (Sobol) importance sampling (qr=, Leary & Dunlavey 2012) SIR M-step acceleration (sir=) available stay thread-count independent. est = "qrpem" (qrpemControl()): sugar impmap EM qr = TRUE sir = TRUE. Mu-referenced FOCEI family: mfocei/ifocei, mfoce/ifoce, mfocep/ifocep, magq/iagq, mlaplace/ilaplace (matching *Control() functions). Mu-referenced population covariate-coefficient thetas profiled outer optimizer -C++ OLS (m*) IRLS (*) regression; bounded mu parameters regression-updated clamped step. New foceiControl() options muModel, muRefCovAlg, muModelTol, muModelMaxCycles, muModelClampRetries. focep/mfocep/ifocep: foce/mfoce/ifoce methods foce = "foce+" forced. *f convenience methods (focef, foceif, focepf mu/irls variants): base method foceiControl(fast = TRUE) default.

title: nlmixr2est 7.0.0
text: foceiControl(fast = TRUE): analytic FOCEI/FOCE outer gradient Almquist sensitivity equations, solved subjects one threaded rxode2 solve; --scope models fall back finite differences. Covers censored M2/M3/M4, estimated boxCox/yeoJohnson lambda, matExp()/indLin(), foce+, modeled dosing (f()/lag()/rate()/dur()), mu-referenced covariate reuse. fast outer optimizer defaults lbfgsb3c mceta defaults -2 (Eq-48 warm-start next inner problem). covMethod = "analytic" (folding old covType): exact analytic observed-information covariance FOCEI/FOCE matching NONMEM $COV MATRIX=R, covering additive/proportional/combined error, censored M2/M3/M4 (censOption = "gauss"), estimated lambda, foce+, matExp()/indLin(), mu-referenced/covariate parameters; --scope fits fall back finite-difference sandwich. covFull = TRUE (now default) reports full theta + residual + Omega covariance analytic finite-difference methods, Omega rows named random effect (om.eta.cl / cov.eta.cl.eta.v). covMethod = "r,s" true sandwich solve(Rfull) %*% Sfull %*% solve(Rfull), "s" solve(Sfull), "r" solve(Rfull); covFull = FALSE keeps theta-shape. foceiControl(foce = c("nonmem", "foce+")): "nonmem" (default) freezes FOCE residual variance eta = 0 prediction match NONMEM; "foce+" keeps live conditional variance. foceiControl(censOption = c("gauss", "laplace")): censored (M2/M3/M4/BLQ) inner-Hessian treatment; "gauss" (default) matches common tools, "laplace" uses exact censored second derivative. foceiControl(warm = c("calc", "save")): "calc" (default) warm-starts n1qn1 inner problem eta Hessian recalculated current theta. sensMethod (nlm-family controls foceiControl()): forward -engine discrete adjoint ("adjoint") ODE parameter sensitivities; "default" reads getOption("nlmixr2est.adjoint"). Residual (error-model) parameters now included focei-family covariance (fixed, IOV mixture-probability thetas skip). Mixture (mix()) support focei/foce/fo/foi.

title: nlmixr2est 7.0.0
text: saemControl(covFull = TRUE) (default): full theta + residual + Omega covariance linearized FIM. New covMethod = "sa" (stochastic-approximation Fisher information, Kuhn & Lavielle 2005). parHistData records -diagonal Omega block covariances. saem fits general log-likelihood endpoints (ll(name) ~ <expr>, e.g. time--event); fixed-effect-parameters refined bounded L-BFGS-B (saemControl() gains lbfgsLmm/lbfgsFactr/lbfgsPgtol/ lbfgsMaxIter).

title: nlmixr2est 7.0.0
text: Matrix-exponential / inductive-linearization models estimate focei, nlm SAEM families, matching equivalent ODE model; compartments ordered source-first k_<>_<> graph default dosing placed correctly.

title: nlmixr2est 7.0.0
text: nonparametric eta-space outputs now carry eta names: est = "npag" support-point matrix (fit$env$npagSupport) posterior eta matrix (fit$env$npagPosteriorEta) get eta column names; est = "npb" two matrices plus posterior mean draws (fit$env$npbMeanDraws) get eta column names, per-eta R-hat vector (fit$env$npbRhat) gets eta row names. est = "npb" now prints per-sweep iteration history shared iteration printer (like every method) stores fit parHistData; sampler’s results unchanged (bit-identical). importance-sampling (covMethod = "imp") covariance step now shows progress bar finite-difference evaluations, like focei covariance step (shown iteration printing ). New vaeCovariates() returns covariates est = "vae" explore. New formatMinWidth() shorter $parFixed display; $parFixed rebuilt data.frame operations (#346, #516). estimators share one iteration printer (iterPrintControl(), src/scale.h) common row layout; analytic gradients tracked parHist type fit header reports gradient mu-model used, e.g. (outer: lbfgsb3c; grad: analytic; mu: irls). est = "vae" training runs entirely C++ (vaeTrainCpp_) reparameterizes inner problem place, fits substantially faster.

title: nlmixr2est 7.0.0
text: outerOpt = "nlminb" finite-difference methods ("lbfgsb3c" fast = TRUE), sigdig = 4, mceta = -2, censOption = "gauss", foce = "nonmem", covMethod = "analytic".

title: nlmixr2est 7.0.0
text: est="nlme" now honors sigdig ODE solver tolerances. reversed condition made nlmeControl() fall back atol=rtol=1e-4 whenever sigdig set (.e. always, since defaults 4) pass sigdig NULL; tolerances now derived sigdig like every method. Fixed FOCEi scaleC band guard corrupting est="vae" covariate selection. guard rescues genuinely-computed derivative-based scaling constant (> 0) now; uninitialized scaleC exactly 0 left usual min/max clamp instead overwritten |init|. overwrite broken VAE covariate discovery theophylline (covariates selected, betas collapsed 0). est="vae" covariateSelection=FALSE now estimates covariate coefficients written model – linear (beta*WT) transformed (beta*log(WT/70)) effects – rather holding ini() value. fit place regress M-step regardless nonMuTheta (previously fixed nonMuTheta="none" errored "fix"/"eta"); coefficient set ini(... ~ fix()) still stays fixed. est="impmap" now estimates non-mu structural residual-error thetas general (custom ll()) likelihood model. endpoint rx_pred_ log-likelihood rx_r_ 0, Gauss-Newton M-step skipped every observation (V<=0) left thetas frozen initial values; M-step now uses analytic d(ll)/d(theta) directly (empirical-Fisher information), raw ll() fit recovers parameters equivalent add() model. est="npag"/est="npb" longer error unused argument: 'dfScan' post-fit importance-sampling covariance recomputed (dfScan field leaked -converted foceiControl). est="npag"/est="npb" transform--sides (lnorm/log/box-Cox) endpoint whose model prediction non-positive observation (e.g. pre-dose observation structural prediction 0) now records note fit’s $runInfo instead silently fitting rxode2-floored value indication. est="vae" nonMuTheta="regress" now shows regressed non-mu-referenced thetas iteration table parameter history. M-step bobyqa regression already estimated , omitted printed parameter walk (latent-space thetas, omega, residual error shown), progress invisible; now appended row correct back-transform. est="vae" covariate selection longer silently selects nothing 32 candidate covariates. best-subset step enumerated 2^nCov subsets, undefined behavior nCov = 32 (1u << 32 wraps 1, empty model ever tried) intractable well . now uses exact branch--bound L0/BIC objective, returning identical optimum scaling dozen covariates. selection penalty now also follows reference implementation’s warmup ramp, tunable via vaeControl(covSelectAlpha=) (default 2, ramped 1 klWarmup iterations); ramp iterations labeled CovSel ramp iteration table. est="vae" longer errors replacement 0 rows data AMT column (dose-free datasets neonate weight data); rows now treated observations (EVID = 0). est="saem" longer dies argument length zero building SAEM model list. rxode2 versions omit ar column model’s predDf, SAEM autocorrelation helpers indexed column directly; now fall back iniDf (err == "ar") representation column absent. mu-referenced method-variant FOCEi fit (ifocei, mfocei, foce, focep, agq, laplace, *f fast variants ifoceif) needed restart – example zero/bad-gradient theta reset – died focei$control must focei control object. controls built foceiControl() reclassed class, carry "foceiControl" class vector, restart-path environment check rejected even though fit set valid control. check now recognises whole FOCEi control family. Models combine linCmt() ODEs (example solved PK driving effect-compartment ODE) now estimate correctly FOCEi nlm families; linear compartments solved ODEs methods. Previously sensitivity compartments methods add (one per eta FOCEi, one per theta nlm) shifted depot/central past compartment numbers data translated , dose silently landed sensitivity compartment, every prediction came back 0 objective function meaningless. Since model longer mixing solved system ODEs, fits now warn (recorded fit$runInfo) analytic linCmt() used. est="saem" never affected, keeps analytic linCmt() warn, linCmt() models ODE (#286). est="saem" longer estimates fix()ed theta eta attached ; parameter now stays initial estimate, already FOCEi family. direct phi0 optimization (nonMuTheta="regress", general-likelihood models) takes phi0 partway fit skips update restores fixed values, fixed non-mu-referenced theta drifted initial estimate. Estimates non-fixed parameters unchanged. foceiControl(freezeResidGrad=TRUE) (default) longer makes fit die “maximum number theta resets (10) exceeded”. base solve caches states/EBEs frozen gradient ran without gradient flag set, ETA-drift theta reset raised inside gradient restarted whole fit – every gradient, reset limit tripped (#641). model combines inter-occasion variability (IOV) term zero inter-individual variability eta another parameter (example eta.ka ~ 0 alongside iov.cl ~ 0.1 | occ) longer fails “initial ‘omega’ matrix inverse non-positive definite”. IOV present omega per-condition list, zero-eta detector read eta names left zero eta matrix, making singular; zero eta now detected removed usual. Restoring original model fit also longer errors est="saem" (including table=list(cwres=TRUE)), IOV eta re-expressed per-occasion id-level etas (#627). est="saem" longer collapses subjects combine two dosing episodes overlapping clock times separated evid=4 reset – example crossover IV arm depot (f(depot)) arm share times. SAEM solves subject ODE solver’s internal time-sorted order, relocated reset ahead first episode’s observations merged two episodes one trajectory; SAEM reported nearly constant PRED grossly inflated residual (focei/posthoc already handled correctly). reset episodes now offset internally solve times increase within subject, matching rxSolve()/focei; predictions unchanged time-since-reset matters (#455). est="fo"/est="foi" linearization pass returned intermediate fit object empty control, .updateParFixed() silently fell back default table settings (ci/sigdigTable) instead fit’s control (#517). FO/FOI fit now carries control, intermediate fit without method-specific nmObjGetControl surfaces stored control rather returning NULL. est="nlme" now accepts common print control alias, nlmixr2(..., "nlme", list(print=0)) longer errors unused argument: 'print'. nlme prints verbose option, print maps (print=0 runs quietly, positive value verbose); explicit verbose still honored print supplied. FOCEi/FOCE models trigonometric term whose argument compound expression divided something (example sinusoidal enterohepatic-cycle release sin(2 * 3.14 * (time - mtime1) / period)) longer fail build “arguments function ‘sin’”. fix rxode2’s rxFromSE() (dropping whole argument, emitting sin()); regression test added (nlmixr2/nlmixr2est#513). FOCEi now estimates population parameter initialized exactly 0 (e.g. covariate effect additive term) instead leaving frozen starting value. default scaling constant 1/|initPar|, Inf initPar 0; clamped scaleCmax made parameter effectively unoptimizable. getScaleC() now falls back unit scaling initial estimate 0. single-subject / fixed-effect (“N 1”) model – one whose random effects fixed zero, dropped estimation – now gives actionable error method requires random effects (fo, foi, saem, nlme) used, pointing methods can fit (focei, foce, population method nlminb, bobyqa nls). error also keeps user’s original model name instead reporting internal .mod (issue #493). focei model whose predictions depend random effect (example y ~ dpois(rate) rate fixed population parameter rather model-predicted value) longer reports generic “Aborted calculation” message. underlying cause raised directly guidance linking endpoint’s distribution parameter eta-varying model quantity (#515). est="saem"’s “mis-match nbr endpoints model & data” error now actionable: reports number endpoints model versus data, lists observation compartments found data, points user check CMT/DVID values match number model endpoints (error terms). common case dataset extra DVID levels model matching endpoint (issue #579). est="advi" now rejects mixture (mix()) model front clear message (rxode2::assertRxUiNoMix) instead running wrong fit ignored mixture structure failed late output tables cryptic “probabilities mixture must sum number 0 1, sum : 0”.

title: nlmixr2est 7.0.0
text: setCov(fit, "analytic") longer silently installs (mislabels) "r,s" finite-difference covariance analytic covariance computed model; fit’s covariance left unchanged instead. fit$etaSE columns now labeled se(<eta>) (matching fit$etaRSE’s rse(<eta>)%); label previously applied matrix’s names() (-op) columns came back bare eta names. covMethod = "r"/"s"/"r,s" standard errors inflated constant factor (sqrt(2) "r", 2 "s") using 2*R^-1/4*S^-1; now match NONMEM $COV (#666). bounded-parameter fit unbounded method (e.g. saem) leaked internal rxBoundedTr.<name> $cov without back-transform Jacobian; $cov now renamed original parameters Jacobian-corrected. analytic FOCE/foce+ covariance longer falls bounds (dropped eta = 0 solve slots) finite-difference Hessian; general (f,R) covariance reports covMethod = "analytic" ("r"), foceiCovAnalytic()/getVarCov() reproduce instead falling back. Fixed segfault analytic covariance --scope models (augmented build freed fit’s solve finite-difference fallback ran), sign M2 upper-tail term censored inner gradient. mu-referenced/irls FOCEI-family fits (mfocei/ifocei/…) now report Condition#(Cov)/Condition#(Cor) $objDf; post-fit covariance install skipped fit tables rendered full-model covariance recomputed. Converting fit different covariance (setCov(), getVarCov()) now refreshes Condition#(Cov)/Condition#(Cor) eigen diagnostics newly installed covariance instead leaving previous method’s values place. SAEM covMethod = "fim" adds mu-block Hessian (indefinite / NaN SEs), "fim"/"sa" report -diagonal Omega combined residual SEs. Fixed covMethod = "linFim" SAEM covariance erroring single population/covariate parameter, cov2cor one-nonzero-diagonal Omega.

title: nlmixr2est 7.0.0
text: FOCEI fit hits theta reset restarts longer aborts Assertion 'fitEnv$etaObj$ID' failed: Must type 'integer', 'factor'. restart re-validated previous attempt’s etaObf, whose ID column factor original subject IDs; now coerced back integer genuinely non-converging fit reports real reason instead spurious assertion (#470). Fixed est = "agq" quadrature node scaling. adaptive Gauss-Hermite nodes placed without change--variable factor, increasing nAGQ converge marginal likelihood – converged wrong value (still better Laplace, objective looked reasonable). nodes Gauss-Hermite e^{-x^2} kernel integral e^{-z'z/2} kernel, belong sqrt(2) * chol(Ht)^-1 * x exp(x'x) untilt. fix objective converges exact marginal likelihood nAGQ grows. Every nAGQ > 1 objective value (standard errors derived ) changes; focei/foce/fo/laplace unaffected. analytic covariance (covType = "analytic") now falls back finite differences cholSECov = TRUE: covariance step re-factors eta Hessian generalized Cholesky, non-positive-definite Ht differs chol() analytic observed information assumes. Fixed fast = TRUE analytic gradient models whose residual variance depends prediction (prop, add+prop, combined1, pow, add+pow): determinant chain-rule aliasing injected spurious term. Fixed fast = TRUE analytic gradient/covariance random effect shared across parameters, enabled sensitivity reuse covariate eta-less parameter, fixed gradient never used live (read finalize-state silently fell back finite differences). Fixed FOCE (interaction = FALSE) objective empirical-Bayes estimates: residual variance now supplied eta = 0 prediction, ODE linCmt() FOCE agree match NONMEM reference. Bounded Shi (2021) finite-difference step curvature-free search can longer corrupt shared solver state. Fixed muModel = "lin"/"irls" erroring two covariate expressions (#711) user-fixed covariate-coefficient regression bias. Fixed impmapControl(impSeed = ) ignored. FOCEI now updates additive mu-referenced population parameters large-magnitude initial estimates (#641). FOCEI theta resets now keep every reset population parameter inside bounds instead restarting optimization range, stop informative error parameter’s bounds infeasible (#454).

title: nlmixr2est 7.0.0
text: Fixed Windows heap-corruption segfault one core (rxode2 saw every worker thread 0); inner loops now pass real thread id. Fixed segfault est = "vae" (thread count capped solve’s core count) nlmSetup first estimator call session. Fixed FOCEi aborting Cube::slice(): index bounds mceta >= 1 maxInnerIterations == 0, heap-buffer overflow / wrong back-transform SAEM Box-Cox residual models. non-positive-definite Omega projected nearest PD matrix (SAEM mid-run, fit$runInfo warning; sym-inv-chol setup degenerate fit) residual/table diagnostics still run; NPDE degenerate simulated covariance sets subject’s NPDE NA instead aborting. Fixed segfault dataset observed subject (every subject placeholder EVID==0 row, aggregate-data output eval babelmixr2/admixr2). -observation-subject drop now keeps rows observed subject fall back , foceiSetup_ longer reads empty id vector bounds. .nlmSetupEnv() also now supplies default iterPrintControl external caller omits , instead erroring Index bounds: [index='iterPrintControl'].

title: nlmixr2est 7.0.0
text: models without etas, BSV(SD) Shrink(SD)% columns longer added $parFixed $parFixedDf; always blank models (#355). Model-defined variables (e.g. ka, cl, v, tad, dosenum, user-added line WT.<- WT) now included output table whether cwres requested. Previously tableControl(cwres=FALSE) dropped columns cwres=TRUE (default) kept , model produced different output columns depending residual request (#497). zero-fixed eta (e.g. bsva ~ 0) restored fitted model’s ini()/model() blocks estimation makes nested nlmixr2() call (e.g. adding focei objective CWRES), fit |> ini(bsva ~ 0.1) works; nested call used wipe restore info held global (#741). augPred() now works focei fit whose model zero-fixed eta appears prediction (e.g. eta.v ~ 0 used ODE residual), instead erroring parameter(s) required solving: eta.v; simulation model drops zero eta consistently saem (#514). laplace/agq family fits label $objDf row Laplace/AGQ<n> (matching $ofvType) instead FOCEi; previously default interaction=TRUE made interaction label win quadrature one. quadrature objective stays active one CWRES; setOfv(fit, "focei") (addCwres()) now evaluate true focei objective quadrature fit instead re-labeling quadrature value. Restored Function Val. objective column $parFixed shrinkage coloring; periodic headers now repeat column labels. $parFixed honors user sigdig/ci fits literally-fixed parameters. Literally-fixed population parameters now report back-transformed value (exp/expit/probitInv) Back-transformed column instead raw log/logit-scale estimate. augPred() now keeps fit’s original subject ids: returned id factor carries actual (character/factor) ids fit instead internal integer re-numbering (#450). vpcSim(fit, pred=TRUE) (hence VPC plots pred line) now works models IOV. IOV fit’s omega list matrices (id plus one per occasion level), pred path treated single matrix errored invalid 'times' argument; population prediction now zeros every random effect across omega levels (#629). fit$time attributes model build/compile setup/configure (nlm family times setup/optimize) instead . Aggregated ODE-solve warnings report real subject id; parHistData shows mixture-probability parameters natural scale fit$mixList returns components; iteration printing labels estimation phase (Burn / KL anneal/EM/Smooth vae, SA/EM saem). fast = TRUE linCmt() model downgrades fast = FALSE message instead silently falling back per gradient call. est = "vae" automatic covariate selection now reports selected covariate coefficients (beta_<par>_<cov>) $parFixed/$parFixedDf instead dropping population parameter fixed, covariate-bearing mu-parameters back-transform (exp) instead printing raw log scale. est = "vae" longer errors find parameter 'NA' structural (mu-referenced) parameter fixed fix(); random effect kept (variance estimated) fixed value carried model.

title: nlmixr2est 7.0.0
text: SAEM longer errors data ID dose-subject; observation-less subjects dropped estimation re-inserted output population PRED NA individual columns, like FOCEi (#687). FOCEi longer errors 'names' attribute [n] must length vector [m] subject’s records removed data translation (e.g. every TIME NA). subject vanishes processed data entirely rather losing observations, now detected dropped subject index alongside observation-less subjects (#606). Fixed nlmControl() listing eventSens/sensMethod twice. “initial ETAs nudged” warning fires nudge actually happened, non-default mceta fully mu-referenced model falls back default warning. saemControl(covMethod = "") (skip covariance) longer errors.

title: nlmixr2est 7.0.0
text: Removed unreachable duplicate missingTable default assignment nlmixr2Est0() (issue #385); earlier default already fixes value, second block never run. change fit results. Removed last bare Rf_error call C++ sources (issue #632): Rcpp::compileAttributes() output now emits parenthesized (Rf_error) form, internal rxError macro switched (Rf_error) well, package longer trips Rcpp’s upcoming Rf_error deprecation warning (RcppCore/Rcpp#1247). C .Call entry-point validators keep justified Rf_errorcall uses. Consolidated data preparation nlm-family control/fit functions, analytic-covariance augmented model now uses rxode2’s chunked rxOptExpr(); change fit results. test suite runs single testthat worker CI/CRAN parallel elsewhere, within-solve threads capped 2 CRAN.

title: nlmixr2est 6.1.0
text: Added focei, foce, foi, fo mixture support nlmixr2est Fix focei mixture models llik residual distributions erroring model exactly one mixture probability parameter Fix fit$mixList returning first mixture component parHistData Back-Transformed rows now show mixture probability parameters natural probability scale (0, 1) instead raw mlogit estimation scale. Fix issue 641: FOCEI now updates additive mu-referenced population parameters whose initial estimates large magnitude. Previously missing branch .foceiOptEnvSetupScaleC() let scaleC fall C++ default 1/|init|, mapped unit steps scaled space negligible steps unscaled space effectively pinned parameters initial value (e.g. tvemax <- -40 transform). model estimation fails, errors raised run now collected reported together, instead last error. supported new collectErr argument internal .collectWarn() helper, captures errors alongside warnings returns error element result list. result, errors hidden .exit({rxode2::rxProgressAbort()}) handlers (“Aborted calculation” message reported issue 607) longer mask underlying cause; inner stop message follow-error .exit now reported user. parameters natural probability scale instead raw mlogit scale. parameters natural probability scale Hardened mixture-model (mix()) estimation: clearer errors est="nlme" invalid initial probabilities, warnings underflowing/collapsing mixture probabilities, fix SAEM omega-diagonal floor raised outside mixture fits Fix segfault nlmSetup first estimator call fresh R session pooled estimators Guard null pointer arithmetic inner.cpp Use OpenMP threading S matrix calculation Use OpenMP threading calculating NPDEs

title: nlmixr2est 6.0.1
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-03 Fix LTO violation requested CRAN adding -DARMA_DONT_USE_OPENMP PKG_CXXFLAGS src/Makevars.Require rxode2 5.1.2 fixed M1-san issues observed .

title: nlmixr2est 6.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-05-31 focei, foce, fo, laplace, agq successfully made thread safe parallelized (single CPU). default tolerance relaxation difficult solve ODEs changed per individual instead entire population (breaking change, major release). allow precision majority subjects optimization process. Add predict(fit, level="ipred"), predict(fit, level="individual") predict(fit, level=1) predict individual fits (possibly new dataset). Change test files .rds files Drop magrittr %>% favor |>. Breaking change: Minimum R version increased 4.0 4.1.0. change required support native pipe operator |>. Users R < 4.1.0 need upgrade R install version nlmixr2est. Bug fixes deparsing nlmixr2 control objects nlm related pooled methods now run parallel (based ID) Tests optimized reduce redundant fits run parallel. nlm (related pooled optimizers: bobyqa, newuoa, uobyqa, n1qn1, lbfgsb3c, optim, nlminb) now support censoring behavior (M2/M3/M4) FOCEI SAEM. $censInformation field populated fits way FOCEI/SAEM. agqControl() laplaceControl() now rxUiDeparse() methods can saved better packages like nlmixr2save shinyMixR. Added new outerOpt; methods focei related methods (agq, laplace, foce, fo, foi): “uobyqa” “newuoa”. saem methods now respect bounds default internally adding appropriate transform applying back-transformation just returning. parameters mu-referenced, breaks mu-referencing. breaks mu-referencing warning issued. best practice still unbounded parameters mu-referencing. want ignore behavior may use control=list(boundedTransform=FALSE) saem control=saemControl(boundedTransform=FALSE) mu referencing covariate procedure made less fragile support mu referencing conjunction iov bounded parameter transformations. Add bench-marking capabilities small speed fixes focei/saem

title: nlmixr2est 5.0.0

title: nlmixr2est 4.1.1
text: CRAN release: 2025-10-09 Request nlmixr2est’s pre-processing hooks augPred(), vpcSim() $simInfo, fixes augPred cases etas=0 used nlmixr2 (#587) Fix scale.h scaleType="none" also require scaleTo=0 Request Armadillo 15 special flag new RcppArmadillo Fix focei without etas (without log-likelihood normal) run ELS (See #590). Change IOV implementation (#596): Now shows estimates CV% sd without shrinkage calculation. Allow different forms iov estimation, controlled iovXform. Retains iov parameter(s) output data.frame. iov, $omega shows list variability conditioning variable(s). fit$iov show IOV deviations conditioning variables(s) exception id IOV models can used estimation methods inherits ETA values. Added $etaMat method nlmixr2 fits give value needs passed estimation method (related iov #596)

title: nlmixr2est 4.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2025-08-29 Updated inferring estimation method control object. Requires control object class length one match estimation method. example foceiControl() assume estimation method related focei. Changed Rstudio completion evaluate (case gets turned data.frames) (See #568) Turned data completion items like $fitMergeInner Breaking change: Changed estimation method posthoc add tables calculate covariance default. now method ’s control, posthocControl(). previously default include interaction term (can turn posthocControl(interaction=TRUE)). Added foceControl(), foControl() foiControl() foce, fo foi methods, respectively. try convert related control structures correct control structure estimation method. Added iov support focei, foce, saem (#614) Added new estimation method agq uses adaptive Gauss-Hermite Quadrature fit nonlinear-mixed effect model. method, can choose number quadrature points estimate likelihood, higher numbers giving accurate likelihoods. AGQ implementation nlmixr2est allows specify number quadrature points via agqControl() function, supports single multiple subject models. method particularly useful models accurate likelihood estimation critical. Also added laplace method agq 1 node (numerically focei, foce log-likelihood focei/laplace, etc), uses agq routine. Fixed saem mu-reference display compressing internal item saem0.

title: nlmixr2est 4.0.2
text: CRAN release: 2025-07-24 loading unloading DLLs minimized version nlmixr2est. avoids loading/reloading DLLs causing CRAN mac m1 ASAN/USBAN false positive issue observed CRAN. Additionally new function nlmixr2fix(fit) added nlmixr2est. attempts make fit loaded different version nlmixr2 compatible nlmixr2 4.0. also prints versions nlmixr2 used creating fit. information likely find way use fit current session (old session). (Issue #562)

title: nlmixr2est 4.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2025-07-15 using model start new focei model, ETAs last fit used starting point. Now can use foceiControl(etaMat=NA) skip use eta=0 items. using foceiControl(etaMat=fit), extract ETAs fit use next optimization. using foceiControl(etaMat=) option nlmixr2 longer evaluates inner problem etaMat value. Add mceta option "focei". mceta=-1 default; eta restarts best eta last step start inner optimization. mceta=0 eta starts 0 start inner optimization. mceta=1 eta starts either 0 best eta, ever gives lowest objective function start inner optimization. mceta=n assumption omega sample n-1 eta values use lowest objective function eta sampled, last best eta eta=0 start inner optimization. Fix Rstudio print (issue #536) Support rxode2’s new +var() definition saem Support literal fixing residuals (#524). methods support literal fix residuals option literalFixRes defaults TRUE. get behavior older models can use literalFixRes=FALSE detailed error messages reported models errors

text: type(s) censoring () now stored fit$censInformation Standard errors $etas can now obtained fit$phiSE, also available fit$phiRSE (relative standard error), fit$phiH, (individual hessian), fit$phiC (individual covariances), fit$phiR (individual correlation matrices) Can also use Shi 2021 differences addition Gill differences. tests (using datasets CPT) produced worse estimates Gill 1983, though unclear since faster accurate method. modified version used calculating individual Hessians numerically generalized likelihood approach. Generalized likelihood estimation now present nlmixr2est focei, foce posthoc nmNearPD() function may use nearest positive definite matrix. derived Matrix::nearPD() implemented C/C++ used (possibly threaded) optimization. Individual Hessians can accessed $phiH, covariance $phiC, eta standard errors $phiSE eta RSEs can accessed $phiRSE. eta aliases well ($etaH, $etaC, $etaSE, $etaRSE). Can now access individual point’s contribution overall likelihood merging original dataset. merges can accessed $dataMergeFull, $dataMergeLeft, $dataMergeRight, $dataMergeInner. columns individual data column nlmixrLlikObs. calculate total focei/foce objective function, sum likelihoods still need adjusted omega/eta contribution, individual Hessians, possibly NONMEM objective function offset constant.

text: CRAN release: 2022-05-12 nlmixr2est contains estimation functions within nlmixr2.

title: nlmixr2est 2.0.6 – new package
text: Remove lower level foceiFit function. Focei, foce, fo, foi, posthoc now directly takes rxode2 ui objects New error types supported focei including mixing theta etas residual errors different types proportional errors Different types additive proportional errors can used endpoint using + combined1() + combined2() otherwise takes supplied addProp option figure type combined model run (default combined2()) Focei model cache now named focei-md5Digest.qs uses qs compression/saving/loading. foceiControl() aligned methods. foceiControl(adjLik=TRUE) uses NONMEM-style objective function throughout. foceiControl(adjLik=FALSE) uses adjusted objective function throughout, adjusts back NONMEM objective function. Lag time subject variability differences longer calculate ideal relative step size, absolute step size using Gill differences (default) Objective function checks infinite/NaN/NA values entire solving space ensures overflow occurs calculating inner hessian

title: nlmixr2est 2.0.6 – new package
text: mu referencing longer required saem; Internally non mu-referenced values converted mu referenced values converted back calculating nlmixr2 object. nlmixr2 forced parameter ordering (1) population effects, non mu-referenced subject effects (3) omega estimates (4) residual effects. changes order nlmixr2 sees parameters. Since based random number generator, optimization trajectory different different results nlmixr Components omega can now fixed. Residual error components can also fixed. optimizing one residual value, nlmixr2’s saem uses nlm R, efficient nealder-meade method. Lower level saem functions (like configsaem()) exported increasingly difficult use convert something standard; methods (like print, summary etc) maintained view lower level object debugging . Parameter history print-longer includes fixed parameters. model calculate residuals closely matches model used estimation remove small rounding differences may occur models. Different types additive proportional errors can used endpoint using + combined1() + combined2() otherwise takes supplied addProp option figure type combined model run (default combined2()) Parameter history printout now uses standard deviation additive components, matching estimation components. rxode2 solving options now saved rxControl part saemControl(). saemControl(rxControl=rxControl(...)); fixes conflicting option names well allowing alignment control structures focei, nlme saem saemControl() aligned methods.

title: nlmixr2est 2.0.6 – new package
text: nlme completely rewritten directly run rxode2 UI nlme always tries use mu-referencing (available) Internally nlme now uses parallel processing solving faster. nlmixr2NlmeControl() (overwrite nlmeControl()) documents adds options nlme. Also aligned methods. weights, fixed, random can specified nlmixr2NlmeControl(). , nlme object returned. returnNlme new option return nlme object instead traditional nlme object. nlme_ode lme_lin_cmpt removed. rxode2 solving options now saved rxControl part saemControl(). nlmeControl(rxControl=rxControl(...)); fixes conflicting option names well allowing alignment control structures focei, nlme saem

title: nlmixr2est 2.0.6 – new package

Even compression, saem objects often bit bigger since include large phiM object. nlmixr2 now supports non-mu referenced ETAs fit$parFixed fit$parFixedDf
